HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 per document. This is iter 1 of 5. Front-load ALL real findings in iter 1. Same quality bar regardless of iteration. Do not pick bone from egg — reserve P0/P1 for real execution/quality/faithfulness risks; classify minor as P2/P3.

## Context
POLARIS deep-research pipeline; repo root = current dir (C:\POLARIS). LIVE RUN PATH = scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py + scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py and everything they import (generator/multi_section_generator, generator/provenance_generator, roles/*, authority/*, retrieval/*, agents/*, llm/openrouter_client). Operator directives: timeouts UNLIMITED-with-watchdog OR 1.5x realistic generate time (sized off the 64000-token section budget); EVERY tunable param must be a PG_ .env var (hardcoding is "super lethal"); the pipeline MUST have CHECKPOINTS (carry DATA not VERDICTS; always re-run faithfulness gates on resume); faithfulness gates (strict_verify/NLI/4-role) are NEVER relaxed. Locked models: generator=deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro, mirror=z-ai/glm-5.1, sentinel=minimax/minimax-m2, judge=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b.

## YOUR LENS
Silent failure & data loss. Find fail-OPEN paths (judge/verifier errors that return a permissive default), swallowed exceptions (try/except that hides errors), silent drops of sources/claims/sentences, default-empty returns that look like success, places where retrieval or evidence silently shrinks, where a 'verified' count and the rendered output can disagree. Faithfulness-adjacent — where wrong/missing data can pass undetected.

## TASK — BLANK SLATE
You are deliberately given NO prior findings list. Independently hunt the LIVE run path line-by-line through YOUR LENS and report EVERY chokepoint you find — anything that hurts throughput, quality, completion, faithfulness, breadth, or cost. Read the actual code (file:line); do not guess. Hunt specifically for things a checklist-driven auditor would NOT think to look for. Be exhaustive within your lens.

failure modes; that's the point.

Usage:

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# BUG-B-101 fix: unified manifest.status taxonomy.
#
# Pre-fix, successful runs wrote manifest.json WITHOUT a "status" key
# while abort runs DID include it. Documentation claimed
# manifest.status was authoritative — it wasn't. See
# outputs/codex_findings/deep_dive_round_1/findings.md for the full
# scoping analysis.
#
# Post-fix, EVERY exit path writes a manifest.json with a "status"
# field from this unified taxonomy. `summary["status"]` is preserved
# as secondary sweep telemetry for backward compatibility with any
# downstream counter that relied on the legacy labels.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
    # success
    "success",
    # released-with-disclosure (I-perm-001 #1195 always-release): the report SHIPPED with honest
    # disclosed gaps (BLOCK->LABEL) — a RELEASED terminal, not an abort. `released_insufficient_
    # safety_evidence` is the honest clinical-safety-floor variant (normal render blocked, honest
    # report still ships). The "released_" prefix is a documented exception to the 4-prefix scheme
    # (alongside "cancelled") in tests/.../test_manifest_contract_status_prefixes.
    "released_with_disclosed_gaps",
    "released_insufficient_safety_evidence",
    # partial — report produced but degraded signal
    "partial_thin_corpus",
    "partial_incomplete_corpus",
    "partial_rule_check_warnings",
    "partial_outline_fallback",      # BUG-M-203: planner failed, fallback used
    "partial_evaluator_advisory",    # BUG-M-205: judge flagged critical axes
    "partial_qwen_advisory",         # I-modref-004 (#530): legacy alias, historical manifests
    "partial_saturation",            # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988): pruned report, some sections under-covered
    # abort — pipeline refused to produce a report
    "abort_scope_rejected",
    "abort_no_sources",
    "abort_corpus_inadequate",
    "abort_corpus_approval_denied",
    "abort_no_verified_sections",
    "abort_evaluator_critical",      # BUG-M-205: PT08/PT11/PT12 integrity failure
    "abort_budget_exceeded",         # I-meta-008 (#1015): PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN breached mid-run (generator OR 4-role verifier)
    "abort_verifier_degraded",       # I-ready-002 (#1071): judge_error_rate > PG_MAX_JUDGE_ERROR_RATE — binding verifier too degraded to trust
    "abort_discovery_degraded",      # FL-05 (#1124): a FORCE-ENABLED discovery feature (STORM/agentic) was on but did NOT fire (firing_status attempted_empty/error) — silently degraded to baseline; refuse to ship as success (gated by PG_RUN_HEALTH_GATE)
    "abort_safety_refused",          # I-ready-007 (#1072): input harm-refusal — explicit harm-intent query refused BEFORE retrieval (PG_USE_SAFETY_REFUSAL); refuse-with-redirection, zero generator spend
    "abort_journal_only_contract_conflict",  # I-ready-017 (#1134): journal_only — a required report-contract slot is bound to a non-journal entity; refuse rather than cite a non-journal source in a journal-restricted review
    "error_journal_only_leak",       # I-ready-017 (#1134): journal_only — a non-journal row reached the generator past the source-filter (should never happen); fail-closed backstop, never synthesize
    "error_corpus_population_mismatch",  # I-ready-017 FX-06b (#1121): the corpus-approval gate and the adequacy artifact would score DIFFERENT populations (total_sources OR tier_counts diverge) — refuse rather than gate/approve on a population the report does not consume (defensive; should never fire)
    "abort_credibility_coverage_gap",  # I-cred-008b (#1162): the activated credibility-disclosure pass found a cited token whose evidence has no credibility/origin coverage — fail-loud rather than disclose a claim whose source was never scored (only fires under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN)
    "abort_four_role_release_held",  # I-ready-016 (#1086): 4-role D8 held release (fabrication/coverage/S0/rewrite) — written via _SUMMARY_TO_UNIFIED["four_role_held"] at L4934/5065; was a real taxonomy gap
    "abort_report_redaction_failed", # I-beatboth-fix-000 (#1171): post-gate report.md reconciliation could not redact a material non-VERIFIED claim that IS present in the body (unlocatable / missing audit row) — fail-closed, refuse to ship a partially-reconciled report (no trustworthy artifact)
    "cancelled",                     # I-ready-016 (#1086): user-requested cancel terminal; _abort_if_cancelled writes manifest.status="cancelled" (consumed by v6 UI + SSE — value preserved, NOT renamed). Does NOT match the 4-prefix scheme — see the documented exception in test_manifest_contract_status_prefixes.
    # error — unhandled exception
    "error_unexpected",
})

# Map legacy summary["status"] labels → unified manifest.status values.
_SUMMARY_TO_UNIFIED: dict[str, str] = {
    "ok": "success",
    # I-perm-001 (#1195) slice 2: the always-release outcome's clean status IS already unified

    "success": "success",

    "ok_incomplete_corpus": "partial_incomplete_corpus",

"""Gate-B production caller — wires the native 4-role evaluation into the honest sweep.

I-meta-002 PR-9/M3b. This is WIRING ONLY: it constructs the verifier `RoleTransport` for the
three verifier roles (Mirror / Sentinel / Judge — the generator stays on OpenRouter, upstream
of this transport), sets `PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE`, builds a no-argument CLOSURE over the native
input builder (`build_native_gate_b_inputs`) + the deterministic evidence normalization, and
hands the transport + builder into `run_one_query`.

TRANSPORT ROUTING (I-meta-007d, operator directive 2026-06-01): the verifier transport is
ENV-GATED via `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT`:
  - "openrouter" (DEFAULT at THIS dev/benchmark stage): all 3 verifier roles route via
    OpenRouter with MAX reasoning (`OpenRouterRoleTransport`). This avoids standing up the
    self-hosted vLLM GPU fleet just to run the benchmark — it saves the Vast.ai GPU rental
    cost. SOVEREIGNTY: OpenRouter is a US router; this is acceptable for dev/benchmark ONLY,
    NOT for the sovereign demo (feedback_sovereignty_threat_model). The lock is NOT mutated —
    it still pins `serving_route: vast_self_host*`, which remains the FINAL SOVEREIGN
    destination; only the runtime routing is overridden, and only at this stage.
  - "self_host": the original `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport` POSTing per-role
    `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` self-hosted vLLM endpoints — the lock's sovereign serving route.

CONTAMINATION-CRITICAL (§-1.1, operator-locked): every input is built ONLY from NATIVE
config — the scope template's `per_query_report_contract[<slug>].required_entities` and the
D8 release-policy config. This module NEVER reads anything under `outputs/dr_benchmark/`
(gold rubric / freeze pin / competitor answers).

NO SPEND / NO NETWORK at import. The transport's `httpx.Client` is created INSIDE
`build_gate_b_transport` (not at module level), so importing this module never opens a client
or touches a socket. The Gate-B LIVE run (real endpoints + real spend) is the later
operator-authorized canary; this module is exercised offline by the seam test with a FAKE
transport injected in place of `build_gate_b_transport`'s output.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping

import httpx

# I-ready-018 (#1138, Codex iter-1 P1-1): install the import-root alias BEFORE any repo import. The
# beat-both run launches from repo-root WITHOUT PYTHONPATH=src, so src/sitecustomize.py does NOT
# auto-load here. The core run path is src.-consistent (unaffected), but the --upload-file path imports
# polaris_v6 internals via the BARE root, which would ModuleNotFoundError root-only. Installing the
# alias here makes any bare polaris_graph/polaris_v6 import resolve to its src. counterpart. Idempotent
# + a no-op when the alias is already installed (sitecustomize) or the canonical tree is absent.
from src._polaris_import_alias import install_import_root_alias

install_import_root_alias()

from scripts.architecture.verify_lock import load_lock
# I-ready-017 FIX-JO (#1100): the canonical journal_only runtime-flag NAME. Imported (not
# re-hardcoded) so the per-slug activation below sets the SAME env the consumer reads in
# run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query -> journal_only_active (single source of truth, LAW VI).
# journal_only_filter imports only stdlib (os/dataclasses/urllib) — NO network/spend at import.
from src.polaris_graph.nodes.journal_only_filter import JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG
from src.polaris_graph.roles.native_gate_b_inputs import (
    NativeGateBBundle,
    build_native_gate_b_inputs,
    normalize_evidence_pool_lookup,
)
from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import (
    OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport,
)
from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import (
    FOUR_ROLE_STAGE,
    OpenRouterRoleTransport,
    benchmark_verifier_family,
    benchmark_verifier_lineup,
    openrouter_base_url,
    role_reasoning_enabled,
)
from src.polaris_graph.roles.release_policy import load_d8_policy_config

# The three verifier roles this caller serves (the generator is excluded — it runs live on
# OpenRouter, upstream of the per-claim verifier transport).
_VERIFIER_ROLES = ("mirror", "sentinel", "judge")

# Env flag the guarded sweep branch reads to activate the 4-role seam.
_FOUR_ROLE_MODE_ENV = "PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE"

# I-meta-007d: which verifier transport to build (LAW VI env-gate). DEFAULT "openrouter" at the
# CURRENT dev/benchmark stage (saves GPU cost). "self_host" selects the lock's sovereign route.
_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV = "PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT"
_TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER = "openrouter"
_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST = "self_host"
_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT = _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER

# OpenRouter catalog endpoint (GET) for the benchmark-stage slug-resolution preflight.
_OPENROUTER_MODELS_PATH = "/models"

# httpx client timeout knob (LAW VI). I-meta-008 FULL-POWER: use the generous verifier timeout
# (default 900s) so the client default never undercuts the reasoning verifiers' per-request budget.
_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "900"))


def four_role_transport_mode() -> str:
    """Resolve `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT` (I-meta-007d). DEFAULT "openrouter" at the dev/benchmark
    stage (saves GPU cost; OpenRouter is a US router — dev/benchmark ONLY, never the sovereign
    demo). "self_host" selects the lock's `vast_self_host*` route. Fails loud on an unknown value
    so a typo never silently picks the wrong (spend) route.
    """
    mode = os.getenv(_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV, _FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT).strip().lower()
    if mode not in (_TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER, _TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST):
        raise ValueError(
            f"{_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV}={mode!r} is invalid; expected "
            f"{_TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER!r} (benchmark-stage OpenRouter, default) or "
            f"{_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST!r} (lock sovereign self-host route)."
        )
    return mode


def build_gate_b_transport(
    http_client: httpx.Client | None = None,
) -> OpenRouterRoleTransport | OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport:
    """Construct the verifier transport (one shared sync `httpx.Client`), ENV-GATED per
    `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT` (I-meta-007d).

    Built INSIDE this function (never at module level) so importing `run_gate_b` opens no
    client and touches no socket.

    - "openrouter" (DEFAULT, dev/benchmark stage — saves GPU cost): returns an
      `OpenRouterRoleTransport` that, when invoked, POSTs `/chat/completions` to OpenRouter with
      each role's lock-pinned slug AND MAX reasoning. SOVEREIGNTY: OpenRouter is a US router —
      acceptable for dev/benchmark ONLY; the lock's `vast_self_host*` self-host route remains
      the FINAL SOVEREIGN destination (the lock is NOT mutated).
    - "self_host": returns the original `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport` (per-role
      `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` self-hosted vLLM) — the lock's sovereign serving route.

    `http_client` (P2): the live caller passes nothing, so a real `httpx.Client` is built here;
    tests inject a `MockTransport`-backed client so transport-selection is exercised with no live
    client and no socket. Resolved ONCE and handed to whichever transport is selected.

    The live invocation is the later operator-authorized Gate-B canary, never a test.
    """
    client = http_client if http_client is not None else httpx.Client(timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
        return OpenRouterRoleTransport(client)
    return OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport(client)


def verifier_model_slugs() -> dict[str, str]:
    """Return the pinned `{mirror,sentinel,judge: model_slug}` map for the ACTIVE transport mode.

    MODE-AWARE (I-meta-007d P1-1) so served == pinned on whichever route is active:
      - "openrouter" (benchmark, DEFAULT): the BENCHMARK lineup
        (`benchmark_verifier_lineup` — the operator-chosen OpenRouter slugs in
        role_selection_final.md: Mirror `z-ai/glm-5.1`, Sentinel `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b`,
        Judge `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`). The transport POSTs these exact slugs as `body["model"]`,
        so the pin the builder threads into `RoleRequest.model_slug` matches the served slug.
      - "self_host": the lock's self-host `model_slug`s (Mirror `cohere/command-a-plus`,
        Sentinel the granite-GUARDIAN, Judge `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`) — the sovereign serving
        route, served by `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport` against `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL`.

    The single machine-readable source of truth per mode (LAW VI). The generator slug is
    intentionally excluded — it is not a per-claim verifier role.
    """
    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
        return benchmark_verifier_lineup()
    lock = load_lock()
    return {role: lock["required_roles"][role]["model_slug"] for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES}


def make_gate_b_input_builder(
    *,
    d8_config_path: str | Path | None = None,
) -> Callable[..., NativeGateBBundle]:
    """Return the Gate-B builder CLOSURE — a factory over RESOLUTION POLICY only.

    The closure captures ONLY the resolution policy (the optional `d8_config_path` + the
    normalization fn + the lock-slug source) — NOT the run-local report objects, which do not
    exist when the caller constructs the builder (they are produced inside `run_one_query` after
    generation). The SEAM calls the closure AFTER generation, passing the run-local objects
    (`multi`, `template`, `slug`, `domain`, `ev_pool`) as keyword args. The closure then OWNS
    resolution: it normalizes the run's raw `ev_pool` into the builder's
    `{evidence_id: {text, doi?, pmid?, url?}}` record contract (deterministic, no network — keys
    preserve the ProvenanceToken.evidence_id space), loads the D8 policy config, sources the
    pinned verifier slugs from the lock, and calls the native `build_native_gate_b_inputs`.
    NATIVE-ONLY: nothing here reads the gold rubric.
    """

    def _builder(
        *,
        multi: Any,
        template: dict,
        slug: str,
        domain: str,
        ev_pool: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
    ) -> NativeGateBBundle:
        evidence_lookup = normalize_evidence_pool_lookup(ev_pool)
        d8_config = load_d8_policy_config(d8_config_path)
        model_slugs = verifier_model_slugs()
        return build_native_gate_b_inputs(
            multi=multi,
            template=template,
            slug=slug,
            domain=domain,
            evidence_lookup=evidence_lookup,
            model_slugs=model_slugs,
            d8_config=d8_config,
        )

    return _builder


def enable_four_role_mode() -> None:
    """Set `PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1` in the process env so the guarded sweep branch activates.

    Wiring helper (LAW VI: env-driven activation). The Gate-B live run sets this before
    invoking the sweep; the offline seam test sets it via monkeypatch instead.
    """
    os.environ[_FOUR_ROLE_MODE_ENV] = "1"


def assert_four_role_families_distinct() -> dict[str, str]:
    """Assert the 4 roles (generator + the 3 verifiers) are all DISTINCT lineages for the
    ACTIVE transport mode (the N-way two-family invariant, CLAUDE.md §9.1 / family_policy:
    all_distinct). Fails LOUD (I-meta-007) — a collision would let one family self-verify.
    Returns the `{role: family}` map for telemetry.

    MODE-AWARE (I-meta-007d P1-1): the family check must run over the families ACTUALLY served:
      - "openrouter" (benchmark, DEFAULT): generator family from the lock (`deepseek` — the
        generator is unchanged + runs upstream on OpenRouter) UNION the 3 BENCHMARK verifier
        families (`benchmark_verifier_family`: Mirror `z-ai`, Sentinel `ibm-granite`, Judge
        `qwen`). These four — deepseek / z-ai / ibm-granite / qwen — are distinct. (Checking the
        LOCK's verifier families here would assert on `cohere`/`ibm-granite`/`qwen`, which are NOT
        the families served on this route.)
      - "self_host": the lock's families for all 4 roles (the sovereign route).
    """
    lock = load_lock()
    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
        fams = {"generator": str(lock["required_roles"]["generator"]["family"])}
        for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES:
            fams[role] = benchmark_verifier_family(role)
    else:
        roles = ("generator", *_VERIFIER_ROLES)
        fams = {r: str(lock["required_roles"][r]["family"]) for r in roles}
    if len(set(fams.values())) != len(fams):
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Gate-B preflight: 4-role family collision — all roles must be distinct "
            f"lineages for the active transport mode, got {fams}"
        )
    return fams


def preflight_self_host_roles() -> dict[str, str]:
    """LIVE-run preflight (self_host mode): resolve each self-hosted verifier endpoint up front
    so a missing `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` fails BEFORE the sweep starts (not mid-run on the
    first claim's Mirror call), AND assert the 4 families are distinct. Returns the
    `{role: base_url}` map. ONLY called when building the real transport (skipped
    when a fake transport is injected for offline tests). I-meta-007.
    """
    from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import role_endpoint

    assert_four_role_families_distinct()
    endpoints: dict[str, str] = {}
    for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES:
        base_url, _api_key, _slug = role_endpoint(role)  # raises LOUD if unset
        endpoints[role] = base_url
    return endpoints


def _fetch_openrouter_catalog(http_client: httpx.Client | None = None) -> list[dict]:
    """Fetch the OpenRouter model catalog (`GET {base}/models`) and return its `data` list.

    The `http_client` is INJECTABLE so the preflight test feeds a faked catalog via
    `httpx.MockTransport` (no real network, no spend). When None, a real sync client is created
    against `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` (LAW VI) — that path only runs on a live, operator-authorized
    benchmark run. Fails loud (LAW II) on a non-200 status or a body without a `data` list.
    """
    url = f"{openrouter_base_url()}{_OPENROUTER_MODELS_PATH}"
    own_client = http_client is None
    client = http_client or httpx.Client(timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
    try:
        response = client.get(url, timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
    finally:
        if own_client:
            client.close()
    if response.status_code != httpx.codes.OK:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: GET {url} returned HTTP {response.status_code}"
        )
    body = response.json()
    data = body.get("data")
    if not isinstance(data, list):
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: GET {url} body has no `data` list (got "
            f"{type(data).__name__})"
        )
    return data


def preflight_openrouter_roles(http_client: httpx.Client | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
    """LIVE-run preflight (openrouter mode, I-meta-007d): resolve each verifier role's BENCHMARK
    lineup slug against OpenRouter's catalog so a non-resolving slug fails BEFORE the sweep spends
    a token, AND assert the 4 families are distinct.

    Resolves each role's BENCHMARK slug (`benchmark_verifier_lineup` — the operator-chosen
    OpenRouter slugs in role_selection_final.md: Mirror `z-ai/glm-5.1`, Sentinel
    `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b`, Judge `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`; P1-1) — NOT the lock's self-host
    slugs (Cohere's Mirror + the granite-GUARDIAN Sentinel are not on OpenRouter). The same lineup
    the transport POSTs, so a slug that resolves here is the slug that gets served. Each is
    asserted present in the OpenRouter catalog as either an entry `id` OR its `canonical_slug`
    (OpenRouter exposes both; a slug may match either). Returns the `{role: benchmark_slug}` map.
    The `http_client` is INJECTABLE so the test feeds a faked catalog (no network). ONLY called
    when building the real transport (skipped when a fake transport is injected for offline tests).

    Fails LOUD (LAW II) on a missing slug or a family collision — never a silent skip.
    """
    assert_four_role_families_distinct()
    catalog = _fetch_openrouter_catalog(http_client)
    # Map EVERY catalog alias (entry `id` AND `canonical_slug`) -> that entry's
    # `supported_parameters` list. `setdefault` so the first occurrence of an alias wins (a later
    # duplicate id never clobbers it). A missing or non-list `supported_parameters` becomes `[]`,
    # which the executability check below reads as "advertises no reasoning".
    slug_params: dict[str, list] = {}
    for entry in catalog:
        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
            continue
        params = entry.get("supported_parameters")
        if not isinstance(params, list):
            params = []
        for key in ("id", "canonical_slug"):
            value = entry.get(key)
            if value:
                slug_params.setdefault(value, params)

    resolved: dict[str, str] = {}
    lineup = benchmark_verifier_lineup()
    for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES:
        slug = lineup[role]
        if slug not in slug_params:
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: role {role!r} benchmark slug {slug!r} is NOT in "
                f"the OpenRouter catalog (neither as an entry `id` nor `canonical_slug`). The "
                "benchmark-stage OpenRouter route cannot serve it — fix the benchmark lineup "
                f"(PG_{role.upper()}_MODEL / role_selection_final.md) or select "
                f"{_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV}={_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST}."
            )
        # Executability check (Codex iter-2 P1): a reasoning-enabled role sends MAX reasoning with
        # `provider.require_parameters=True`. If its benchmark slug does NOT advertise `reasoning`
        # in OpenRouter `supported_parameters`, OpenRouter refuses to route (no provider honors the
        # param) and the FIRST call fails. Catch it here, before any token is spent.
        if role_reasoning_enabled(role) and "reasoning" not in slug_params[slug]:
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: role {role!r} is reasoning-enabled (sends MAX "
                f"reasoning + provider.require_parameters=True), but its benchmark slug {slug!r} "
                f"does NOT advertise `reasoning` in OpenRouter `supported_parameters` (advertised: "
                f"{slug_params[slug]!r}); with require_parameters=True OpenRouter would fail routing "
                f"at the first {role!r} call. Fix the benchmark lineup (PG_{role.upper()}_MODEL) to "
                f"a reasoning-capable slug, disable reasoning for this role "
                f"(PG_{role.upper()}_REASONING=0), or select "
                f"{_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV}={_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST}."
            )
        resolved[role] = slug
    return resolved


def preflight_four_role_transport() -> dict[str, str]:
    """LIVE-run preflight dispatcher (I-meta-007d): run the preflight matching the ENV-gated
    transport mode. openrouter -> resolve slugs against the OpenRouter catalog; self_host ->
    resolve each `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL`. Both assert the 4-distinct-family invariant. Returns the
    mode's `{role: resolved}` map.
    """
    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
        return preflight_openrouter_roles()
    return preflight_self_host_roles()


# Filename of the machine-readable stage marker written next to a Gate-B run (P2, I-meta-007d
# diff-gate iter-1). A future gate/manifest reader checks `stage` to tell a benchmark OpenRouter
# run apart from the sovereign self-host serving path.
FOUR_ROLE_STAGE_MARKER_FILENAME = "four_role_stage.json"


def four_role_stage_marker() -> dict[str, object]:
    """Build the machine-readable stage marker for the ACTIVE transport mode (P2).

    For the OpenRouter benchmark route the `stage` is `FOUR_ROLE_STAGE` ("benchmark_openrouter")

codex

src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:1:"""ARCH-3: Dedicated citation agent for accurate inline citation insertion.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:3:Inspired by Claude Research's architecture — separate citation from prose writing.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:4:Current approach: section_writer writes prose WITH citations in one pass, leading
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:5:to citation mismatches (e.g., EPA data attributed to a blog) and citation poverty
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:6:(792 words with only 2 citations in Section 8 of PG_TEST_035).
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:8:This agent reviews draft prose and inserts citations at correct positions by:
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:9:1. Identifying every factual claim in the text
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:10:2. Finding the best-matching evidence piece(s) for each claim
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:11:3. Inserting [CITE:ev_xxx] citations at the correct position
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:12:4. Flagging claims with no matching evidence as [UNSUPPORTED]
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:14:OUTPUT FORMAT: [CITE:ev_xxx] — compatible with downstream resolve_citations().
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:30:CITATION_SYSTEM = """You are a precision citation specialist for academic research reports.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:31:Your ONLY job is to insert inline citations [N] into text.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:34:1. Every specific number, date, percentage, finding, or factual claim MUST have a citation.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:35:2. Only cite evidence that DIRECTLY supports the specific claim (not just the topic).
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:36:3. Use the format [N] where N is the evidence number from the provided mapping.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:37:4. Do NOT add citations for general knowledge, definitions, or transitional phrases.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:38:5. If a claim has no matching evidence, leave it without a citation (do NOT add [UNSUPPORTED]).
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:40:7. Place citations immediately after the claim they support, before any punctuation.
src/polaris_graph/agents\citation_agent.py:41:8. If multiple evidence pieces support the same claim, cite all: [1][3][7]."""

src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:43:VERIFICATION_SYSTEM = """You are an ADVERSARIAL claim verifier. Your job is NOT to confirm claims.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:44:Your job is to FIND WEAKNESSES in each claim.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:47:- You read the claim and evidence and default to "SUPPORTED" without checking specifics.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:49:- You accept vague evidence for specific claims.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:52:COUNTERACT THESE BIASES. For each claim:
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:53:1. Find the WEAKEST point. What specific word, number, or causal link is NOT in the evidence?
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:55:3. Check directionality. "reduces" vs "may reduce" vs "is associated with" are different claims.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:56:4. Check population. A claim about "adults" is not supported by evidence about "mice".
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:58:ONLY mark SUPPORTED if you tried to break the claim and could not find a weakness.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:61:1. SUPPORTED: You actively tried to disprove it and failed. The evidence directly states the claim.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:62:2. PARTIALLY_SUPPORTED: The claim goes beyond the evidence (adds specificity, stronger causation, broader population than studied).
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:63:3. NOT_SUPPORTED: The claim has no basis in the evidence, OR you cannot verify it from the provided text.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:68:- When source content is provided, verify AGAINST THE ACTUAL TEXT. Check numbers, dates, names.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:69:- If no source content is provided, mark NOT_SUPPORTED. Title-only is insufficient.
src/polaris_graph/agents\verifier.py:70:- If the source is about a completely different topic, mark NOT_SUPPORTED regardless of superficial matches.

src/polaris_graph/generator\slot_fill.py:654:TASK: Weave the extracted fields above into a 14-20 sentence narrative paragraph (300-450 words). Use AT LEAST 2-3 contrast markers ("however", "in contrast", "whereas", "by comparison", "although", "despite") per paragraph when the extracted fields support contrastive framing.

src/polaris_graph/generator\slot_fill.py:663:VERBATIM CONSTRAINT (CRITICAL — every sentence is independently re-verified by verify_sentence_provenance against the cited spans AFTER you write it, and any sentence that is not entailed by the cited span is DROPPED from the report and CANNOT be rescued):
src/polaris_graph/generator\slot_fill.py:664:- RESTATE ONLY the provided field payloads above. You are re-expressing already-verified facts in flowing prose — you are NOT adding information.
src/polaris_graph/generator\slot_fill.py:666:- Do not introduce new NAMED CONCEPTS, metrics, mechanisms, outcomes, subgroups, or causal claims that are not present in the extracted field values. src/polaris_graph/generator\slot_fill.py:668:- If a field is missing (e.g., no comparator value), do not invent it — phrase as "the comparator details were not extractable from the cited primary source".
src/polaris_graph/generator\slot_fill.py:669:- When in doubt, say LESS: a shorter paragraph that only restates the fields is correct; a longer paragraph that adds unstated specifics will be dropped at verification.

   60: # section (a 0-socket provider stall — observed on the drb_72 smoke: the section
   61: # asyncio.gather hung 19 min with NO open sockets / ~0 CPU, immune to the httpx read
   62: # timeout because no socket was open to time out) gets a hard wall-clock bound: it
   63: # RETRIES once (a fresh call likely hits a healthy provider), then FAILS LOUD instead
   64: # of hanging the whole report forever. Faithfulness-neutral: only affects WHETHER a
   65: # section generates; strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance are untouched.
   66: def _section_wallclock_seconds() -> int:
   67:     try:
   68:         return int(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS", "0"))
   69:     except ValueError:
   70:         return 0
   71: 
   72: 
   73: async def _run_section_with_wallclock(runner, plan):
   74:     """Wrap a section bounded-runner in a per-section wall-clock guard (I-arch-002
   75:     #1248). OFF (<= 0) => exact ``await runner(plan)`` (byte-identical). ON => one
   76:     wait_for, one retry, then fail-loud on a persistent wedge. ``runner`` re-runs the
   77:     full section on retry (re-acquires the semaphore + re-does the work); a cancelled
   78:     first attempt appends nothing, so there is no double-write."""
   79:     wall = _section_wallclock_seconds()
   80:     if wall <= 0:
   81:         return await runner(plan)
   82:     last_exc: BaseException | None = None
   83:     for attempt in (1, 2):
   84:         try:
   85:             return await asyncio.wait_for(runner(plan), timeout=wall)
   86:         except asyncio.TimeoutError as exc:
   87:             last_exc = exc
   88:             logger.warning(
   89:                 "[gen-wallclock] section exceeded %ds wall-clock (attempt %d/2) — "
   90:                 "likely a transient provider stall; %s",
   91:                 wall, attempt, "retrying" if attempt == 1 else "failing loud",
   92:             )
   93:     raise TimeoutError(
   94:         f"section generation exceeded {wall}s wall-clock x2 — failing loud instead "
   95:         f"of hanging the report (I-arch-002 #1248)"
   96:     ) from last_exc
   97: 
   98: 
   99: # I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W4gen: master WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE flag reader (CLAUDE.md §-1.3).

 5436:     async def _m63_llm_call(prompt: str) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
 5437:         """Adapter: one OpenRouter call per M-58 slot prompt.
 5438:         Returns (response_text, input_tokens, output_tokens).
 5439:         M-58's `parse_slot_fill_response` handles the JSON
 5440:         parsing; we just hand the raw text through.
 5441: 
 5442:         V30 Phase-2 M-66 run-5 diagnostic: contract slots with
 5443:         25K-char direct_quote (e.g. FDA Mounjaro label via
 5444:         M-66b-T OA full-text fetch) produced JSON truncation
 5445:         (`Unterminated string starting at pos 10561`) when the
 5446:         LLM tried to echo a long regulatory prose span under the
 5447:         default section_max_tokens=2400 budget. Raise the cap
 5448:         for contract extraction calls — the JSON schema is much
 5449:         terser than legacy section prose (max 10 fields × verbatim
 5450:         quotes × ~500 chars = ~5K tokens), so 6000 gives safe
 5451:         headroom without inviting runaway verbosity.
 5452:         """
 5453:         from ..llm.openrouter_client import (
 5454:             OpenRouterClient,
 5455:             set_reasoning_call_context,
 5456:         )
 5457:         client = OpenRouterClient(model=gen_model)
 5458:         try:
 5459:             # I-gen-004 (#496): tag the V30 contract-slot extraction call.
 5460:             set_reasoning_call_context(
 5461:                 section="_contract_slot", call_type="contract_slot",
 5462:             )
 5463:             response = await client.generate(
 5464:                 prompt=prompt,
 5465:                 system=(
 5466:                     "You are a JSON-only extraction assistant. "
 5467:                     "Output ONLY the JSON schema the user prompt "
 5468:                     "specifies. Do not include prose, preamble, "
 5469:                     "code fences, or any text outside the JSON "
 5470:                     "object."
 5471:                 ),
 5472:                 max_tokens=max(section_max_tokens, PG_CONTRACT_SLOT_MIN_MAX_TOKENS),
 5473:                 temperature=section_temperature,
 5474:             )
 5475:         finally:
 5476:             if hasattr(client, "close"):
 5477:                 try:
 5478:                     await client.close()
 5479:                 except Exception:
 5480:                     pass
 5481:         return (
 5482:             (response.content or "").strip(),
 5483:             response.input_tokens,
 5484:             response.output_tokens,
 5485:         )

 7286:             # captured from the floor selection object BEFORE the capped-finding-dedup reassignment
 7287:             # (whose dropped_count is 0 on the relevance_floor=None short path). Surfaced so the
 7288:             # manifest does not launder the honest floor cut that evidence_selector.py:1468-1474 logs.
 7289:             # Telemetry-only; selection unchanged. Added only when the relevance floor was active
 7290:             # (`_relevance_floor is not None`) so a no-floor run keeps a byte-identical manifest.
 7291:             **(
 7292:                 {"evidence_floor_dropped": _floor_dropped_count}
 7293:                 if _relevance_floor is not None
 7294:                 else {}
 7295:             ),
 7296:             # I-pipe-015 (#1240, Codex iter-1 REQUEST_CHANGES): per-RUN token-honesty telemetry
 7297:             # (malformed_canonicalized / malformed_dropped). Reset at run start (above) so these
 7298:             # counts are THIS query's, not the accumulated process total. Gated on
 7299:             # _token_honest_drop_enabled() so a PG_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_HONEST_DROP=0 run keeps a
 7300:             # byte-identical manifest (the path is off, counters stay 0, key omitted).
 7301:             **(
 7302:                 {"token_honesty": get_token_honesty_telemetry()}
 7303:                 if _token_honest_drop_enabled()
 7304:                 else {}
 7305:             ),
 7306:             # I-perm-011 (#1205): the post-floor extraction funnel endpoint —
 7307:             # rows that survived the relevance floor + capped-dedup and form the
 7308:             # generator BASE (pre-prepend; the contract/upload prepends are added
 7309:             # later, and the post-floor base is the inflation-free count). This is
 7310:             # the honest counterpart to extraction_yield.total_extracted_rows (the
 7311:             # pre-select pool). It is read from the SAME `evidence_selection` object
 7312:             # the manifest already exposes above (the round-0 post-floor/dedup
 7313:             # selection — NOT reassigned by the saturation loop, which tracks its
 7314:             # rounds via `evidence_for_gen`/`_resel`), so the two keys are always
 7315:             # consistent. This is exactly the drb_76 `selected=53 of 597` endpoint.
 7316:             # Gated on PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR so a flag-OFF / slate-absent run
 7317:             # keeps a byte-identical manifest.
 7318:             **(
 7319:                 {"selected_to_generator_initial": len(
 7320:                     evidence_selection.selected_rows
 7321:                 )}
 7322:                 if _env_flag("PG_SELECT_SUBQUERY_FLOOR", default=False)
 7323:                 else {}
 7324:             ),
 7325:             "protocol_sha256": scope.protocol_sha256,
 7326:             # #958 (S2): use the shared retrieval-section writer so the
 7327:             # corpus-truncation flag + counts land on the SUCCESS path too
 7328:             # (this inline block previously bypassed _base_manifest_envelope).

 7330:             "corpus": {
 7331:                 "count": dist.total_sources,
 7332:                 "tier_fractions": dist.tier_fractions,
 7333:                 "material_deviation": dist.has_material_deviation,
 7334:                 "approved": approved,
 7335:             },
 7336:             "adequacy": {
 7337:                 "decision": adequacy.decision,
 7338:                 "findings_ok": sum(1 for f in adequacy.findings if f.ok),
 7339:                 "findings_total": len(adequacy.findings),
 7340:                 "critical_count": sum(
 7341:                     1 for f in adequacy.findings
 7342:                     if f.severity == "critical"
 7343:                 ),
 7344:             },
 7345:             "completeness": {
 7346:                 "total_applicable": completeness.total_applicable,
 7347:                 "total_covered": completeness.total_covered,
 7348:                 "total_uncovered": completeness.total_uncovered,
 7349:                 "covered_fraction": round(completeness.covered_fraction, 3),
 7350:                 "completeness_state": completeness.completeness_state,  # FX-10
 7351:                 "notes": completeness.notes,
 7352:                 "uncovered_topic_ids": completeness.uncovered_topic_ids(),
 7353:             },
 7354:             "contradictions_found": len(contradictions),
 7355:             "generator": {
 7356:                 "outline_sections": [p.title for p in multi.outline],
 7357:                 # D-5 (#1182): sections_kept feeds the §-1.1 beat-both scoring surface
 7358:                 # (.codex/beatboth5/build_forensic_input.py). A 0-verified gap stub ships
 7359:                 # dropped_due_to_failure=False with non-empty disclosure verified_text, so
 7360:                 # the bare not-dropped count inflated POLARIS's section count vs Gemini/
 7361:                 # ChatGPT. Exclude gap stubs via the SAME universal survivor signal
 7362:                 # filter_verified_sections uses. Metadata honesty — not a content change.
 7363:                 "sections_kept": sum(
 7364:                     1 for s in multi.sections
 7365:                     if not s.dropped_due_to_failure
 7366:                     and not getattr(s, "is_gap_stub", False)
 7367:                     and getattr(s, "sentences_verified", 1) > 0
 7368:                 ),
 7369:                 "words": multi.total_words,
 7370:                 # I-bug-105 two-layer reporting: distinguish verified
 7371:                 # word count from analyst-synthesis word count so
 7372:                 # downstream consumers cannot mistake total length for
 7373:                 # audited length (per Codex iter-1 brief P0).
 7374:                 "verified_words": (
 7375:                     multi.total_words
 7376:                     - getattr(multi, "analyst_synthesis_words", 0)
 7377:                 ),
 7378:                 "analyst_synthesis_words": getattr(
 7379:                     multi, "analyst_synthesis_words", 0
 7380:                 ),
 7381:                 "analyst_synthesis_input_tokens": getattr(
 7382:                     multi, "analyst_synthesis_input_tokens", 0
 7383:                 ),
 7384:                 "analyst_synthesis_output_tokens": getattr(
 7385:                     multi, "analyst_synthesis_output_tokens", 0
 7386:                 ),
 7387:                 "sentences_verified": multi.total_sentences_verified,
 7388:                 "sentences_dropped": multi.total_sentences_dropped,
 7389:                 "limitations_words": len(multi.limitations_text.split()),
 7390:             },
 7391:             "evaluator_rule_pass": ev_out.rule_check_pass_count,
 7392:             "evaluator_rule_fail": ev_out.rule_check_fail_count,
 7393:             "judge_verdicts": (
 7394:                 {v: sum(1 for j in jr.verdicts.values()
 7395:                         if j["verdict"] == v)
 7396:                  for v in ("good", "acceptable", "needs_revision", "unknown")}
 7397:                 if jr and jr.parse_ok
 7398:                 # I-meta-007: when the 4-role seam ran, the legacy judge was
 7399:                 # deliberately skipped (D8 is the binding gate) — say so, don't
 7400:                 # mislabel it "failed".
 7401:                 else ({"superseded_by_four_role_seam": True} if _seam_will_run
 7402:                       else {"error": "failed"})
 7403:             ),
 7404:             "cost_usd": run_cost,
 7405:             "budget_cap_usd": get_max_cost_per_run(),
 7406:             # GH#423 I-gen-002: cross-section fact-dedup telemetry.
 7407:             # Persists fact_dedup pass results (groups, redundants,

 7409:             # of the dedup behavior survives into manifest.json.

 7413:         # I-cred-006b (#1170): surface the weighted-corpus credibility disclosure in the per-run

 7421:         # I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985, P1-8): record the SHA-pinned ResearchPlan

 7434:         # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988) — Codex diff-gate iter-2 P2a: surface the
 7435:         # saturation trajectory + per-section shortfall (incl. uncovered sub-query
 7436:         # text) in the per-run manifest too. It already lands in sweep_summary.json
 7437:         # via `summary`; this makes the PER-RUN audit artifact self-contained for a
 7438:         # partial_saturation result. ON-mode only (key absent in OFF -> legacy
 7439:         # manifest shape preserved).
 7440:         if summary.get("saturation"):
 7441:             manifest["saturation"] = summary["saturation"]
 7442: 
 7443:         # I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989): finding-dedup telemetry + per-cluster
 7444:         # corroboration (independent hosts) in the per-run manifest. ON-mode only

 7446:         if _finding_dedup_telemetry is not None:
 7447:             manifest["finding_dedup"] = _finding_dedup_telemetry
 7448: 
 7449:         # I-meta-005 Phase 7 (#991): quantified-analysis telemetry (spec produced,
 7450:         # execution success, sourced/modeled input counts, verified/dropped
 7451:         # sentence counts, sourced-input conflicts). ON-mode only (key absent in
 7452:         # OFF -> legacy manifest shape preserved byte-for-byte).
 7453:         if _quantified_telemetry is not None:
 7454:             # I-meta-008 P1-3 (#1018): normalize a `fired` boolean so a post-run assertion can
 7455:             # detect a SILENT no-op, and surface it LOUDLY in the log. The block never aborts the
 7456:             # run, so without this an error / spec_produced=False completes silently without the
 7457:             # Phase-7 differentiator and the manifest gives no single clear signal.
 7458:             _quantified_telemetry = _normalize_quantified_telemetry(_quantified_telemetry)
 7459:             manifest["quantified_analysis"] = _quantified_telemetry
 7460:             if not _quantified_telemetry["fired"]:
 7461:                 _log(
 7462:                     "[phase7]      WARNING: quantified analysis ran but produced NO verified "
 7463:                     "quantified output (silent no-op) — manifest.quantified_analysis.fired=False"
 7464:                 )
 7465: 
 7466:         # I-meta-002 sub-PR-6: GUARDED 4-role evaluation seam (default OFF, NO spend).
 7467:         # Activates ONLY when an explicit RoleTransport is INJECTED (four_role_transport)
 7468:         # AND PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE is enabled. There is NO default real transport: the live
 7469:         # 4-role sweep is Gate-B (after lock promotion + operator spend authorization). When
 7470:         # this branch is OFF (the default), every line below is the unchanged legacy path —
 7471:         # eval_gate already drove manifest['release_allowed'] + status above.
 7472:         #
 7473:         # When ON: D8 (apply_d8_release_policy, via sweep_integration) is the SINGLE binding
 7474:         # gate. We OVERRIDE both manifest['release_allowed'] AND manifest['status'] from the
 7475:         # D8 decision (so status and release_allowed cannot contradict — the double-gate the
 7476:         # Codex P2 forbids) and DEMOTE the legacy evaluator_gate to advisory metadata only.
 7477:         # claims/ledger/required-set are caller-supplied (four_role_inputs); the sweep never
 7478:         # synthesizes claim_ids or a coverage denominator (fail-closed: sweep_integration
 7479:         # raises on a blank id or an empty canonical required set).
 7480:         _four_role_on = os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE", "0").strip() in (
 7481:             "1", "true", "True",
 7482:         )
 7483:         # I-pipe-001 (#1226): track whether the 4-role D8 audit ACTUALLY ran+completed for this
 7484:         # run. Under the shared benchmark strict gate, a manifest may not read `success` unless this
 7485:         # is True (a benchmark run that fell onto the legacy single-evaluator gate, or never ran D8
 7486:         # at all, must NOT be marked success). Set True only after run_four_role_seam returns a
 7487:         # FourRoleEvaluationResult (incl. the fail-closed HELD result on timeout/error — D8 ran and
 7488:         # produced a binding decision). The legacy path leaves it False. Default OFF strict gate =>
 7489:         # the guard never fires => byte-identical.
 7490:         _d8_audit_ran = False
 7491:         # I-meta-008 (#1014) loud guard: PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE is on but NO transport was injected,
 7492:         # so the 4-role seam stays INERT and this run would silently use the legacy single-evaluator
 7493:         # gate. That is the exact "benchmark started via a legacy entrypoint" trap (#1014): the
 7494:         # 4-role benchmark ONLY runs via scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py (its main()/the
 7495:         # run_gate_b_query entrypoint INJECTS a transport).
 7496:         # I-run11-009 (#1055): the loud log alone was NOT enough — the run still fell onto the legacy
 7497:         # gate, whose own judge-unavailable fail-open could ship an unjudged report. A caller that
 7498:         # explicitly asked for 4-role mode (PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1) but supplied no transport is
 7499:         # MISCONFIGURED; it must FAIL CLOSED, never silently downgrade to the legacy gate (LAW II;
 7500:         # §-1.1). Legacy callers that never set PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE are unaffected (the guard does not
 7501:         # fire for them). Override the manifest built above to a hold.
 7502:         if _four_role_on and four_role_transport is None:
 7503:             print(
 7504:                 "[I-meta-008][GUARD] PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE is ON but no four_role_transport was "
 7505:                 "injected -- the 4-role benchmark seam is INERT. FAILING CLOSED (release HELD): "
 7506:                 "this run will NOT silently fall back to the legacy single-evaluator gate. The "
 7507:                 "native 4-role benchmark runs ONLY via scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py "
 7508:                 "(CLI: python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b)."
 7509:             )
 7510:             # Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: set the LOCAL summary_status/unified_status too, not just the
 7511:             # manifest fields. The tail writes `summary["status"] = summary_status` (~L4813) and
 7512:             # sweep_summary.{json,md} read that top-level status — without this a held misconfiguration
 7513:             # could still surface as "ok"/"warn" in the summary while the manifest says held.
 7514:             summary_status = "four_role_held"
 7515:             unified_status = to_unified_status(summary_status)
 7516:             manifest["release_allowed"] = False
 7517:             manifest["status"] = unified_status
 7518:             manifest["four_role_seam_inert"] = True
 7519:         if _four_role_on and four_role_transport is not None:
 7520:             # M3b: the seam resolves inputs (builder WINS over static four_role_inputs; both
 7521:             # None -> fail-closed), runs the SINGLE binding D8 gate, and persists the per-claim
 7522:             # audit map next to the run. The builder closure is called HERE — AFTER generation —
 7523:             # so it sees the finished `multi` report; the sweep still synthesizes nothing itself.
 7524:             from src.polaris_graph.roles.sweep_integration import (  # noqa: E402
 7525:                 FourRoleEvaluationResult,
 7526:                 build_evaluator_agrees_map,
 7527:                 run_four_role_seam,
 7528:             )
 7529:             # I-meta-008 (#1028-followup / hang): the seam runs SYNCHRONOUS httpx verifier calls.
 7530:             # Calling it directly in this async coroutine BLOCKS the event loop, and a wedged
 7531:             # verifier call (per-call 900s x up to 3 attempts) could hang the whole run ~45 min with
 7532:             # NO terminal artifact (run 5: 18-min poll() hang, no manifest.json). Run it on a worker
 7533:             # thread bounded by an overall wall-clock timeout and FAIL CLOSED on timeout/error
 7534:             # (release HELD — never release un-evaluated output, LAW II / §9.1). The timeout/error
 7535:             # path returns a real HELD FourRoleEvaluationResult so the existing post-seam manifest
 7536:             # block is byte-unchanged. Budget integrity: mirror the planner-LLM cost-reconciliation
 7537:             # (this file ~L1840) — copy_context for READ visibility + write the worker's accumulated
 7538:             # _RUN_COST_CTX delta back to the parent so PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN stays intact even though
 7539:             # the seam ran in a copied ThreadPoolExecutor context.
 7540:             import concurrent.futures as _seam_futures
 7541:             import contextvars as _seam_cv
 7542:             from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
 7543:                 BudgetExceededError as _SeamBudgetExceededError,
 7544:                 _RUN_COST_CTX as _seam_cost_ctx,
 7545:             )
 7546:             # I-run11-004: default raised 2400 -> 7200s. 2400 was the run-12 truncator — the
 7547:             # 4-role seam (now incl. the reasoning-ON MiniMax-M2 decomposition Sentinel + the xhigh
 7548:             # Mirror/Judge) takes minutes per claim, and a 2400s cap fired mid-run and held a
 7549:             # truncated manifest. PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (the per-call budget) stays 900.
 7550:             # LAW VI: env-overridable.
 7551:             _seam_timeout = float(
 7552:                 os.environ.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "7200")
 7553:             )
 7554:             _seam_kg_path = out_root / "verified_claim_graph_campaign.db"
 7555:             _seam_parent_cost = _seam_cost_ctx.get()
 7556:             _seam_cost_holder = [_seam_parent_cost]
 7557:             _seam_parent_ctx = _seam_cv.copy_context()
 7558: 
 7559:             # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: narrow progress callback — keeps the heartbeat's last_update_utc
 7560:             # advancing during the up-to-7200s verifier phase (the parent blocks on .result()).
 7561:             # Plain closure threaded as an argument (NOT a ContextVar), so it is unaffected by the
 7562:             # worker-thread context copy; _hb's atomic writer is concurrency-safe (unique temps).
 7563:             # Codex AC1-gate P1-1: a 1-element "live" flag (matches the _seam_cost_holder idiom;
 7564:             # GIL-atomic boolean read/write) so an ORPHANED worker that keeps running after a seam
 7565:             # TIMEOUT (parent does shutdown(wait=False)) can NOT overwrite the terminal heartbeat or
 7566:             # bleed a stale query's progress into the next query's state/run_status.json. The parent
 7567:             # clears it in the seam finally below; the worker's callback then no-ops.
 7568:             _seam_hb_live = [True]
 7569: 
 7570:             def _hb_claims(done: int, n: int) -> None:
 7571:                 # Codex AC1-gate iter-2 P1-1: check the live flag AND write the heartbeat under the
 7572:                 # SAME lock the parent holds when it clears the flag, so an entered-but-preempted
 7573:                 # worker callback can NOT land a stale four_role_progress after the parent has cleared
 7574:                 # the flag and written terminal status (the check-then-write race in iter-1's guard).
 7575:                 with _hb_lock:
 7576:                     if not _seam_hb_live[0]:
 7577:                         return
 7578:                     _hb("four_role_progress", claims_verified=done, claims_total=n)
 7579: 
 7580:             def _seam_worker() -> FourRoleEvaluationResult:
 7581:                 def _run_under_ctx() -> FourRoleEvaluationResult:
 7582:                     try:
 7583:                         return run_four_role_seam(
 7584:                             four_role_transport,
 7585:                             run_dir=run_dir,
 7586:                             timestamp=_utc_now_iso(),
 7587:                             four_role_input_builder=four_role_input_builder,
 7588:                             four_role_inputs=four_role_inputs,
 7589:                             multi=multi,
 7590:                             template=_template,
 7591:                             slug=q["slug"],
 7592:                             domain=q["domain"],
 7593:                             ev_pool=ev_pool,
 7594:                             # I-meta-002-q1d (#948): persist the snowball KG to the CAMPAIGN-scoped
 7595:                             # db so later questions in this sweep can reuse THIS question's VERIFIED
 7596:                             # claims (fail-closed read).
 7597:                             campaign_kg_db=str(_seam_kg_path),
 7598:                             # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: progress callback (anti-staleness during the
 7599:                             # up-to-7200s verifier phase). Opaque to sweep_integration.
 7600:                             heartbeat_claims_cb=_hb_claims,
 7601:                         )
 7602:                     finally:
 7603:                         # Capture accumulated verifier spend even on raise/timeout so the parent
 7604:                         # write-back below cannot under-report (budget-cap integrity, LAW II).
 7605:                         _seam_cost_holder[0] = _seam_cost_ctx.get()
 7606:                 return _seam_parent_ctx.run(_run_under_ctx)
 7607: 
 7608:             _seam_held_reason = None
 7609:             # Manage the executor MANUALLY (NOT `with`): a `with ThreadPoolExecutor` __exit__ calls
 7610:             # shutdown(wait=True), which BLOCKS until the wedged worker finishes — defeating the
 7611:             # timeout entirely (Codex diff-gate P1). shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True) returns
 7612:             # promptly so the held manifest is written AT PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS; the
 7613:             # orphaned worker thread exits on its own per-call timeout. Mirrors audit_ir/parallel_fetch.py.
 7614:             _hb("four_role_started")  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: bracket the up-to-7200s verifier phase
 7615:             _seam_pool = _seam_futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
 7616:             try:
 7617:                 four_role_result = _seam_pool.submit(_seam_worker).result(
 7618:                     timeout=_seam_timeout
 7619:                 )
 7620:             except _seam_futures.TimeoutError:
 7621:                 _seam_held_reason = "seam_timeout"
 7622:             except _SeamBudgetExceededError:
 7623:                 # A verifier cap breach (PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN) MUST propagate to the existing outer
 7624:                 # abort_budget_exceeded handler (the clean budget-abort contract) — NOT be swallowed
 7625:                 # into a held seam_error (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1). The worker's `finally` already
 7626:                 # updated the cost holder, so the `finally` below reconciles the full spend before
 7627:                 # this re-raises.
 7628:                 raise
 7629:             except Exception as _seam_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any OTHER seam failure must fail CLOSED
 7630:                 _seam_held_reason = f"seam_error:{type(_seam_exc).__name__}:{str(_seam_exc)[:120]}"
 7631:             finally:
 7632:                 # Codex AC1-gate iter-2 P1-1: clear the live flag UNDER _hb_lock so it is mutually
 7633:                 # exclusive with the worker's check-then-write in _hb_claims. An in-flight callback
 7634:                 # either completes its write BEFORE this acquires the lock, or is blocked and then sees
 7635:                 # the cleared flag and returns — so NO four_role_progress write can land after this
 7636:                 # point. Stops an orphaned post-timeout worker from overwriting the terminal heartbeat
 7637:                 # or bleeding stale progress into the next query's state/run_status.json.
 7638:                 with _hb_lock:
 7639:                     _seam_hb_live[0] = False
 7640:                 # NON-BLOCKING shutdown so a wedged worker cannot delay the held manifest (P1).
 7641:                 _seam_pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
 7642:                 # Budget reconciliation: on the SUCCESS path the worker's `finally` already updated
 7643:                 # `_seam_cost_holder`, so this write-back is EXACT. On the TIMEOUT path the worker is
 7644:                 # still running (holder == parent cost -> delta 0), so the in-flight verifier spend is
 7645:                 # NOT added to the parent cap — an acceptable tradeoff (Codex P2): the run is ABORTING
 7646:                 # (release HELD), the operator authorized spend, the orphaned worker's cost is bounded
 7647:                 # by its own per-call timeout, and prompt fail-closed termination outranks exact
 7648:                 # budget accounting on an already-aborted run.
 7649:                 _seam_delta = _seam_cost_holder[0] - _seam_parent_cost
 7650:                 if _seam_delta > 0:
 7651:                     _seam_cost_ctx.set(_seam_parent_cost + _seam_delta)
 7652:             if _seam_held_reason is not None:
 7653:                 _log(
 7654:                     f"[four_role]   SEAM {_seam_held_reason} after <= {_seam_timeout}s "
 7655:                     "-> release HELD (fail-closed; terminal manifest written)"
 7656:                 )
 7657:                 four_role_result = FourRoleEvaluationResult(
 7658:                     release_allowed=False,
 7659:                     held_reasons=[_seam_held_reason],
 7660:                     gaps=[],
 7661:                     final_verdicts={},
 7662:                     records=[],
 7663:                     coverage_fraction=0.0,
 7664:                     fabricated_occurrence_latched=False,
 7665:                     needs_rewrite=[],
 7666:                     kg_path=_seam_kg_path,
 7667:                 )
 7668:             # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: verifier phase complete — stamp claims processed.
 7669:             try:
 7670:                 _n_claims = len(getattr(four_role_result, "final_verdicts", {}) or {})
 7671:                 _hb("four_role_done", claims_verified=_n_claims, claims_total=_n_claims)
 7672:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
 7673:                 pass
 7674:             # Demote the legacy gate to ADVISORY metadata; D8 owns the headline decision.
 7675:             manifest["evaluator_gate_advisory"] = manifest.pop("evaluator_gate")
 7676:             # I-perm-001 (#1195) slice 2: under PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE the D8 BLOCK becomes a LABEL —
 7677:             # non-hard holds ship as disclosed gaps and the report RELEASES; only the
 7678:             # no-fabrication hard line (fabricated / zero-grounding) withholds; the clinical
 7679:             # safety floor ships the honest insufficient-safety report (normal render blocked).
 7680:             # release_allowed = outcome.released, so bundle.py refuses ONLY a hard block. Default
 7681:             # OFF: the legacy held/success path below is byte-identical.
 7682:             from src.polaris_graph.roles.release_policy import always_release_enabled
 7683:             _release_outcome = getattr(four_role_result, "release_outcome", None)
 7684:             if always_release_enabled() and _release_outcome is not None:
 7685:                 manifest["release_allowed"] = _release_outcome.released
 7686:                 summary_status = _release_outcome.status
 7687:                 # release_disclosure is the D8 LABEL snapshot. It deliberately does NOT carry a
 7688:                 # `released` field (Codex slice-2 P2): manifest["release_allowed"] is the SINGLE
 7689:                 # binding decision, and a later fail-closed gate (redaction / run-health) can flip
 7690:                 # it to False — a duplicated `released` here would go stale and contradict it.
 7691:                 manifest["release_disclosure"] = {
 7692:                     "hard_block": _release_outcome.hard_block,
 7693:                     "hard_block_reasons": list(_release_outcome.hard_block_reasons),
 7694:                     "normal_release_blocked": _release_outcome.normal_release_blocked,
 7695:                     "disclosed_gaps": list(_release_outcome.disclosed_gaps),
 7696:                     "release_quality_score": _release_outcome.release_quality_score,
 7697:                     "safety_floor": _release_outcome.safety_floor,
 7698:                 }
 7699:             else:
 7700:                 manifest["release_allowed"] = four_role_result.release_allowed
 7701:                 # Single binding status: released => success; held => release-blocking abort.
 7702:                 summary_status = (
 7703:                     "four_role_released"
 7704:                     if four_role_result.release_allowed
 7705:                     else "four_role_held"

 2467:     """Run the full honest pipeline on one query. Returns a summary dict.
 2468: 
 2469:     I-meta-002 sub-PR-6 (4-role wiring, GUARDED + default OFF): the 4-role evaluation path
 2470:     activates ONLY when BOTH ``four_role_transport`` is supplied (an explicit, INJECTED
 2471:     ``RoleTransport`` — there is NO default real transport; live transport is Gate-B after lock
 2472:     promotion) AND ``PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE`` is enabled (env "1"/"true"/"True"). When OFF (the
 2473:     default), the legacy evaluator-gate path below is byte-unchanged. ``four_role_inputs`` is the
 2474:     caller-supplied ``FourRoleEvaluationInputs`` (claims with EXISTING ids, the canonical
 2475:     required-element coverage ledger, and the required-S0 set) — the sweep NEVER synthesizes
 2476:     them from the report (that extraction is Gate-B). When the branch fires it delegates entirely
 2477:     to ``sweep_integration.run_four_role_seam`` (D8 is the single binding gate) and
 2478:     overrides BOTH ``manifest['release_allowed']`` AND ``manifest['status']`` from the D8
 2479:     decision, demoting the legacy evaluator_gate to ADVISORY metadata only.
 2480:

 2482:     closure (wired by ``scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py`` over the native
 2483:     ``build_native_gate_b_inputs`` + evidence normalization). When supplied it WINS over a
 2484:     static ``four_role_inputs``: it is called AFTER generation to PRODUCE the inputs+audit
 2485:     bundle, and the seam writes the per-claim audit map to ``four_role_claim_audit.json`` next
 2486:     to the run. The default (both None while the branch is OFF) leaves the legacy path
 2487:     byte-unchanged.
 2488:     """
 2489:     reset_run_cost()

 1385: def load_locked_questions(slugs: tuple[str, ...] | None = None) -> list[dict]:
 1386:     """Resolve the locked benchmark question(s) by FILTERING the existing `SWEEP_QUERIES`.
 1387: 
 1388:     `SWEEP_QUERIES` (in `scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3`) is the single runtime source of
 1389:     truth the sweep AND the routing tests already key off; this loader is a thin filter
 1390:     over it, NOT a new question source (§0 of the I-meta-007e brief). Imported LAZILY so
 1391:     importing `run_gate_b` opens no socket and pulls no big sweep file at module load.
 1392: 
 1393:     `slugs=None` resolves all 5 locked slugs (in `LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS` order); a tuple
 1394:     resolves that subset. Each requested slug must be one of the 5 locked slugs AND present
 1395:     in `SWEEP_QUERIES` exactly once with a `domain` — otherwise this fails LOUD (LAW II),
 1396:     never a silent skip / empty run.
 1397: 
 1398:     CALLER CONTRACT (env-preservation): importing `scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3` triggers
 1399:     `load_dotenv(override=False)` at its module top, which mutates `os.environ` from `.env`.
 1400:     The `--list` path (enumeration-only, NO spend) wraps the CALL to this loader in an
 1401:     os.environ snapshot/restore so it leaves the process env byte-identical; the real-run
 1402:     path (`--only`/`--all`) lets `.env` stand because the live run needs the keys.
 1403:     """
 1404:     requested = LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS if slugs is None else tuple(slugs)
 1405:     unknown = [s for s in requested if s not in LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS]
 1406:     if unknown:
 1407:         raise ValueError(
 1408:             f"load_locked_questions: slug(s) {unknown!r} are not locked benchmark slugs. "
 1409:             f"Valid locked slugs: {list(LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS)}."
 1410:         )
 1411: 
 1412:     from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import SWEEP_QUERIES
 1413: 
 1414:     resolved: list[dict] = []
 1415:     for slug in requested:
 1416:         matches = [q for q in SWEEP_QUERIES if q.get("slug") == slug]
 1417:         if not matches:
 1418:             raise ValueError(
 1419:                 f"load_locked_questions: locked slug {slug!r} is NOT registered in "
 1420:                 f"SWEEP_QUERIES — the loader filters the existing registration (no hardcoded "
 1421:                 f"slug->question fallback). Fix the SWEEP_QUERIES registration."
 1422:             )
 1423:         if len(matches) > 1:
 1424:             raise ValueError(
 1425:                 f"load_locked_questions: locked slug {slug!r} is registered {len(matches)} "
 1426:                 f"times in SWEEP_QUERIES; a benchmark slug must route to exactly one domain "
 1427:                 f"(fail-closed)."
 1428:             )
 1429:         entry = matches[0]
 1430:         if not entry.get("domain"):
 1431:             raise ValueError(
 1432:                 f"load_locked_questions: SWEEP_QUERIES entry for slug {slug!r} has no domain; "
 1433:                 f"the 4-role builder keys the frozen contract by (domain template, slug) — a "
 1434:                 f"domain-less registration cannot route (fail-closed)."
 1435:             )
 1436:         resolved.append(entry)
 1437:     return resolved
 1438: 
 1439: 
 1440: def _format_list_preview(questions: list[dict]) -> str:
 1441:     """Build the `--list` / `--dry-run` preview text — NO spend, NO network, NO env mutation.
 1442: 
 1443:     Uses ONLY the PURE readers (`four_role_transport_mode`, `verifier_model_slugs`,
 1444:     `assert_four_role_families_distinct`) — none opens a socket or mutates process env; each
 1445:     reads only the in-memory architecture lock / process env. It calls NONE of
 1446:     `run_gate_b_query` / `enable_four_role_mode` / `preflight_four_role_transport` /
 1447:     `build_gate_b_transport`. Surfaces, for the blind operator, exactly what a real run WOULD
 1448:     do before any money is authorized: the resolved questions, the active transport mode, the
 1449:     4 role slugs+families, and a DESCRIPTIVE preflight plan (not an executed preflight).
 1450:     """
 1451:     mode = four_role_transport_mode()
 1452:     families = assert_four_role_families_distinct()  # in-memory lock read; asserts 4 distinct
 1453:     verifier_slugs = verifier_model_slugs()
 1454:     generator_family = families.get("generator", "<unknown>")
 1455: 
 1456:     lines: list[str] = []
 1457:     lines.append("=" * 72)
 1458:     lines.append("GATE-B 4-ROLE BENCHMARK -- NO-SPEND PREVIEW (--list / --dry-run)")
 1459:     lines.append("NOTHING below spends money, opens a socket, or mutates the environment.")
 1460:     lines.append("=" * 72)
 1461:     lines.append(f"resolved questions: {len(questions)}")
 1462:     for q in questions:
 1463:         domain = q["domain"]
 1464:         slug = q["slug"]
 1465:         prompt = (q.get("question") or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
 1466:         prompt_preview = prompt[:100] + ("..." if len(prompt) > 100 else "")
 1467:         has_amplified = bool(q.get("amplified"))
 1468:         amplified_note = (
 1469:             f"curated amplified set ({len(q['amplified'])} entries)"
 1470:             if has_amplified
 1471:             else "NO curated amplified set (no-amplified stub -- elevated abort_corpus_inadequate risk)"
 1472:         )
 1473:         lines.append("")
 1474:         lines.append(f"  - slug={slug}")
 1475:         lines.append(f"    domain={domain}")
 1476:         lines.append(f"    scope_template=config/scope_templates/{domain}.yaml")
 1477:         lines.append(f"    retrieval={amplified_note}")
 1478:         lines.append(f"    prompt={prompt_preview!r}")
 1479:     lines.append("")
 1480:     lines.append("-" * 72)
 1481:     lines.append(f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT mode: {mode}")
 1482:     lines.append(f"  generator: family={generator_family} (runs upstream on OpenRouter; from lock)")
 1483:     for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES:
 1484:         lines.append(
 1485:             f"  {role}: slug={verifier_slugs[role]} family={families[role]}"
 1486:         )
 1487:     lines.append("")
 1488:     if mode == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
 1489:         plan = (
 1490:             "openrouter mode -> a REAL run would resolve the Mirror/Sentinel/Judge benchmark "
 1491:             "slugs against the OpenRouter /models catalog (incl. a reasoning-capability check) "
 1492:             "and assert the 4 families distinct, BEFORE spending any token."
 1493:         )
 1494:     else:
 1495:         plan = (
 1496:             "self_host mode -> a REAL run would resolve each PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL self-hosted "
 1497:             "endpoint and assert the 4 families distinct, BEFORE the sweep starts."
 1498:         )
 1499:     lines.append(f"preflight plan (descriptive, NOT executed): {plan}")
 1500:     lines.append("-" * 72)
 1501:     return "\n".join(lines)
 1502: 
 1503: 
 1504: def _resolve_benchmark_upload(
 1505:     upload_file: str, classification: str
 1506: ) -> tuple[list[dict], int]:
 1507:     """Ingest a local file and shape it as a Gate-B ``uploaded_documents`` entry.
 1508: 
 1509:     I-ready-010 (#1073). Returns ``(allowed, blocked_count)`` where ``allowed``
 1510:     is the sovereignty-cleared partition (the ONLY docs that may ground the

 succeeded in 1039ms:
 8575:     # BUG-M-206 + BUG-N-301 teardown: per-run ledger copy + run_id reset.
 8576:     try:
 8577:         n_ledger = write_per_run_cost_ledger(run_dir, run_id)
 8578:         summary["cost_ledger_entries"] = n_ledger
 8579:     except Exception as ledger_exc:
 8580:         logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
 8581:             "per-run ledger copy failed: %s", ledger_exc
 8582:         )
 8583: 
 8584:     # I-arch-001e: success + error_unexpected terminal events. The 5 abort
 8585:     # paths emit inline before their early returns; this catches the
 8586:     # success path (status set at line ~2865) plus the error path (status
 8587:     # set to "error" at line ~2873, mapped to error_unexpected here).
 8588:     if q.get("v6_mode") and q.get("external_run_id"):
 8589:         _final_status = summary.get("status") or "error_unexpected"
 8590:         if _final_status == "error":
 8591:             _final_status = "error_unexpected"
 8592:         # Use the manifest's unified status if available — it is the
 8593:         # canonical taxonomy (success / partial_* / abort_* / error_unexpected).
 8594:         _manifest = summary.get("manifest") or {}
 8595:         _manifest_status = _manifest.get("status")
 8596:         if _manifest_status:
 8597:             _final_status = _manifest_status
 8598:         emit_terminal_event(
 8599:             q.get("external_run_id"),
 8600:             _final_status,
 8601:             error_msg=summary.get("error"),
 8602:         )
 8603: 
 8604:     set_current_run_id(None)
 8605:     set_reasoning_sink(None)  # I-gen-004 (#496): release the run-scoped sink
 8606: 
 8607:     log_f.close()
 8608:     return summary

 8691:         print(f"REPLAY MODE — applying pin {replay_pin_obj.run_id}")
 8692:     print("=" * 72)
 8693:     print()
 8694: 
 8695:     # M-INT-0b: enter replay context if a pin was loaded.
 8696:     # apply_replay_plan returns a context manager that restores
 8697:     # the prior env snapshot on exit, so this whole sweep is
 8698:     # scoped + reversible. Use manual __enter__/__exit__ so we
 8699:     # don't have to re-indent the existing sweep loop body.
 8700:     from contextlib import nullcontext
 8701:     if replay_pin_obj is not None:
 8702:         replay_plan = build_replay_plan(replay_pin_obj)
 8703:         replay_ctx_mgr = apply_replay_plan(replay_plan)
 8704:     else:

 8706:

 8708:     # list from each query (when defined) and warms the cache so

 8711:     sweep_warm_summary: dict | None = None
 8712:     canonical_urls: list[str] = []
 8713:     for q in queries_to_run:
 8714:         for u in (q.get("canonical_urls") or []):
 8715:             if isinstance(u, str) and u not in canonical_urls:
 8716:                 canonical_urls.append(u)

 8719:             workspace_id="sweep",
 8720:             canonical_urls=canonical_urls,

 8722:         )
 8723:         if sweep_warm_summary is not None:

 8727:                 f"skipped={sweep_warm_summary['skipped_count']} "

 8729:             )
 8730: 
 8731:         # M-INT-3: post-warm freshness check.
 8732:         sweep_freshness_summary = _check_corpus_freshness(
 8733:             workspace_id="sweep",

 8747:             )
 8748: 
 8749:     # M-INT-7: sweep-level billing quota check + consume.
 8750:     # Charges one AUDIT_RUN_ENQUEUED unit per sweep invocation
 8751:     # (not per query — the sweep is the unit of work).
 8752:     # Per LAW II — wrap in try/except for defense-in-depth.
 8753:     #
 8754:     # Codex round-2 MEDIUM fix (v3): only consume quota when
 8755:     # there's actual work to bill for. v1+v2 consumed
 8756:     # unconditionally; an empty sweep (e.g. --only matched no
 8757:     # slugs, or SWEEP_QUERIES=[]) burned one unit per invocation.
 8758:     # Codex repro: cap=1 + SWEEP_QUERIES=[] → first empty sweep
 8759:     # returned rc=0 but used the unit; second empty sweep was
 8760:     # then refused. Fix: skip the helper entirely when
 8761:     # queries_to_run is empty.
 8762:     if not queries_to_run:

 8765:             "no charge incurred)"
 8766:         )
 8767:         billing_summary = None
 8768:     else:
 8769:         try:
