 8771:         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 8772:             print(f"[M-INT-7] WARN: billing quota helper raised: {exc}")
 8773:             billing_summary = None
 8774:     if billing_summary is not None:
 8775:         if billing_summary.get("exceeded"):
 8776:             # Codex round-1 HIGH fix (v2): EXCEEDED must GATE
 8777:             # the sweep, not just log. Per FINAL_PLAN M-INT-7:
 8778:             # "M-NEW billing/quota gates production". v1 only
 8779:             # printed the EXCEEDED line and continued to run
 8780:             # queries — Codex repro: exhausted quota + stubbed
 8781:             # run_one_query showed the loop still executed.
 8782:             # v2 returns rc=2 before the query loop, writing a
 8783:             # quota-refusal sweep summary so callers can detect
 8784:             # the refusal.
 8785:             print(

 8787:                 f"org={billing_summary['org_id']} "
 8788:                 f"reason={billing_summary.get('reason', '<n/a>')!r}"
 8789:             )
 8790:             print(
 8791:                 "[M-INT-7] sweep refused: org over quota; "
 8792:                 "no queries executed"
 8793:             )
 8794:             try:

 8796:                     "status": "abort_quota_exceeded",

 8798:                     "queries_attempted": 0,
 8799:                     "out_root": str(out_root),
 8800:                 }
 8801:                 refusal_path = out_root / "sweep_quota_refusal.json"
 8802:                 refusal_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 8803:                 refusal_path.write_text(
 8804:                     json.dumps(refusal_summary, indent=2, sort_keys=True)
 8805:                     + "\n",
 8806:                     encoding="utf-8",
 8807:                 )
 8808:             except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 8809:                 # Best-effort artifact write — don't gate on FS errors.
 8810:                 print(
 8811:                     f"[M-INT-7] WARN: refusal summary write failed: {exc}"
 8812:                 )
 8813:             return 2
 8814:         print(
 8815:             f"[M-INT-7] billing_quota: "
 8816:             f"org={billing_summary['org_id']} "
 8817:             f"used={billing_summary['used']} "
 8818:             f"cap={billing_summary['cap']} "
 8819:             f"remaining={billing_summary['remaining']}"
 8820:         )
 8821: 
 8822:     all_summaries: list[dict] = []
 8823:     replay_ctx_mgr.__enter__()
 8824:     sweep_exc_info: tuple | None = None
 8825:     try:
 8826:         for q in queries_to_run:
 8827:             print(f"\n>>> {q['domain']} / {q['slug']}")
 8828:             t0 = time.time()

 8835:                 enabled=args.pathB_gate, run_dir=_pathb_run_dir,
 8836:             ):
 8837:                 summary = await run_one_query(q, out_root)
 8838:             dt = time.time() - t0
 8839:             summary["wall_time_seconds"] = round(dt, 1)
 8840:             # M-INT-0b: capture a ModelPin for every run so later
 8841:             # replays can reproduce the exact runtime configuration.
 8842:             # Best-effort — failure does NOT gate the sweep summary.
 8843:             run_dir = Path(summary.get("run_dir", out_root))
 8844:             run_id = summary.get("run_id", "unknown")
 8845:             pin_path = _capture_run_pin(

 succeeded in 1211ms:
 1510:     is the sovereignty-cleared partition (the ONLY docs that may ground the
 1511:     external-generator benchmark) in the ``{document_id, classification,
 1512:     filename, chunks}`` shape the sweep injection
 1513:     (``scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3458``) + ``build_upload_evidence_rows``
 1514:     already consume, and ``blocked_count`` is the number the sovereignty router
 1515:     rejected.
 1516: 
 1517:     Mirrors the production worker resolver
 1518:     (``polaris_v6.api.runs._resolve_uploaded_documents``): same ``chunk_text``
 1519:     chunking, same dict shape, same fail-loud-on-empty (LAW II — a silent
 1520:     zero-evidence run would mislead the operator). Imports are LAZY so importing
 1521:     ``run_gate_b`` opens no socket and pulls neither ``document_ingester`` nor
 1522:     ``fastapi`` (the module's NO-SPEND/NO-NETWORK-at-import invariant); they load
 1523:     only when ``--upload-file`` is actually used.
 1524: 
 1525:     Raises:
 1526:         FileNotFoundError: the path does not exist.
 1527:         ValueError: the file yields no extractable text (empty/whitespace).
 1528:     """
 1529:     import asyncio
 1530:     from pathlib import Path
 1531: 
 1532:     # I-ready-018 (#1138): these stay BARE + LAZY (inside the resolver) — the NO-SPEND/NO-NETWORK-
 1533:     # at-import invariant (test_upload_imports_are_lazy_inside_the_resolver) requires the bare module
 1534:     # paths inside the function body, NOT hoisted to module top. They are root-safe because
 1535:     # ``install_import_root_alias()`` ran at module load (top of this file), so bare ``polaris_graph``/
 1536:     # ``polaris_v6`` alias to their ``src.`` counterparts even under the root-only run launch.
 1537:     from polaris_graph.document_ingester import (
 1538:         DocumentIngester,
 1539:         DocumentIngestionError,
 1540:     )
 1541:     from polaris_v6.adapters.upload_evidence import (
 1542:         partition_uploads_by_sovereignty,
 1543:     )
 1544:     from polaris_v6.api.upload import chunk_text
 1545: 
 1546:     path = Path(upload_file)
 1547:     if not path.exists():
 1548:         raise FileNotFoundError(f"--upload-file {upload_file!r} does not exist")
 1549: 
 1550:     try:
 1551:         result = asyncio.run(DocumentIngester().ingest(path))
 1552:     except DocumentIngestionError as exc:
 1553:         # I-ready-010 diff-gate iter-1 P2: an unsupported extension / oversized
 1554:         # file / missing parser dependency must FAIL LOUD as a clean pre-spend
 1555:         # rc2 (ValueError -> main's parser.error), never a raw traceback/rc1.
 1556:         raise ValueError(f"--upload-file {upload_file!r}: {exc}") from exc
 1557:     content = (result.get("content") or "").strip()
 1558:     if not content:
 1559:         raise ValueError(
 1560:             f"--upload-file {upload_file!r} yielded no extractable text "
 1561:             "(empty/whitespace extraction) — a zero-evidence benchmark run "
 1562:             "would mislead the operator (LAW II fail-loud)."
 1563:         )
 1564:     chunks = chunk_text(content)
 1565:     if not chunks:
 1566:         raise ValueError(
 1567:             f"--upload-file {upload_file!r} produced no grounding chunks."
 1568:         )
 1569: 
 1570:     doc = {
 1571:         "document_id": result["doc_id"],
 1572:         "classification": classification,
 1573:         "filename": path.name,
 1574:         "chunks": chunks,
 1575:     }
 1576:     allowed, blocked = partition_uploads_by_sovereignty([doc])
 1577:     return allowed, len(blocked)

 1580: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
 1581:     """Gate-B CLI launcher (I-meta-008 #1014). The ONLY entrypoint that runs the 4-role
 1582:     benchmark pipeline. NO SPEND / NO NETWORK at import — all runtime work is here.
 1583: 
 1584:     Exactly one of {--only, --all, --list} is required. `--list`/`--dry-run` previews the
 1585:     resolved config with NO spend, NO network, and NO env mutation. A real run
 1586:     (`--only <slug>` / `--all`) delegates ENTIRELY to `run_gate_b_query` per question (which
 1587:     owns the env-flips, the fail-loud preflight BEFORE any token, the stage marker, and
 1588:     `run_one_query`). Returns 0 on success, 2 on a usage/loader error, 1 if any question run
 1589:     does not reach a `success`/`partial_*` status.
 1590:     """
 1591:     import argparse
 1592: 
 1593:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
 1594:         prog="run_gate_b",
 1595:         description=(
 1596:             "Gate-B 4-role benchmark launcher (I-meta-008 #1014) — the ONLY CLI that fires "
 1597:             "the native 4-role evaluation seam. Runs the LOCKED golden DRB-EN questions."
 1598:         ),
 1599:     )
 1600:     selection = parser.add_mutually_exclusive_group(required=True)
 1601:     selection.add_argument(
 1602:         "--only", type=str, default=None, metavar="SLUG",
 1603:         help=f"Run ONE locked question by slug. Valid: {', '.join(LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS)}.",
 1604:     )
 1605:     selection.add_argument(
 1606:         "--all", action="store_true",
 1607:         help="Run ALL 5 locked benchmark questions (sequentially, one at a time).",
 1608:     )
 1609:     selection.add_argument(
 1610:         "--list", "--dry-run", dest="list_only", action="store_true",
 1611:         help=(
 1612:             "NO-SPEND, NO-NETWORK, NO-ENV-MUTATION preview: print the resolved questions, the "
 1613:             "active PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT mode, the 4 role slugs/families, and the preflight "
 1614:             "plan. Runs NO question and spends nothing."
 1615:         ),
 1616:     )
 1617:     parser.add_argument(
 1618:         "--out-root", type=str, default=DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT, metavar="DIR",
 1619:         help=f"Output root (tree <out_root>/<domain>/<slug>). Default: {DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}.",
 1620:     )
 1621:     # I-ready-010 (#1073): attach an uploaded document to the benchmark questions so
 1622:     # the upload->citable-evidence capability is live + measurable on the beat-both
 1623:     # path. NOT in the selection group (combines with --only/--all; ignored by --list,
 1624:     # which returns before the real-run resolve).
 1625:     parser.add_argument(
 1626:         "--upload-file", type=str, default=None, metavar="PATH",
 1627:         help=(
 1628:             "Attach a local document as grounding evidence to EVERY benchmark question "
 1629:             "in this run. Ingested in-process via DocumentIngester; chunked + injected "
 1630:             "through the SAME build_upload_evidence_rows -> strict_verify -> 4-role path "
 1631:             "as the UI. Only PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC content clears the sovereignty router for "
 1632:             "the external generator."
 1633:         ),
 1634:     )
 1635:     parser.add_argument(
 1636:         "--upload-classification", type=str, default="UNKNOWN",
 1637:         choices=["PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC", "CAN_REAL", "PRIVATE", "CLIENT", "UNKNOWN"],
 1638:         metavar="CLASS",
 1639:         help=(
 1640:             "Sovereignty classification of --upload-file (default UNKNOWN, conservative). "
 1641:             "Only PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC clears the sovereignty router for the external generator; "
 1642:             "any other value fails loud (the doc could never become benchmark evidence)."
 1643:         ),
 1644:     )
 1645:     args = parser.parse_args(argv)
 1646: 
 1647:     # --only validates against the locked slug set BEFORE any env-touching import (fail loud).
 1648:     if args.only is not None and args.only not in LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS:
 1649:         parser.error(
 1650:             f"--only {args.only!r} is not a locked benchmark slug. "
 1651:             f"Valid: {', '.join(LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS)}."
 1652:         )
 1653: 
 1654:     requested_slugs: tuple[str, ...] | None
 1655:     if args.only is not None:
 1656:         requested_slugs = (args.only,)
 1657:     else:
 1658:         requested_slugs = None  # --all and --list both resolve the full set
 1659: 
 1660:     if args.list_only:
 1661:         # ENUMERATION-ONLY path (AC-3): snapshot os.environ BEFORE importing the SWEEP_QUERIES
 1662:         # source (its `load_dotenv(override=False)` mutates env from `.env`), read the transport
 1663:         # mode + slugs/families WHILE `.env` is applied (so the preview reports what a real run
 1664:         # would use), then restore the full mapping in `finally` so `--list` leaves the process
 1665:         # environment byte-identical even on error.
 1666:         env_snapshot = dict(os.environ)
 1667:         try:
 1668:             questions = load_locked_questions(requested_slugs)
 1669:             preview = _format_list_preview(questions)
 1670:         finally:
 1671:             os.environ.clear()
 1672:             os.environ.update(env_snapshot)
 1673:         print(preview)
 1674:         return 0
 1675: 
 1676:     # --- REAL RUN (--only / --all): operator-authorized spend. `.env` may load normally. ---
 1677:     import asyncio
 1678: 
 1679:     out_root = Path(args.out_root)
 1680:     # Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2: apply the full-capability FLOOR slate HERE — BEFORE
 1681:     # load_locked_questions() imports SWEEP_QUERIES -> run_honest_sweep_r3 -> live_retriever, which cache
 1682:     # PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX / PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT / PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND as import-time module
 1683:     # constants. Applying it after that import would leave those constants at the low .env/defaults.
 1684:     # run_gate_b_query re-applies it (idempotent) for the direct-call path + per-query.
 1685:     apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate()
 1686:     questions = load_locked_questions(requested_slugs)
 1687:     # Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2: load_locked_questions has now imported the sweep -> live_retriever ->
 1688:     # state WITH the slate applied above, so the import-time constants are at full capability. Validate in
 1689:     # a FRESH SUBPROCESS (test_run_gate_b_import_order) rather than here — an in-process runtime gate would
 1690:     # false-fire under pytest where those modules are pre-imported with defaults. The slate-before-import
 1691:     # ORDER above is the fix; preflight_import_time_constants() is the assertion the subprocess test runs.
 1692:     # I-ready-010 (#1073): resolve the optional --upload-file ONCE (before the loop)
 1693:     # into the sovereignty-cleared `uploaded_documents` shape. FAIL LOUD here (before
 1694:     # any token is spent) on a missing/empty file or a non-PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC doc that the
 1695:     # external-generator benchmark could never use — never a silent zero-upload run.
 1696:     _attach_uploads: list[dict] = []
 1697:     _upload_blocked = 0
 1698:     if args.upload_file:
 1699:         try:
 1700:             _attach_uploads, _upload_blocked = _resolve_benchmark_upload(
 1701:                 args.upload_file, args.upload_classification
 1702:             )
 1703:         except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError) as exc:
 1704:             parser.error(str(exc))
 1705:         if not _attach_uploads:
 1706:             parser.error(
 1707:                 f"--upload-file {args.upload_file!r} classified "
 1708:                 f"{args.upload_classification!r} is EXTERNAL_LEAK_FORBIDDEN — only "
 1709:                 "PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC may ground the external-generator benchmark. "
 1710:                 "Reclassify (--upload-classification PUBLIC_SYNTHETIC) or remove it."
 1711:             )
 1712:         print(
 1713:             f"attached upload: {args.upload_file} "
 1714:             f"({len(_attach_uploads[0]['chunks'])} chunk(s), "
 1715:             f"classification={args.upload_classification}, blocked={_upload_blocked})"
 1716:         )
 1717: 
 1718:     print("=" * 72)
 1719:     print(f"GATE-B 4-ROLE BENCHMARK RUN -- {len(questions)} question(s)")
 1720:     print(f"transport mode: {four_role_transport_mode()}")
 1721:     print(f"output root: {out_root}")
 1722:     print("=" * 72)
 1723: 
 1724:     overall_rc = 0
 1725:     # I-obs-001 #1141 AC1: enumerate so the heartbeat can report "query N of 5".
 1726:     for query_index, q in enumerate(questions, 1):
 1727:         slug = q["slug"]
 1728:         domain = q["domain"]
 1729:         if _attach_uploads:
 1730:             # COPY — load_locked_questions returns the live SWEEP_QUERIES dict
 1731:             # (run_gate_b.py:886,893); mutating it would leak `uploaded_documents`
 1732:             # into the global registry, poisoning the routing tests + later runs in
 1733:             # the same process. `uploaded_documents` is a fresh key, read-only-consumed
 1734:             # by run_one_query -> build_upload_evidence_rows.
 1735:             q = dict(q)
 1736:             q["uploaded_documents"] = _attach_uploads
 1737:             q["uploaded_documents_blocked_count"] = _upload_blocked
 1738:         print(f"\n>>> {domain} / {slug}")
 1739:         # Sequential — one question at a time (CLAUDE.md §8.4 resource discipline; no parallel
 1740:         # runs). Each delegates entirely to the existing 4-role entrypoint.
 1741:         summary = asyncio.run(
 1742:             run_gate_b_query(
 1743:                 q, out_root, query_index=query_index, query_total=len(questions)
 1744:             )
 1745:         )
 1746:         status = summary.get("status", "<no-status>")
 1747:         print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: status={status}")
 1748:         if not (status == "success" or str(status).startswith("partial")):
 1749:             overall_rc = 1
 1750:     return overall_rc

 1754:     import sys

  970: 
  971: def apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate() -> None:
  972:     """Make the full-capability slate AUTHORITATIVE over .env / conservative defaults (FLOOR semantics).
  973: 
  974:     Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-1: ``setdefault`` was WRONG. ``load_dotenv`` (openrouter_client import)
  975:     puts .env values into ``os.environ`` BEFORE this slate runs, so a conservative .env default
  976:     (e.g. ``PG_MOST_MAX_EVIDENCE=300``, ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND=6``) SURVIVED the setdefault and
  977:     silently throttled the benchmark below the slate. Fix: every numeric key is a **FLOOR** —
  978:     ``max(existing, slate)`` — so a HIGHER operator/.env value (more capability, e.g.
  979:     ``PG_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600`` in .env) is KEPT, but a LOWER default is RAISED to full capability.
  980:     No silent downgrade in either direction (operator no-downgrade directive). Feature flags in
  981:     ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS`` are FORCED ON (a benchmark feature off is a capability downgrade).
  982:     Exact-value flags in ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS`` are forced to their slate value.
  983: 
  984:     Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2: MUST run BEFORE the sweep / live_retriever / state imports, which cache
  985:     ``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` / ``PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT`` / ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND`` as import-time module
  986:     constants. ``main()`` applies it before ``load_locked_questions()``; ``run_gate_b_query`` applies it
  987:     before the sweep import. ``preflight_full_capability`` then validates the LIVE module constants so a
  988:     too-late application is caught fail-closed.
  989:     """
  990:     for name, value in _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE.items():
  991:         if name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS or name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS:
  992:             os.environ[name] = value                       # force exact value — no silent benchmark drift
  993:             continue
  994:         try:
  995:             current = float(os.environ.get(name, value))
  996:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
  997:             current = float(value)
  998:         os.environ[name] = str(int(max(current, float(value))))   # FLOOR: raise-to-slate, keep-if-higher
  999:     # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN is an import-time constant in openrouter_client (cached before this slate via
 1000:     # the role import chain), so the floor above only fixes the env; sync the live module global too so
 1001:     # check_run_budget enforces the floored cap and run_one_query's manifest reads it (Codex iter-1 P1-2).
 1002:     from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import set_max_cost_per_run
 1003:     set_max_cost_per_run(float(os.environ["PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN"]))

 1006: def preflight_full_capability() -> None:
 1007:     """FAIL CLOSED if the effective benchmark config is below full capability or unobservable — so a
 1008:     silent throttle (the ~40-URL bug) can NEVER reach a paid run undetected. Raises RuntimeError."""
 1009:     for name, floor in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_FLOORS.items():
 1010:         # mirror run_one_query's read of the real PG_SWEEP_* knob (defaults 12/12/40 if absent).
 1011:         _defaults = {"PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP": "40", "PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER": "12", "PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2": "12"}
 1012:         try:
 1013:             eff = int(os.getenv(name, _defaults.get(name, "0")))
 1014:         except ValueError:
 1015:             raise RuntimeError(f"benchmark preflight: {name}={os.getenv(name)!r} is not an int")
 1016:         if eff < floor:
 1017:             raise RuntimeError(
 1018:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {name}={eff} < full-capability floor {floor} — the run "
 1019:                 f"would be SILENTLY THROTTLED (the ~40-URL bug). Set {name}>={floor} or fix the slate."
 1020:             )
 1021:     for flag in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS:
 1022:         if os.getenv(flag, "0").strip() not in ("1", "true", "True"):
 1023:             raise RuntimeError(
 1024:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {flag} is not enabled — the feature is dead-by-config "
 1025:                 f"or its firing is unobservable (tracker off). Enable it before the run."
 1026:             )
 1027:     # I-ready-017 FX-03 (#1107) Codex iter-2 P1: the cited-span window must stay BOUNDED. An oversized
 1028:     # PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES makes _cited_window_text expand to the whole direct_quote — BUG-02
 1029:     # whole-doc evidence WITH the cited-span flag on. The slate force-exacts it to the 400-byte policy;
 1030:     # this fails closed if it is ever outside (0, ceiling] (force-exact removed / slate value changed).
 1031:     if os.getenv("PG_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
 1032:         try:
 1033:             _win = int(os.getenv("PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES", "400"))
 1034:         except ValueError:
 1035:             raise RuntimeError(
 1036:                 "benchmark preflight FAILED: PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES="
 1037:                 f"{os.getenv('PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES')!r} is not an int."
 1038:             )
 1039:         if _win <= 0 or _win > _BENCHMARK_SPAN_WINDOW_MAX_BYTES:
 1040:             raise RuntimeError(
 1041:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES={_win} is outside "
 1042:                 f"(0, {_BENCHMARK_SPAN_WINDOW_MAX_BYTES}] — an oversized cited-span window expands to the "
 1043:                 f"whole record (BUG-02 whole-doc evidence with the cited-span flag on)."
 1044:             )
 1045:     for flag in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_OFF_FLAGS:
 1046:         if os.getenv(flag, "1").strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
 1047:             raise RuntimeError(
 1048:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {flag} is enabled — Gate-B report.md would include the "
 1049:                 f"un-span-verified Analyst Synthesis layer. Set {flag}=0 for the verified-only benchmark."
 1050:             )
 1051:     # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: the binding faithfulness verifier MUST be at "enforce" — otherwise the
 1052:     # entailment judge does not bind as a drop gate and/or a judge_error fails OPEN (unverified clinical
 1053:     # claims ship as "verified"). Fail closed before any spend.

 1055:         if os.getenv(mode_env, "off").strip().lower() != "enforce":
 1056:             raise RuntimeError(
 1057:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {mode_env}={os.getenv(mode_env)!r} != 'enforce' — the "
 1058:                 f"BINDING faithfulness verifier is degraded (entailment not binding or judge_error fails "
 1059:                 f"OPEN). Set {mode_env}=enforce before the run."
 1060:             )
 1061:     # Codex diff-gate I-cap-005 P1-2: validate the EFFECTIVE (live) per-run budget cap — reads the
 1062:     # module global the guard actually enforces (synced by the slate via set_max_cost_per_run), NOT just

 1127: def preflight_import_time_constants() -> None:
 1128:     """FAIL CLOSED if an IMPORT-TIME module constant is below its full-capability floor — the regression
 1129:     guard for the import-order bug (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2). ``live_retriever`` /``state`` cache
 1130:     ``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` / ``PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT`` / ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND`` at IMPORT, so reading
 1131:     os.getenv is NOT enough — if the slate ran AFTER the import the env is right but the constant is stale.
 1132: 
 1133:     Called ONLY on the REAL CLI path (``main()``), AFTER ``load_locked_questions()`` has done the import
 1134:     with the slate already applied — so it validates the production import order. It is intentionally NOT
 1135:     in ``preflight_full_capability`` (which tests call): in a pytest process those modules are pre-imported
 1136:     with defaults, which is a test artifact, not a production throttle."""
 1137:     import importlib
 1138:     for module_path, attr, floor in _BENCHMARK_IMPORT_TIME_CONSTANT_FLOORS:
 1139:         try:
 1140:             mod = importlib.import_module(module_path)
 1141:             eff = float(getattr(mod, attr))
 1142:         except (ImportError, AttributeError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
 1143:             raise RuntimeError(f"benchmark preflight: cannot read {module_path}.{attr}: {exc}")
 1144:         if eff < floor:
 1145:             raise RuntimeError(
 1146:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {module_path}.{attr}={eff:g} < full-capability floor "
 1147:                 f"{floor} — the slate was applied AFTER that module was imported, so the import-time "
 1148:                 f"constant is STALE. Apply the slate before that import (it runs before "
 1149:                 f"load_locked_questions in main())."
 1150:             )
 1151:

 1153: # I-ready-019 (#1146): EMPTY per the operator credibility-model directive (2026-06-07). FIX-JO
 1154: # (I-ready-017 #1100/#1134) originally placed drb_72_ai_labor here because that AI-labor LITERATURE
 1155: # REVIEW question's text says "only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles". The
 1156: # operator REVERSED that: "many things are not journal, but still credibility — mainstream news,
 1157: # gov, some credible sites; why you have tendency to make yourself into tunnel view." No benchmark
 1158: # question is journal-only. Credibility is multi-source and domain-aware (peer-reviewed journals +
 1159: # government statistics + working papers (NBER/IZA) + reputable institutes + quality news);
 1160: # faithfulness is enforced by the verify gates (strict_verify / 4-role D8 / provenance / two-family),

 1170: 
 1171: def apply_journal_only_for_slug(slug: str) -> bool:
 1172:     """DETERMINISTICALLY set/clear the journal_only runtime flag for ONE benchmark slug.
 1173: 
 1174:     Sets ``PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY=1`` iff ``slug`` is a journal_only benchmark slug
 1175:     (``JOURNAL_ONLY_BENCHMARK_SLUGS``); otherwise it REMOVES the flag from the env so a value
 1176:     left over from a prior journal_only slug in the SAME process (e.g. the ``--all`` loop, or a
 1177:     conservative ``.env`` default) can NEVER carry into a non-journal slug. Returns True iff the
 1178:     flag was set ON for this slug.
 1179: 
 1180:     This is the per-question seam (mirrors how the slate is applied per ``run_gate_b_query``): it
 1181:     runs INSIDE ``run_gate_b_query`` so the flag is correct for exactly the question being run,
 1182:     NOT added to the always-on global ``_FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE`` (which would blanket-
 1183:     activate journal_only for every workforce query and kill the generic T3 path). The downstream
 1184:     consumer (``run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query`` -> ``_jo_active`` -> ``journal_only_active``)
 1185:     reads ``JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG`` from the env at run time AND requires the protocol's
 1186:     ``source_restriction: journal_only``, so setting it here before the ``run_one_query`` call is
 1187:     what makes journal_only fire for the journal-only slug only. Default non-journal runs are
 1188:     byte-identical (the flag is removed, not set).
 1189:     """
 1190:     if slug in JOURNAL_ONLY_BENCHMARK_SLUGS:
 1191:         os.environ[JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG] = "1"
 1192:         return True
 1193:     os.environ.pop(JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG, None)
 1194:     return False
 1195: 
 1196: 
 1197: async def run_gate_b_query(
 1198:     q: dict,

 1206:     """Run ONE query through the honest sweep with the native 4-role Gate-B seam ACTIVE.
 1207: 
 1208:     WIRING ONLY (LAW VII CLI isolation): activates `PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE`, builds the verifier
 1209:     `transport` (unless one is injected — the offline seam test injects a FAKE), builds the
 1210:     argument-taking Gate-B builder closure, and hands transport + builder into `run_one_query`.
 1211:     The seam calls the builder AFTER generation with the run-local objects.
 1212: 
 1213:     Transport routing is ENV-gated (`PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT`, I-meta-007d): "openrouter"
 1214:     (DEFAULT, dev/benchmark — saves GPU cost; OpenRouter is a US router, dev/benchmark ONLY) or
 1215:     "self_host" (the lock's sovereign route). The LIVE preflight matches the mode
 1216:     (`preflight_four_role_transport`).
 1217: 
 1218:     This function is the Gate-B production entrypoint; its LIVE invocation (real endpoints + real
 1219:     spend) is the later operator-authorized canary. It is NEVER invoked against a live endpoint by
 1220:     any test — the seam test exercises `run_four_role_seam` with a fake transport directly.

 succeeded in 1186ms:
    1: """I-cap-002 feature 4/4 (#1060): NLI entailment as an ADDITIVE, advisory benchmark annotation.
    2: 
    3: strict_verify is regex/numeric — it can pass a qualitative-negation hallucination ("drug X did NOT
    4: reduce mortality") whose numbers/content-words still match the cited span. NLI is the **second validator
    5: path**: for each delivered sentence it scores whether the CITED evidence span entails the sentence; a low
    6: entailment prob is a hallucination the regex missed. This is ADVISORY only — it annotates the manifest, it
    7: never changes ``release_allowed``/``status`` (the 4-role D8 seam remains the single binding gate).
    8: 
    9: **No silent degrade (LAW II + operator no-downgrade directive):** ``annotate_nli_entailment`` raises
   10: ``NliUnavailableError`` when the model/deps cannot load — it NEVER returns an empty "clean" result that
   11: reads as "NLI verified". The caller records ``nli_status:"unavailable"`` LOUDLY in the manifest. (This is
   12: the deliberate difference from ``nli_verifier.verify_evidence_nli``, which returns ``[]`` on a missing model.)
   13: 
   14: Heavy deps (``torch`` / ``transformers`` / ``minicheck``) are imported LAZILY inside the async function, so
   15: importing this module pulls nothing heavy; offline tests mock the scorer (no torch). The live model runs on
   16: the VM (CLAUDE.md §8.4).
   17: """
   18: 
   19: from __future__ import annotations
   20: 
   21: import os
   22: import re
   23: from typing import Any
   24: 
   25: from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import strip_tokens
   26: 
   27: # Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2.1: the benchmark's provenance offsets index into the row's
   28: # ``direct_quote`` / ``statement`` (the field strict_verify validates against), so those MUST be tried
   29: # FIRST — slicing ``full_text`` for a row that carries both would pick the wrong byte range.
   30: _SPAN_TEXT_FIELDS = ("direct_quote", "statement", "full_text", "snippet", "text", "source_text")
   31: 
   32: # Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2.3: strip_tokens removes only ``[#ev:...]``; a pre-resolve sentence can still
   33: # carry ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` calc tokens and ``(atom_NNN)`` markers that are cleaned from the
   34: # final delivered prose elsewhere. Strip those too, or they create advisory false NLI disputes.
   35: _RESIDUAL_ARTIFACT_RE = re.compile(r"\[#calc:[^\]]*\]|\(?\batom_\d+\b\)?")
   36: 
   37: 
   38: class NliUnavailableError(RuntimeError):
   39:     """The NLI model/deps could not be loaded. Raised (never silently swallowed) so the caller can
   40:     surface ``nli_status:"unavailable"`` rather than report a false clean pass."""
   41: 
   42: 
   43: def _resolve_span_text(row: dict[str, Any], start: int, end: int) -> str:
   44:     """Slice the cited [start:end] span out of an evidence row's text (get_span_text semantics)."""
   45:     text = ""
   46:     for field in _SPAN_TEXT_FIELDS:
   47:         value = row.get(field)
   48:         if isinstance(value, str) and value:
   49:             text = value
   50:             break
   51:     if not text or start < 0 or end > len(text) or start > end:
   52:         return ""
   53:     return text[start:end]
   54: 
   55: 
   56: def build_nli_pairs(
   57:     kept_sentences: list[dict[str, Any]],
   58:     ev_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
   59: ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   60:     """Build ``[{sentence, span, section, evidence_id}]`` NLI pairs from delivered kept sentences.
   61: 
   62:     Per Codex brief-gate P2: (1) the sentence is CLEANED of ``[#ev:...]`` / atom artifacts via
   63:     ``strip_tokens`` so citation tokens don't create false NLI disputes; (2) ALL cited spans of the
   64:     sentence are concatenated as the premise (not first-token-only), so a sentence grounded by multiple
   65:     spans is not over-disputed. A sentence with no cleanable text or no resolvable span is skipped.
   66: 
   67:     ``kept_sentences``: ``[{"sentence": str, "tokens": [{"evidence_id","start","end"}], "section": str}]``.
   68:     Pure (no model, no network).
   69:     """
   70:     pairs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   71:     for ks in kept_sentences or []:
   72:         raw = ks.get("sentence") or ""
   73:         # P2.1: drop [#ev:...] tokens; P2.3: also drop [#calc:...] + (atom_NNN) residuals; collapse ws.
   74:         claim = _RESIDUAL_ARTIFACT_RE.sub(" ", strip_tokens(raw))
   75:         claim = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", claim).strip()
   76:         if not claim:
   77:             continue
   78:         spans: list[str] = []
   79:         first_ev_id = ""
   80:         for tok in ks.get("tokens", []) or []:
   81:             ev_id = tok.get("evidence_id") or ""
   82:             row = ev_pool.get(ev_id)
   83:             if not isinstance(row, dict):
   84:                 continue
   85:             span = _resolve_span_text(row, int(tok.get("start", -1)), int(tok.get("end", -1)))
   86:             if span:
   87:                 spans.append(span)
   88:                 if not first_ev_id:
   89:                     first_ev_id = ev_id
   90:         premise = " ".join(spans).strip()
   91:         if not premise:
   92:             continue  # no resolvable cited span -> nothing to entail against; skip
   93:         pairs.append({
   94:             "sentence": claim,
   95:             "span": premise,
   96:             "section": ks.get("section", ""),
   97:             "evidence_id": first_ev_id,
   98:         })
   99:     return pairs
  100: 
  101: 
  102: async def annotate_nli_entailment(
  103:     pairs: list[dict[str, Any]],
  104: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
  105:     """Score each (span ⊨ sentence) pair with the frontier LLM entailment judge; ADVISORY annotation.
  106: 
  107:     I-cap-003 (#1066): the scoring backend is now the project's LLM entailment judge
  108:     (``src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge``) — a frontier OPEN-weight model (default
  109:     ``google/gemma-4-31b-it``) via OpenRouter, family-segregated from the generator, already used by
  110:     strict_verify. This REPLACES the old flan-t5/minicheck path (an old/weak F1-62.1 encoder that was
  111:     git-only and unavailable on the CPU VM → the previous ``nli_status:unavailable`` bug).
  112: 
  113:     Each pair yields a verdict ``ENTAILED | NEUTRAL | CONTRADICTED``. ``disputed`` = NEUTRAL ∪
  114:     CONTRADICTED (strict_verify's contract is "no unsupported additions"; the per-sentence ``verdict``
  115:     distinguishes them). Returns ``{nli_status:"ok", judge, model, sentences_checked, entailed_count,
  116:     neutral_count, contradicted_count, disputed_count, disputed:[…], advisory:True}``.
  117: 
  118:     FAIL-LOUD only on a genuine config error: a missing ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` raises
  119:     ``NliUnavailableError`` (surfaced as ``nli_status:"unavailable"``, never a silent pass). The judge is
  120:     called SYNCHRONOUSLY (NOT offloaded to a thread) so its per-call cost accumulates in the run's
  121:     ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` ContextVar and ``BudgetExceededError`` (re-raised inside ``judge.judge``) propagates
  122:     out to abort the run on a cap breach. A family-segregation error from ``_get_judge()`` PROPAGATES
  123:     (not masked as "unavailable").
  124:     """
  125:     if not pairs:                                   # fast path — needs no API key / no judge
  126:         return {
  127:             "nli_status": "ok", "judge": "llm_entailment", "sentences_checked": 0,
  128:             "sentences_scored": 0, "entailed_count": 0, "neutral_count": 0, "contradicted_count": 0,
  129:             "disputed_count": 0, "disputed": [], "judge_error_count": 0, "judge_errors": [],
  130:             "advisory": True,
  131:         }
  132: 
  133:     # Fail-loud on a genuine config error BEFORE constructing the judge (the judge __init__ raises a
  134:     # RuntimeError for a missing key that is indistinguishable from a family-collision RuntimeError —
  135:     # check the key here so only the real missing-key case maps to NliUnavailableError).
  136:     if not os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip():
  137:         raise NliUnavailableError(
  138:             "OPENROUTER_API_KEY missing — the NLI entailment judge cannot run"
  139:         )
  140: 
  141:     from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import _get_judge
  142: 
  143:     judge = _get_judge()   # family-segregation RuntimeError propagates (NOT masked as unavailable)
  144:     entailed = neutral = contradicted = judge_errors = 0
  145:     disputed: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
  146:     errors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
  147:     for pair in pairs:
  148:         # SYNC call (no asyncio.to_thread): keeps _RUN_COST_CTX in-context so judge spend counts against
  149:         # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN and BudgetExceededError propagates. Runs post-generation (last step), so
  150:         # blocking the loop for N sequential entailment calls is fine.
  151:         verdict, reason = judge.judge(pair["sentence"], pair["span"])
  152:         # I-cap-005 (#1068): the judge FAILS OPEN to ("ENTAILED", "judge_error: ...") on an API/parse
  153:         # error to keep the run alive. Counting that as a genuine ENTAILED would silently report a
  154:         # DEGRADED judge as "NLI clean" — a silent downgrade (LAW II / operator no-downgrade directive).
  155:         # Detect it, count it as an ERROR (NOT entailed), and surface it loudly in the annotation.
  156:         if isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
  157:             judge_errors += 1
  158:             errors.append({
  159:                 "section": pair.get("section", ""),
  160:                 "evidence_id": pair.get("evidence_id", ""),
  161:                 "reason": reason,
  162:                 "sentence": pair["sentence"],
  163:             })
  164:             continue
  165:         if verdict == "ENTAILED":
  166:             entailed += 1
  167:             continue
  168:         if verdict == "CONTRADICTED":
  169:             contradicted += 1
  170:         else:
  171:             verdict = "NEUTRAL"   # normalize any unexpected verdict to NEUTRAL (still disputed)
  172:             neutral += 1
  173:         disputed.append({
  174:             "section": pair.get("section", ""),
  175:             "evidence_id": pair.get("evidence_id", ""),
  176:             "verdict": verdict,
  177:             "reason": reason,
  178:             "sentence": pair["sentence"],
  179:         })
  180:     scored = len(pairs) - judge_errors
  181:     # If EVERY call errored, nothing was actually entailment-checked — surface nli_status:"error" LOUDLY
  182:     # (not "ok" with a misleading zero-dispute count). Partial errors keep "ok" but expose the count so
  183:     # an audit sees the degradation. Errored sentences are excluded from entailed/neutral/contradicted.
  184:     nli_status = "error" if (pairs and scored == 0) else "ok"
  185:     return {
  186:         "nli_status": nli_status,
  187:         "judge": "llm_entailment",
  188:         "model": judge._model,
  189:         "sentences_checked": len(pairs),
  190:         "sentences_scored": scored,
  191:         "entailed_count": entailed,
  192:         "neutral_count": neutral,
  193:         "contradicted_count": contradicted,
  194:         "disputed_count": len(disputed),
  195:         "disputed": disputed,
  196:         "judge_error_count": judge_errors,
  197:         "judge_errors": errors,
  198:         "advisory": True,
  199:     }

 succeeded in 1228ms:

  191:     def judge(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
  192:         """Return (verdict, reason).
  193: 
  194:         verdict is one of "ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED".
  195: 
  196:         I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 4: a transient transport/parse/empty-choices/bad-verdict
  197:         fault is now RETRIED on the same provider up to `PG_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES` extra times
  198:         (default 2 => 3 total attempts; env-driven per LAW VI) before the method emits the
  199:         ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...') sentinel on exhaustion. This is NOT a fail-OPEN: both
  200:         consumers FAIL CLOSED on that sentinel — `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:295-301`
  201:         keys on the `judge_error:` PREFIX alone (drops in enforce); `provenance_generator.py:1795`
  202:         sets `judge_error_flag` on `verdict=='ENTAILED' AND reason.startswith('judge_error:')`
  203:         (consumed -> drop). The sentinel stays EXACTLY ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...') so that
  204:         detection is preserved (do NOT flip to NEUTRAL standalone — that bypasses the
  205:         provenance detection at :1795). The retry's only effect is to stop a SINGLE transient
  206:         judge fault from OVER-DROPPING a salvageable verdict (the drb_72-class coverage collapse),
  207:         without loosening the binding faithfulness gate.
  208: 
  209:         I-bug-100: after each successful httpx call this method records
  210:         the call cost via openrouter_client's module-level helpers
  211:         (`_add_run_cost`, `check_run_budget`, `_COST_LEDGER_PATH`) so
  212:         judge spend is visible to the per-run budget cap and the cost
  213:         ledger. `BudgetExceededError` is explicitly re-raised before
  214:         the broad retry/fail-closed handler so a cap breach aborts the
  215:         sweep cleanly instead of being masked as a transient judge error
  216:         or being retried.
  217:         """
  218:         prompt = _select_entailment_prompt().format(span=span, sentence=sentence)
  219:         started = time.monotonic()
  220:         # I-bug-946 (#932): when Path-B gate is active, force singleton provider routing in
  221:         # the request body to match the resolved-at-preflight per-role provider. Without this,
  222:         # this direct httpx path bypasses the gate's routing intent (the OpenRouterClient path
  223:         # also got the override via openrouter_client.py:1400-1410). Codex iter-2 P1#2.
  224:         # Codex iter-1 diff P1#2: this lookup MUST use explicit role="evaluator", NOT the
  225:         # ambient _ROLE contextvar. The entailment judge fires during section generation

  301:                     headers={
  302:                         "Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}",

  304:                     },
  305:                     json=json_body,
  306:                 )
  307:                 response.raise_for_status()
  308:                 data = response.json()
  309: 
  310:                 # I-safety-002b (#925): Path-B gate capture. The entailment judge is the
  311:                 # evaluator-family LLM call that bypasses OpenRouterClient (direct httpx),
  312:                 # so without capturing here the gate's two-family completeness check would
  313:                 # be a silent no-op. Best-effort + gate-flagged; lazy import keeps off-mode
  314:                 # import cost zero. `data` is the genuinely-served non-stream JSON.

  317:                     if _pathb.is_active():

  321:                             raw_response=data,
  322:                         )
  323:                 except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — capture must never break the judge
  324:                     pass
  325: 
  326:                 # I-bug-100: cost recording. Reads + records BEFORE verdict
  327:                 # parse so a cap breach aborts the sweep regardless of
  328:                 # downstream parse outcome. Each real POST (incl. a retried one) bills its OWN
  329:                 # tokens — correct, real money was spent — so this runs inside the loop per attempt.
  330:                 usage = data.get("usage", {}) or {}
  331:                 input_tokens = int(usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
  332:                 output_tokens = int(usage.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
  333:                 api_cost = float(usage.get("cost", 0) or 0)
  334:                 actual_cost = api_cost or _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
  335:                     self._model, input_tokens, output_tokens, 0,
  336:                 )
  337:                 # I-bug-100 iter-1 diff P2 fix: when the entire usage block
  338:                 # is absent, both api_cost and the imputed value are 0, which
  339:                 # silently bypasses the budget guard. Fall back to a
  340:                 # conservative estimate based on typical judge-call shape
  341:                 # (~500 prompt + ~100 completion tokens) priced at Opus-tier
  342:                 # so the budget cap is preserved on degraded responses.
  343:                 if actual_cost == 0 and not usage:
  344:                     actual_cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
  345:                         self._model, 500, 100, 0,
  346:                     )
  347:                 _orc._add_run_cost(actual_cost)
  348:                 duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - started) * 1000.0
  349:                 try:
  350:                     _append_judge_ledger_entry(
  351:                         input_tokens=input_tokens,
  352:                         output_tokens=output_tokens,
  353:                         duration_ms=duration_ms,
  354:                         actual_cost=actual_cost,
  355:                     )
  356:                 except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger IO is non-critical
  357:                     logger.warning("entailment ledger write failed: %s", exc)

  363:                 reason = str(parsed.get("reason", ""))

  365:                     # Bad verdict = a recoverable failed verification: raise the RETRYABLE error so a

  379:                     logger.warning(
  380:                         "entailment judge bad verdict (attempt %d/%d): %s — retrying.",

  437: _JUDGE_SINGLETON: _EntailmentJudge | None = None
  438: 
  439: 
  440: def _get_judge() -> _EntailmentJudge:
  441:     """Lazy singleton so off-mode pays zero import/connection cost."""
  442:     global _JUDGE_SINGLETON
  443:     if _JUDGE_SINGLETON is None:
  444:         _JUDGE_SINGLETON = _EntailmentJudge()
  445:     return _JUDGE_SINGLETON
  446: 
  447: 
  448: # I-bug-096: process-lifetime telemetry counters for the entailment gate.
  449: # In enforce mode the judge fail-open path returns ("ENTAILED",
  450: # "judge_error: ...") on transient API errors. A persistent OpenRouter
  451: # outage or model-format change could make the 6th check silently inert
  452: # — every sentence falls through as ENTAILED while WARNING lines
  453: # accumulate. These counters give an operator a concise "the gate ran N
  454: # times, M of those errored" signal.
  455: #
  456: # Counters are gate-side (not judge-side) per the original I-bug-092
  457: # brief verdict: gate behavior is what we want to measure, not a
  458: # specific judge implementation. Tests with FakeJudge tick these too,
  459: # and a future swapped judge cannot bypass the judge_error counter.
  460: _JUDGE_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
  461:     "calls": 0,
  462:     "entailed": 0,
  463:     "neutral": 0,
  464:     "contradicted": 0,
  465:     "judge_error": 0,
  466: }
  467: 
  468: # FX-09 (I-ready-017): per-RUN, concurrency-isolated judge counters. The process-
  469: # lifetime _JUDGE_TELEMETRY above is shared across threads, so a per-run
  470: # judge_error_rate computed from its delta is contaminated when two runs share one
  471: # process (the v6 Dramatiq worker runs `asyncio.run(run_one_query(...))` under
  472: # `--threads 2`). A ContextVar is isolated per OS-thread AND per asyncio Task, so each
  473: # run that calls begin_run_judge_telemetry() gets its OWN {calls, judge_error}
  474: # counter. The global counter (health endpoint) is unaffected.
  475: import contextvars  # noqa: E402  (kept next to the counters it scopes)
  476: 
  477: _RUN_JUDGE_TELEMETRY: contextvars.ContextVar[dict[str, int] | None] = (
  478:     contextvars.ContextVar("_run_judge_telemetry", default=None)
  479: )
  480: 
  481: 
  482: def begin_run_judge_telemetry() -> dict[str, int]:
  483:     """Start a per-run, concurrency-isolated judge counter and return it.
  484: 
  485:     Bind a fresh {calls, judge_error} dict to the contextvar for THIS run. The judge
  486:     ticks both the process-lifetime counter and (if set) this per-run dict. Read the
  487:     returned dict at the end of the run for an isolated denominator — never
  488:     contaminated by a sibling run sharing the process (FX-09 #1114). Sequential calls
  489:     in one context each rebind to a fresh dict, so no reset is required for
  490:     correctness; the previous run's dict is simply replaced.
  491:     """
  492:     tel = {"calls": 0, "judge_error": 0}
  493:     _RUN_JUDGE_TELEMETRY.set(tel)
  494:     return tel
  495: 
  496: 
  497: def get_judge_telemetry() -> dict[str, int]:
  498:     """Snapshot of process-lifetime entailment-judge counters.
  499: 
  500:     Read once before a job to compute deltas if needed. Operators
  501:     concerned that the gate has gone silently inert can poll this from
  502:     a health endpoint or scripts/observability tooling and alert on
  503:     judge_error rate.
  504:     """
  505:     return dict(_JUDGE_TELEMETRY)
  506: 
  507: 
  508: def reset_judge_telemetry() -> None:
  509:     """Zero all judge telemetry counters in-place.
  510:

src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:1661:12b. **Claim-frame hard constraint (M-38, eliminating under-framed study mentions)**: Rule #12 is asymmetric and STRICT — when you name a specific study, trial, cohort, or experiment by its short name (phase-N trial identifier in clinical; long-run battery cycling test in materials; named longitudinal cohort in epidemiology; named regulatory docket in policy), that sentence — or the IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING sentence in the same paragraph — MUST carry at LEAST THREE frame elements drawn from: sample size / cohort N; baseline value; comparator / control arm; specific dose or intervention level; primary endpoint; timepoint; effect size WITH uncertainty (CI, SD, or p-value). If you cannot produce three of those elements from the cited evidence, DO NOT name the study by its short name — phrase the sentence generically as "one randomized trial showed ... [ev_X]" or "a prospective cohort in the target population reported ... [ev_X]" or "one pooled analysis found ... [ev_X]" or "a long-run cycling test reported ... [ev_X]" instead. This hard floor prevents the failure mode where a sentence names a specific study but gives only a single effect-size number without N, baseline, or comparator — producing a news-style summary mis-labelled with a primary-study name. Concrete templates (use placeholders): GOOD: "In [STUDY NAME] (N=[SAMPLE_SIZE], baseline [PRIMARY_METRIC]=[BASELINE_VALUE]), [INTERVENTION_ARM] reduced [ENDPOINT] by [EFFECT_SIZE] versus [COMPARATOR_ARM] at [TIMEPOINT] [ev_X]." GOOD (generic when frame is unavailable): "A pre-planned pooled analysis of two phase-3 trials reported [ENDPOINT]=[VALUE] at [TIMEPOINT] [ev_X]." — no short-name attribution because pooled data lack per-trial N. BAD (under-framed, must be rewritten): "[STUDY NAME] showed that [INTERVENTION] reduced [ENDPOINT] more than [COMPARATOR] [ev_X]." — names the study with only one frame element (effect direction); rewrite as "A head-to-head trial of [INTERVENTION] versus [COMPARATOR] reported greater [ENDPOINT] reduction with [INTERVENTION] [ev_X]" which drops the study name because the frame is too thin. BAD (under-framed, must be rewritten): "[STUDY NAME] found median time to [THRESHOLD] was [TIMEPOINT] [ev_X]." — names study with only endpoint + effect; rewrite as "One pooled analysis of two phase-3 trials found median time to [THRESHOLD] was [TIMEPOINT] [ev_X]" ONLY if the cited evidence confirms pooled data across two trials. This rule is what converts a LOSE_BOTH on Claim frames into a competitive synthesis.

 succeeded in 1048ms:
    1: """Strict verifier per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3.
    2: 
    3: Per-sentence check enforcing:
    4:   (a) at least one provenance token present
    5:   (b) every token references an evidence_id in the pool
    6:   (c) span bounds are valid (0 <= start <= end <= len(text))
    7:   (d) every decimal in the sentence appears in the cited spans
    8:   (e) sentence and span share >= MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP content words
    9:   (f) I-bug-092: span semantically ENTAILS the sentence's specific claims
   10:       (gated behind PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, defaults off)
   11: 
   12: Checks (a)-(e) are mechanical "provenance hygiene" — they bound a claim to
   13: the same TOPIC as its span. Check (f) is "provenance correctness" — it
   14: verifies the span actually entails the sentence's specific facts. The
   15: audit on 2026-05-09 found that a capable generator can pass (a)-(e) by
   16: producing topically-overlapping prose that introduces unsourced specifics
   17: (e.g. inserting "pancreatic β-cells", "lipid metabolism", "energy storage"
   18: into a span that says only "synergistic actions on insulin secretion,
   19: glucagon suppression, appetite regulation, and adipocyte metabolism").
   20: 
   21: Returns a (verifier_pass: bool, drop_reason: str | None) pair, or directly
   22: constructs a VerifiedSentence for use by the generator orchestrator.
   23: 
   24: Tunables (read at call time so tests can override):
   25:   PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP — minimum shared content words (default 2)
   26:   PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT       — "off" | "warn" | "enforce" (default)
   27:                                        off:     skip check (f), pre-I-bug-092 behavior
   28:                                                 — operator override; production runs
   29:                                                   ship without provenance-correctness
   30:                                                   enforcement
   31:                                        warn:    run check (f), log violations,
   32:                                                 do NOT drop (collect telemetry)
   33:                                        enforce: run check (f), drop on
   34:                                                 NEUTRAL or CONTRADICTED verdict
   35:                                                 (default per I-bug-095, validated
   36:                                                 empirically by I-bug-094 live test)
   37:   PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL               — entailment judge model
   38:                                        (default: google/gemma-4-31b-it,

   65: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   66: # Tunables

   68: 
   69: DEFAULT_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP = 2
   70: 
   71: # Minimal English stopword list. Strict-verify's content-word overlap should
   72: # count substantive vocabulary, not function words. This is intentionally
   73: # small — the rule is conservative ("at least 2 shared content words")
   74: # so even a short stoplist yields the right behavior.
   75: _STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
   76:     "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "if", "of", "in", "on", "at",
   77:     "to", "for", "with", "as", "by", "from", "into", "through", "during",
   78:     "before", "after", "above", "below", "between", "is", "are", "was",
   79:     "were", "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had", "having", "do",
   80:     "does", "did", "doing", "will", "would", "should", "could", "may",
   81:     "might", "must", "can", "this", "that", "these", "those", "it",
   82:     "its", "their", "there", "they", "them", "we", "us", "our", "you",
   83:     "your", "i", "me", "my", "he", "she", "his", "her",
   84: })
   85: 
   86: _DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
   87: _WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_\-]+")
   88: 
   89: 
   90: def _min_overlap_threshold() -> int:
   91:     raw = os.environ.get("PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP", "")
   92:     try:
   93:         return max(0, int(raw))
   94:     except ValueError:
   95:         return DEFAULT_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP
   96: 
   97: 
   98: def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
   99:     """Lowercase content words (>=3 chars, not stopwords) from `text`."""
  100:     return {
  101:         m.group(0).lower()
  102:         for m in _WORD_RE.finditer(text)
  103:         if len(m.group(0)) >= 3 and m.group(0).lower() not in _STOPWORDS
  104:     }
  105: 
  106: 
  107: def _decimals(text: str) -> set[str]:
  108:     """Numeric tokens (integers + decimals) from `text`."""
  109:     return {m.group(0) for m in _DECIMAL_RE.finditer(text)}
  110: 
  111: 
  112: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  113: # Entailment judge (I-bug-092 — provenance correctness)
  114: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  115: #
  116: # Definitions extracted to `polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge` per
  117: # I-bug-099 so future call sites have a single canonical home for the
  118: # judge class + lazy singleton + telemetry counters. Re-exported here
  119: # for backwards compatibility with the existing test pattern that
  120: # monkey-patches `strict_verify._get_judge`, `strict_verify._JUDGE_TELEMETRY`,
  121: # etc. via `monkeypatch.setattr(strict_verify, ...)`. Both `verify_sentence`
  122: # (in this module) and `provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance`
  123: # (which lazy-imports `_get_judge` from this module) read the re-exported
  124: # names — a single rebind on `strict_verify` propagates to both.
  125: 
  126: from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import (  # noqa: E402  -- re-export
  127:     _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL,
  128:     _ENTAILMENT_PROMPT,
