  138: # Note: `_JUDGE_SINGLETON` is NOT re-exported here. The resolver state
  139: # lives in `polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge` and is updated via
  140: # that module's `global _JUDGE_SINGLETON` declaration in `_get_judge()`.
  141: # Tests that do `monkeypatch.setattr(strict_verify, "_JUDGE_SINGLETON",
  142: # fake, raising=False)` create a vestigial attribute that the resolver
  143: # never reads — but the same tests also patch `_get_judge`, which IS
  144: # re-exported and DOES propagate (provenance_generator lazy-imports
  145: # `_get_judge` from this module at call time, so it picks up the patched
  146: # value). Test patch surface is preserved by the `_get_judge` rebind;
  147: # the `_JUDGE_SINGLETON` rebind is incidental.
  148: 
  149: 
  150: # I-bug-097: dedup unknown-mode warnings so a long-running process logs
  151: # at most ONE WARNING per typo string. Operators sometimes set
  152: # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=ENFORCE (already lowercased fine) or
  153: # =enforced (verb form, falls through to off). Without a warning, the
  154: # typo silently disables the gate.
  155: #
  156: # KEPT here (not moved to entailment_judge.py per I-bug-099 iter-2
  157: # brief): tests rebind via `monkeypatch.setattr(strict_verify,
  158: # "_UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED", set(), raising=False)` and `_entailment_mode()`
  159: # reads it from this module's globals — preserving the rebind contract.
  160: _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED: set[str] = set()
  161: 
  162: 
  163: # I-bug-095: production default flipped from "off" to "enforce" per
  164: # Codex APPROVE iter 1. Empirical evidence: I-bug-094 live test ran
  165: # 4/4 audit-derived cases (M2/C2/C1 + positive control) against real
  166: # OpenRouter Gemma 4 31B and got correct verdicts. The cage is
  167: # structurally complete with I-bug-092..097 + I-cj-008; flipping the
  168: # default closes the loop on the 2026-05-09 audit-revealed gap.
  169: #
  170: # Operator escape hatch: `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off` continues
  171: # to disable the check explicitly. Documented per Codex iter-1 brief
  172: # verdict.
  173: _DEFAULT_MODE = "enforce"
  174: 
  175: 
  176: def _entailment_mode() -> str:
  177:     """Return one of 'off', 'warn', 'enforce'. Unknown values map to default.
  178: 
  179:     I-bug-097: emit a single WARNING per process per unrecognized typo
  180:     string. Empty / unset env returns the default mode silently —
  181:     that is the intended default, not a misconfiguration.
  182: 
  183:     I-bug-095: default mode is 'enforce' (was 'off' before this Issue).
  184:     Operators who need the pre-graduation behavior can set
  185:     PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off explicitly.
  186:     """
  187:     raw = os.environ.get("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT", _DEFAULT_MODE).lower().strip()
  188:     if raw and raw not in ("off", "warn", "enforce"):
  189:         if raw not in _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED:
  190:             _UNKNOWN_MODE_WARNED.add(raw)
  191:             logger.warning(
  192:                 "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=%r unrecognized; "
  193:                 "treating as %r (default). Valid: off, warn, enforce.",
  194:                 raw, _DEFAULT_MODE,
  195:             )
  196:         return _DEFAULT_MODE
  197:     return raw or _DEFAULT_MODE
  198: 
  199: 
  200: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  201: # Per-sentence verifier
  202: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  203: 
  204: def verify_sentence(
  205:     sentence_text: str,
  206:     pool: EvidencePool,
  207:     min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
  208:     is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
  209: ) -> tuple[bool, DropReason | None]:
  210:     """Return (verifier_pass, drop_reason) for `sentence_text` against `pool`.
  211: 
  212:     Pass: returns (True, None).
  213:     Fail: returns (False, drop_reason) with reason from DropReason literal.
  214: 
  215:     Implements CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3 in order:
  216:       1. at least one well-formed token        → no_provenance_token
  217:       2. every token references known source_id → invalid_token
  218:       3. spans within source bounds            → span_out_of_range
  219:       4. every decimal in sentence in spans    → numeric_mismatch
  220:       5. >=N shared content words              → overlap_too_low
  221:       6. (I-bug-092) span entails sentence     → entailment_failed
  222:          (gated by PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, off by default)
  223: 
  224:     If `is_synthesis_claim=True` AND the sentence has no tokens, return
  225:     (True, None) — synthesis claims pass without provenance by definition
  226:     (I-f5-006). If a synthesis claim DOES carry tokens, that's a generator
  227:     bug; we still run token checks so the underlying invariant holds.
  228:     """
  229:     threshold = (
  230:         min_content_overlap
  231:         if min_content_overlap is not None
  232:         else _min_overlap_threshold()
  233:     )
  234: 
  235:     tokens = extract_tokens(sentence_text)
  236:     if not tokens:
  237:         if is_synthesis_claim:
  238:             return True, None
  239:         return False, "no_provenance_token"
  240: 
  241:     # Validate each token against the pool. Collect span texts for
  242:     # later numeric + overlap checks.
  243:     span_texts: list[str] = []
  244:     for token in tokens:
  245:         reason = validate_token_against_pool(token, pool)
  246:         if reason == "invalid_token":
  247:             return False, "invalid_token"
  248:         if reason == "span_out_of_range":
  249:             return False, "span_out_of_range"
  250:         # Token valid — fetch its span text
  251:         span = get_span_text(token, pool)
  252:         if span is None:
  253:             # Belt-and-suspenders: token validated but get_span_text said no.
  254:             return False, "span_out_of_range"
  255:         span_texts.append(span)
  256: 
  257:     sentence_clean = strip_tokens(sentence_text)
  258:     combined_span = " ".join(span_texts)
  259: 
  260:     # Decimal match: every decimal in sentence must appear in the combined
  261:     # span text. Permits the spans to contain MORE decimals than the
  262:     # sentence cites — that's fine; the constraint is one-way.
  263:     sentence_decimals = _decimals(sentence_clean)
  264:     span_decimals = _decimals(combined_span)
  265:     if not sentence_decimals.issubset(span_decimals):
  266:         return False, "numeric_mismatch"
  267: 
  268:     # Content-word overlap
  269:     sentence_words = _content_words(sentence_clean)
  270:     span_words = _content_words(combined_span)
  271:     # BUG-03 (FX-02, #1106): a truly contentless sentence — NO content words AND no decimals —
  272:     # is dropped EXPLICITLY here (mirrors the provenance_generator floor). With the default
  273:     # threshold=2 it would already fail as overlap_too_low, but this fail-closes the
  274:     # PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP=0 edge (where overlap=0 is not < 0) and gives a precise
  275:     # reason. Faithfulness-TIGHTENING: any content word OR decimal routes to the numeric/overlap
  276:     # floors, so a real clinical sentence can never trip this.
  277:     if not sentence_words and not sentence_decimals:
  278:         return False, "empty_or_contentless_sentence"
  279:     overlap = len(sentence_words & span_words)
  280:     if overlap < threshold:
  281:         return False, "overlap_too_low"
  282: 
  283:     # Check (f) — entailment judge (I-bug-092). Synthesis claims with no
  284:     # tokens already short-circuited above; if a synthesis claim DOES
  285:     # carry tokens it must clear the same content-correctness bar as
  286:     # any other cited sentence.
  287:     mode = _entailment_mode()
  288:     if mode in ("warn", "enforce"):
  289:         verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)
  290:         _record_judge_outcome(verdict, reason)
  291:         # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: the judge FAILS OPEN to ("ENTAILED","judge_error:...") on an API/parse
  292:         # error. Counting that as a genuine ENTAILED ships an UNVERIFIED clinical claim as "verified" — a
  293:         # silent downgrade of the binding faithfulness gate (LAW II / lethal-in-clinical). Detect it and
  294:         # FAIL CLOSED in enforce mode (drop the sentence, like an unsupported one); warn-mode logs only.
  295:         if isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
  296:             logger.warning(
  297:                 "entailment judge_error (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r — failing closed",
  298:                 mode, sentence_clean, reason,
  299:             )
  300:             if mode == "enforce":
  301:                 return False, "entailment_judge_error_fail_closed"
  302:         if verdict in ("NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
  303:             logger.warning(
  304:                 "entailment %s (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r",
  305:                 verdict, mode, sentence_clean, reason,
  306:             )
  307:             if mode == "enforce":
  308:                 return False, "entailment_failed"
  309: 
  310:     return True, None
  311: 
  312: 
  313: def verify_sentence_to_record(
  314:     sentence_text: str,
  315:     section_id: str,
  316:     pool: EvidencePool,
  317:     min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
  318:     is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
  319: ) -> VerifiedSentence:
  320:     """Convenience: wrap verify_sentence into a VerifiedSentence record.
  321: 
  322:     Used by the generator orchestrator to build Section.verified_sentences.
  323:     """
  324:     passed, reason = verify_sentence(
  325:         sentence_text,
  326:         pool,
  327:         min_content_overlap=min_content_overlap,
  328:         is_synthesis_claim=is_synthesis_claim,
  329:     )
  330:     tokens = [t.raw for t in extract_tokens(sentence_text)]
  331:     return VerifiedSentence(
  332:         section_id=section_id,
  333:         sentence_text=sentence_text,
  334:         provenance_tokens=tokens,
  335:         verifier_pass=passed,
  336:         drop_reason=reason,
  337:         evaluator_agrees=passed,
  338:         is_synthesis_claim=is_synthesis_claim,
  339:     )
  340: 
  341: 
  342: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  343: # Section-level rollup
  344: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  345: 
  346: def section_pass_rate(sentences: list[VerifiedSentence]) -> float:
  347:     """Fraction of sentences that passed verify, in [0.0, 1.0].
  348: 
  349:     Empty list returns 0.0 (vacuously below any threshold).
  350:     """
  351:     if not sentences:
  352:         return 0.0
  353:     passed = sum(1 for s in sentences if s.verifier_pass)
  354:     return passed / len(sentences)

 succeeded in 1051ms:
  360: # to be `e`, whereas the canonical token has `#` there. (Verified in tests:
  361: # `_MALFORMED_EV_TOKEN_RE.search("[#ev:y:0-3]")` is None.) So canonicalization
  362: # is idempotent on already-canonical input — OFF == identity holds.
  363: _MALFORMED_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(
  364:     r"\[ev:(?P<ev_id>[A-Za-z0-9_]+):(?P<start>\d+)-(?P<end>\d+)\]"
  365: )
  366: 
  367: # I-pipe-015 (#1240) — a BROADER recognizer for a `[ev:...]` cross-ref ATTEMPT
  368: # that is NOT the clean canonicalizable shape above (e.g. `[ev:abc]` with no
  369: # span, `[ev:abc:12]` with a malformed span). These are recognizable as a
  370: # cross-ref attempt but cannot be rewritten into a valid canonical token, so
  371: # they are COUNTED as dropped-malformed (never silently vanished). The literal
  372: # `[ev:` prefix means this never matches the canonical `[#ev:...]` (which has
  373: # `#` after `[`). Requires `[ev:` followed by at least one non-`]` char. The
  374: # clean canonicalizable shape is a SUBSET of this; the caller subtracts the
  375: # clean count so only the UNFIXABLE attempts are counted as dropped.
  376: _RECOGNIZABLE_EV_ATTEMPT_RE = re.compile(r"\[ev:[^\]]+\]")
  377: 
  378: 
  379: def _token_honest_drop_enabled() -> bool:
  380:     """I-pipe-015 (#1240). True (default) => malformed `[ev:...]` tokens are
  381:     canonicalized to `[#ev:...]` (then run through the SAME full validation) OR
  382:     counted as dropped-malformed in telemetry — never silently lost.
  383: 
  384:     Kill-switch: PG_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_HONEST_DROP=0 reverts to the legacy
  385:     behavior where a malformed `[ev:...]` token is invisible to the parser and
  386:     silently vanishes (byte-identical to pre-#1240). Read at call time so tests
  387:     can toggle without re-import.
  388:     """
  389:     v = os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_TOKEN_HONEST_DROP", "1").strip().lower()
  390:     return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")
  391: 
  392: 
  393: # I-pipe-015 (#1240) — token-honesty telemetry. Module-level counters, read +
  394: # reset by the sweep (mirrors _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY). ONLY mutated on the
  395: # flag-ON path, so OFF-mode never touches them (byte-identity).
  396: _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
  397:     # A malformed `[ev:...]` token that was recognizable and got rewritten to
  398:     # the canonical `[#ev:...]` shape (it STILL runs through the full
  399:     # evidence-id/span/numeric validation — only the bracket format is fixed).
  400:     "malformed_canonicalized": 0,
  401:     # A malformed-but-recognizable token that could NOT be canonicalized into a
  402:     # canonical token (so it would otherwise vanish) — counted, never silent.
  403:     "malformed_dropped": 0,
  404: }
  405: 
  406: 
  407: def get_token_honesty_telemetry() -> dict[str, int]:
  408:     """Snapshot of the malformed-token counters (canonicalized / dropped)."""
  409:     return dict(_TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY)
  410: 
  411: 
  412: def reset_token_honesty_telemetry() -> None:
  413:     """Zero the malformed-token counters (call between runs / tests)."""
  414:     for k in _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY:
  415:         _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY[k] = 0
  416: 
  417: 
  418: def _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(sentence: str) -> str:
  419:     """I-pipe-015 (#1240). Rewrite recognizable malformed `[ev:id:s-e]` tokens
  420:     to the canonical `[#ev:id:s-e]` form so the parser sees them, and bump the
  421:     `malformed_canonicalized` telemetry counter for each rewrite.
  422: 
  423:     IMPORTANT — faithfulness: this ONLY fixes the bracket FORMAT. The resulting
  424:     `[#ev:...]` token is parsed and validated by the SAME full pipeline
  425:     (evidence-id-in-pool, span-bounds, numeric match, >=2 content-word overlap,
  426:     NLI entailment). A token whose evidence id / span is invalid still FAILS
  427:     verification and its sentence is dropped — canonicalization NEVER bypasses
  428:     any validation check. It only prevents the silent loss of an otherwise-valid
  429:     citation. A canonical `[#ev:...]` token already present is untouched.
  430: 
  431:     No-op (returns the input unchanged) when there is no malformed token. The
  432:     caller gates this behind `_token_honest_drop_enabled()`.
  433:     """
  434:     n_clean = len(_MALFORMED_EV_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence))
  435:     # All recognizable `[ev:...]` attempts (clean + unfixable). Anything that is
  436:     # a recognizable attempt but NOT a clean canonicalizable token is
  437:     # unfixable-malformed and is COUNTED as dropped (never silently lost).
  438:     n_attempts = len(_RECOGNIZABLE_EV_ATTEMPT_RE.findall(sentence))
  439:     n_unfixable = max(0, n_attempts - n_clean)
  440:     if n_unfixable:
  441:         _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY["malformed_dropped"] += n_unfixable
  442:     if not n_clean:
  443:         # Nothing to canonicalize. Any unfixable attempt was already counted
  444:         # above; the literal stays in the sentence text (it is NOT a valid
  445:         # token, so the parser ignores it and the sentence verifies/drops on
  446:         # its remaining content exactly as before).
  447:         return sentence
  448:     _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY["malformed_canonicalized"] += n_clean
  449:     return _MALFORMED_EV_TOKEN_RE.sub(
  450:         lambda m: f"[#ev:{m.group('ev_id')}:{m.group('start')}-{m.group('end')}]",
  451:         sentence,
  452:     )
  453: 
  454: # I-meta-005 Phase 7 (#991): Regime C — COMPUTED numbers. A calc token binds a
  455: # rendered number to ONE field of THIS run's quantified model. Grammar:
 1680: 
 1681:     tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
 1682:     failures: list[str] = []
 1683:     # Phase 0b Delta 3 (I-meta-005, gap-#18): tracks whether the entailment
 1684:     # judge failed OPEN ((ENTAILED,"judge_error: ...")). Set in either judge
 1685:     # call below; consumed by the ON-mode fail-closed gate near the return.
 1686:     # HOISTED to function-body top (build agent EDIT-8 scope check): the
 1687:     # entailment block is nested inside `if require_number_match and
 1688:     # valid_token_found:`, so an init at the `if not failures:` site would NOT
 1689:     # be in scope at the return when valid_token_found is False (e.g. all
 1690:     # tokens fail evidence_not_in_pool) -> NameError. Init here so it is always
 1691:     # defined at the return.
 1692:     judge_error_flag = False
 1693: 
 1694:     # I-perm-004 (#1198) slice 3: when the gap-#18 local-window rescue ACCEPTS a sentence whose
 1695:     # narrow bound span did not directly entail, the [#ev] token is RE-POINTED to the rescue window
 1696:     # (the genuinely-entailing span) instead of shipping the original mis-pointed span. Captured here
 1697:     # at function scope as (evidence_id, start, end); applied at the return. None => no re-point.
 1698:     # Gated by PG_SPAN_RESOLVER (default OFF -> byte-identical) and SINGLE-token scope only (the
 1699:     # which-token-to-move combinatorics for multi-token sentences are out of scope, matching
 1700:     # _try_reanchor v1).
 1701:     reanchor_local_to: Optional[tuple[str, int, int]] = None
 1702: 
 1703:     # I-gen-005 Step 3b commit 1: verifier_text strips ALL citation
 1704:     # artifacts (provenance tokens + atom_NNN + bare [ev_XXX]) for ALL
 1705:     # internal verifier checks. The original sentence is preserved
 1706:     # on the returned SentenceVerification.sentence so downstream
 1707:     # (atom_refusal_validator, citation resolver) see atom_NNN tokens.
 1708:     # Per Codex Step 3b iter-2 P1: leaving atom_NNN in numeric or
 1709:     # content-overlap or entailment inputs would falsely drop valid
 1710:     # atom-cited sentences.
 1711:     sentence_for_numbers = _verifier_cleaned_text(sentence)
 1712: 
 1713:     if not tokens:
 1714:         # Sentences without provenance are rejected outright.
 1715:         return SentenceVerification(
 1716:             sentence=sentence,
 1717:             tokens=[],
 1718:             is_verified=False,
 1719:             failure_reasons=["no_provenance_token"],
 1720:         )
 1721: 
 1722:     # Fix-3b: When a sentence has multiple citations (e.g.,
 1723:     # [#ev:a][#ev:b][#ev:c]), the decimals in the sentence may come
 1724:     # from DIFFERENT cited spans — the ICER trial's 86.6% treatment
 1725:     # arm AND the placebo-comparator 47.6% from a different span. The
 1726:     # verifier should require that each decimal in the sentence is
 1727:     # found in SOME cited span (union), not in every span.
 1728:     #
 1729:     # Also: placebo-arm numbers (e.g., "vs 2.4% with placebo") and
 1730:     # achievement thresholds ("≥5%") are filtered from the set of
 1731:     # decimals we require to verify — they're comparator/structural,
 1732:     # not the claim itself.
 1733:     aggregated_span_decimals: set[str] = set()
 1734:     aggregated_span_text: list[str] = []
 1735:     valid_token_found = False
 1736:     for tok in tokens:
 1737:         ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
 1738:         if ev is None:
 1739:             failures.append(f"evidence_not_in_pool:{tok.evidence_id}")
 1740:             continue
 1741:         direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
 1742:         if tok.end > len(direct_quote):
 1743:             failures.append(
 1744:                 f"span_out_of_bounds:{tok.evidence_id}:{tok.end}>{len(direct_quote)}"
 1745:             )
 1746:             continue
 1747:         if tok.start < 0 or tok.start >= tok.end:
 1748:             failures.append(
 1749:                 f"span_invalid:{tok.evidence_id}:{tok.start}-{tok.end}"
 1750:             )
 1751:             continue
 1752:         valid_token_found = True
 1753:         span_text = direct_quote[tok.start:tok.end]
 1754:         span_stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(span_text)
 1755:         aggregated_span_decimals |= _decimals_in(span_stripped)
 1756:         aggregated_span_text.append(span_text)
 1757: 
 1758:     # BUG-03 (FX-02, #1106): the empty/contentless floor runs UNCONDITIONALLY whenever a valid
 1759:     # token exists — NOT nested inside `if require_number_match and valid_token_found:` (Codex
 1760:     # iter-1 P2: nesting it left a token-only sentence verifiable when a caller passes
 1761:     # require_number_match=False). A sentence with NO content words AND no decimals AND no integers
 1762:     # (residue reduces to ".") is a token-only / punctuation-only / all-stopword "sentence" and is
 1763:     # never a valid clinical claim, regardless of number-matching. Computed on the same
 1764:     # dose/placebo/threshold-stripped text the numeric floor uses. Strictly faithfulness-TIGHTENING:
 1765:     # any content word OR number routes to the existing overlap/numeric floors (no false-drop).
 1766:     if valid_token_found:
 1767:         _bug03_stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(sentence_for_numbers)
 1768:         _bug03_stripped = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(" ", _bug03_stripped)
 1769:         _bug03_stripped = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(" ", _bug03_stripped)
 1770:         _bug03_no_content = not _content_words(_bug03_stripped)
 1771:         _bug03_no_numbers = (
 1772:             not _decimals_in(_bug03_stripped) and not _numbers_in(_bug03_stripped)
 1773:         )
 1774:         # Drop a content-word-LESS sentence when EITHER it is fully empty (no numbers either —
 1775:         # token-only/punctuation residue) OR number-matching is disabled. Codex iter-2 P2: under
 1776:         # require_number_match=False the numeric/content/entailment block is SKIPPED, so a
 1777:         # numeric-only fragment like "23.5 [#ev:...]" is never validated against the span — a bare
 1778:         # number with no content words is unverifiable and is not a prose claim, so fail closed.
 1779:         if _bug03_no_content and (_bug03_no_numbers or not require_number_match):
 1780:             failures.append("empty_or_contentless_sentence")
 1781: 
 1782:     if require_number_match and valid_token_found:
 1783:         sentence_stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(sentence_for_numbers)
 1784:         # Strip placebo-comparator phrases (treat their numbers as
 1785:         # structural, not claim). Examples: "vs 2.4% with placebo",
 1786:         # "versus 47.6% placebo", "compared to 5% placebo".
 1787:         sentence_stripped = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(" ", sentence_stripped)
 1788:         # Strip achievement-threshold patterns.
 1789:         sentence_stripped = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(" ", sentence_stripped)
 1790: 
 1791:         sentence_decimals = _decimals_in(sentence_stripped)
 1792:         # I-ready-018 FIX-A3 (#1143): build the span INTEGER aggregate from the CITED span text only
 1793:         # (aggregated_span_decimals was already built from direct_quote[tok.start:tok.end] above).
 1794:         aggregated_span_numbers: set[str] = set()
 1795:         for tok in tokens:
 1796:             ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
 1797:             if ev is None:
 1798:                 continue
 1799:             direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
 1800:             span_text = direct_quote[tok.start:tok.end]
 1801:             aggregated_span_numbers |= _numbers_in(_strip_dose_patterns(span_text))
 1802:         ev_ids = ",".join(sorted({t.evidence_id for t in tokens}))
 1803:         # FIX-A3 ROOT CAUSE: `_numbers_in` (-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?) is a SUPERSET of `_decimals_in`
 1804:         # (-?\d+\.\d+). The prior gate checked decimals OR integers (`if sentence_decimals: ...
 1805:         # else: ...`), so a sentence with an IN-span decimal AND an OUT-of-span standalone integer
 1806:         # (drb_72 03-004: in-span "5.4"/"3.7" + out-of-span "50"/"19" at index 8421) took the decimal
 1807:         # branch and its integers were NEVER checked → it passed VERIFIED. Now BOTH are checked, each
 1808:         # SPAN-SCOPED: every sentence decimal AND every standalone integer must appear in a cited span.
 1809:         # The prior I-gen-005 local-window fallback (which rescued a number found ANYWHERE in the whole
 1810:         # direct_quote, even outside the cited span) is removed: a number genuinely inside a cited span
 1811:         # is already in the span aggregate and so never reaches the missing-set, so the rescue could
 1812:         # only ever pass an out-of-span number — the §-1.1 "number not in the cited span" leak.
 1813:         if sentence_decimals:
 1814:             missing_in_span = sentence_decimals - aggregated_span_decimals
 1815:             if missing_in_span:
 1816:                 failures.append(
 1817:                     f"number_not_in_any_cited_span:{ev_ids}:"
 1818:                     f"missing={sorted(missing_in_span)}"
 1819:                 )
 1820:             # FIX-A3 iter-2 (Codex P1): the decimal is the claim, but a PERCENT-expressed standalone
 1821:             # integer beside it is ALSO a claim (drb_72 03-004 "50% versus 19%"). Check ONLY
 1822:             # %-expressed integers here — NOT every integer — so structural/admin integers (week 68,
 1823:             # STEP 1, 104 weeks, phase 3) are NOT required in the span and a decimal claim sitting
 1824:             # beside a trial/week label is not false-dropped (per the _DECIMAL_NUMBER_RE exemption).
 1825:             claimed_pct_ints = {
 1826:                 m.group(1) for m in _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE.finditer(sentence_stripped)
 1827:             } - sentence_decimals
 1828:             if claimed_pct_ints:
 1829:                 aggregated_span_int_only = aggregated_span_numbers - aggregated_span_decimals
 1830:                 missing_pct_int = claimed_pct_ints - aggregated_span_int_only
 1831:                 if missing_pct_int:
 1832:                     failures.append(
 1833:                         f"no_integer_overlap_any_cited_span:{ev_ids}:"
 1834:                         f"missing={sorted(missing_pct_int)}"
 1835:                     )
 1836:         else:
 1837:             # No decimals: the integers ARE the claim — EVERY standalone integer must appear in a
 1838:             # cited span (I-faith-001 Fix D, unchanged in scope). FIX-A3 only removed the prior
 1839:             # local-window out-of-span rescue (a number in no cited span now fails directly).
 1840:             sentence_numbers = _numbers_in(sentence_stripped)
 1841:             missing_int_in_span = sentence_numbers - aggregated_span_numbers
 1842:             if sentence_numbers and missing_int_in_span:
 1843:                 failures.append(
 1844:                     f"no_integer_overlap_any_cited_span:{ev_ids}:"
 1845:                     f"missing={sorted(missing_int_in_span)}"
 1846:                 )
 1847: 
 1848:         # Codex round 1 B-1: semantic grounding for non-numeric claims.
 1849:         # A sentence like "Semaglutide improved sleep quality [#ev:ev1:0-20]"
 1850:         # used to pass verification because it had no numbers — only the
 1851:         # numeric-mismatch branches ran. Now we ALSO require at least
 1852:         # MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP content words (non-stopword, >=3 chars)
 1853:         # to appear in the aggregated cited-span text. Zero overlap =
 1854:         # unsupported claim, sentence dropped.
 1855:         sentence_content = _content_words(sentence_stripped)
 1856:         span_content = _content_words(" ".join(aggregated_span_text))
 1857:         if sentence_content:
 1858:             overlap = sentence_content & span_content
 1859:             if len(overlap) < MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP:
 1860:                 ev_ids = ",".join(sorted({t.evidence_id for t in tokens}))
 1861:                 # Phase 0b Delta 1 (I-meta-005, gap-#18): the narrow cited
 1862:                 # byte-range may miss content words the FULL cited row supports
 1863:                 # (the gap-#18 grounded-prose drop). Per brief §3.3 + R1: the
 1864:                 # floor-clear only PROPOSES a candidate (a bounded <=400-char
 1865:                 # window in a cited row holding >=MIN content words); the
 1866:                 # entailment BIND happens DOWNSTREAM — the entailment block
 1867:                 # below judges the narrow span, and on NEUTRAL the Delta-2
 1868:                 # bounded-window re-judge accepts iff ENTAILED (else fail-closed).
 1869:                 #
 1870:                 # HARD GATE (Codex diff-gate P1 + architect P1, brief HARD
 1871:                 # CONSTRAINT #5): the floor-clear is gated on the entailment
 1872:                 # judge being in ENFORCE mode — the ONLY mode where the
 1873:                 # downstream NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED bind actually DROPS. Under
 1874:                 # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off the judge never runs, and under
 1875:                 # =warn the judge runs but NEVER drops (log-only). In both, a
 1876:                 # content-words-only floor-clear would be the SOLE gate —
 1877:                 # laundering a drop into a pass with no enforced bind. So Delta 1
 1878:                 # proposes ONLY when entailment is enforce; otherwise the pre-0b
 1879:                 # content-floor drop stays (fail-closed). off = no rescue
 1880:                 # (byte-identical). shadow = log would-propose, still fail
 1881:                 # (output + spend neutral, NO judge call). enforce-verification +
 1882:                 # enforce-entailment + window = clear, deferring to the bind.
 1883:                 _vmode_c = _verification_mode()
 1884:                 _rescued_c = False
 1885:                 # I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P1: when allow_local_window_fallback
 1886:                 # is False (the re-anchor accept gate), this full-row content-floor
 1887:                 # rescue is DISABLED — the bound span's OWN narrow content overlap
 1888:                 # must clear the floor, so a different in-row window can never
 1889:                 # rescue a non-supporting candidate span. Default True preserves
 1890:                 # the Phase 0b grounded-prose rescue byte-for-byte.
 1891:                 if allow_local_window_fallback and _vmode_c in ("shadow", "enforce"):
 1892:                     from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import (  # noqa: PLC0415
 1893:                         _entailment_mode as _emode_c,
 1894:                     )
 1895:                     if _emode_c() == "enforce":
 1896:                         for tok in tokens:
 1897:                             ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
 1898:                             if ev is None:
 1899:                                 continue
 1900:                             dq_c = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
 1901:                             if _find_local_content_window(
 1902:                                 sentence_content, dq_c, window=400,
 1903:                                 min_content_overlap=MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP,
 1904:                             ):
 1905:                                 _rescued_c = True
 1906:                                 logger.warning(
 1907:                                     "[provenance] %s content_floor_full_row "

 1909:                                     "bounded full-row window exists, deferring "
 1910:                                     "to the downstream entailment bind",
 1911:                                     "ENFORCE_propose" if _vmode_c == "enforce"
 1912:                                     else "SHADOW_would_propose",
 1913:                                     tok.evidence_id,
 1914:                                 )
 1915:                                 break
 1916:                 if not (_vmode_c == "enforce" and _rescued_c):
 1917:                     failures.append(
 1918:                         f"no_content_word_overlap_any_cited_span:{ev_ids}:"
 1919:                         f"sentence_words={sorted(sentence_content)[:5]}"
 1920:                     )
 1921: 
 1922:         # M-25a: trial-name match. If the sentence names a specific
 1923:         # trial (SURPASS-N, SURMOUNT-N, SELECT, LEADER, etc.), at least
 1924:         # one cited evidence row must mention that trial. Prevents the
 1925:         # DR pass-4 FABRICATED-#20 defect (SURMOUNT-1 claim bound to
 1926:         # SURMOUNT-3 paper).
 1927:         sentence_trials = extract_trial_names(sentence_for_numbers)
 1928:         if sentence_trials:
 1929:             # I-meta-002-q1d (#949): resolve each cited row ROW-LOCALLY — title/statement authority, else
 1930:             # the CITED SPANS for THAT row only (never whole direct_quote, never cross-row spans).
 1931:             spans_by_ev: dict[str, list[tuple[int, int]]] = {}
 1932:             for tok in tokens:
 1933:                 spans_by_ev.setdefault(tok.evidence_id, []).append((tok.start, tok.end))
 1934:             evidence_trials: set[str] = set()
 1935:             for ev_id, cited_spans in spans_by_ev.items():

 2211: def split_into_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
 2212:     """Lightweight sentence splitter. Good enough for our generator output.
 2213: 
 2214:     Handles trailing citation markers like `.[1]`, `.[#ev:ev_a:0-5]`,
 2215:     `.[1][2]` — the second alternative in the split regex triggers on
 2216:     the closing `]` so the marker stays attached to the preceding
 2217:     sentence rather than being eaten.
 2218:     """
 2219:     if not text:
 2220:         return []
 2221:     parts = _SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE.split(text.strip())
 2222:     return [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]

 2229:     total_in: int
 2230:     total_kept: int
 2231:     total_dropped: int

 2233: 
 2234: def split_findings_and_limitations(text: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
 2235:     """Split a draft into (findings_text, limitations_text).
 2236: 
 2237:     Gap-3: the generator now writes a "Limitations:" paragraph that
 2238:     discusses pipeline telemetry (tier mix, contradictions) without
 2239:     per-sentence [ev_XXX] markers. Verification should be relaxed for
 2240:     that paragraph — no citation tokens are required.
 2241: 
 2242:     Returns ("findings text", "limitations text"). If no Limitations
 2243:     block is found, returns (full_text, "").
 2244:     """
 2245:     if not text:
 2246:         return "", ""
 2247:     # Find the literal word "Limitations:" at start of a line or after
 2248:     # whitespace. Case-insensitive, allow markdown heading variants.
 2249:     m = re.search(
 2250:         r"(^|\n\s*)(?:#{0,3}\s*)?(?:\*\*)?Limitations(?:\*\*)?\s*:",
 2251:         text, re.IGNORECASE,
 2252:     )
 2253:     if not m:
 2254:         return text.strip(), ""
 2255:     findings = text[:m.start()].strip()
 2256:     limitations = text[m.start():].strip()
 2257:     return findings, limitations

 2259: 
 2260: def verify_limitations_sentence_against_telemetry(
 2261:     sentence: str,
 2262:     telemetry_block: str,
 2263: ) -> SentenceVerification:
 2264:     """BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): check that every decimal / integer
 2265:     quoted in a Limitations sentence appears verbatim in the telemetry
 2266:     block. Sentences that make unsupported numeric claims about the
 2267:     pipeline's own corpus are dropped, not passed through.
 2268: 
 2269:     Returns SentenceVerification with is_verified=True/False.
 2270:     """
 2271:     import re as _re
 2272:     tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
 2273:     # Extract all decimal-or-integer numeric literals from the sentence.
 2274:     # Allow percentages; strip '%'.
 2275:     sentence_nums = _re.findall(
 2276:         r"(?<![A-Za-z\d])-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?(?:%)?", sentence,
 2277:     )
 2278:     # Only include numbers that are NOT trivial (e.g., "1" in "table 1"
 2279:     # isn't a claim; 3+ digit or decimal is more likely a real number).
 2280:     substantive = [n for n in sentence_nums if
 2281:                    len(n.replace("-", "").replace("%", "").replace(".", "")) >= 2
 2282:                    or "." in n or "%" in n]
 2283: 
 2284:     failures: list[str] = []
 2285:     if not telemetry_block:
 2286:         # No telemetry to check against; treat as pass-through (backward
 2287:         # compat when caller doesn't pass the block).
 2288:         return SentenceVerification(
 2289:             sentence=sentence, tokens=tokens,
 2290:             is_verified=True, failure_reasons=[],
 2291:             soft_warnings=["limitations_pass_through_no_telemetry"],
 2292:         )
 2293:     # BUG-M-209 fix (pass 2 remediation): the telemetry block is a
 2294:     # whitelist of known metric KEYS. A numeric literal in a Limitations
 2295:     # sentence must appear close to a known metric keyword, not just
 2296:     # anywhere in telemetry. "T-cell count 500" should NOT pass when
 2297:     # telemetry says "http_status: 500".
 2298:     #
 2299:     # Known telemetry metric anchors, case-insensitive:
 2300:     _TELEMETRY_METRIC_KEYS = (
 2301:         "tier_distribution",
 2302:         "t1", "t2", "t3", "t4", "t5", "t6", "t7",
 2303:         "unknown",  # UNKNOWN tier
 2304:         "contradictions_detected", "contradictions",
 2305:         "rel_diff", "relative_difference",
 2306:         "severity",
 2307:         "date_range",
 2308:         "completeness_gaps", "uncovered", "uncovered_topic",
 2309:     )
 2310:     telemetry_lower = telemetry_block.lower()
 2311:     telemetry_lines = telemetry_lower.split("\n")
 2312: 
 2313:     def _line_mentions_metric(line: str) -> bool:
 2314:         for key in _TELEMETRY_METRIC_KEYS:
 2315:             if key in line:

 succeeded in 1087ms:
 2350:     )
 2351: 
 2352: 
 2353: # I-pipe-016 (#1241) — content-empty sentence honesty.
 2354: #
 2355: # WHY: the lightweight splitter (split_into_sentences) can emit an ORPHANED
 2356: # citation-only fragment as its own "sentence" (e.g. a bare `[#ev:a:0-5]` left
 2357: # over from a rewrite, or `.[3]` punctuation residue). In the Limitations
 2358: # PASS-THROUGH branch these fragments were counted as is_verified=True and
 2359: # pulled into BOTH the kept total (numerator) AND total_in (denominator) —
 2360: # inflating the verified-sentence telemetry with content-empty noise. (The
 2361: # findings branch already drops them via BUG-03 / no_provenance_token, but the
 2362: # pass-through branch did not.)
 2363: #
 2364: # FIX (default-ON, PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY=0 reverts): a content-empty
 2365: # "sentence" — no content words AND no numeric values after stripping citation
 2366: # artifacts, dose/placebo/threshold residue — is EXCLUDED from the verified
 2367: # numerator AND from total_in (denominator). It is neither kept nor dropped; it
 2368: # simply does not count. This changes ONLY the counting of empty noise; it does
 2369: # NOT alter which REAL sentences pass strict_verify (a real sentence has at
 2370: # least one content word or number and is never content-empty).
 2371: def _skip_empty_enabled() -> bool:
 2372:     """I-pipe-016 (#1241). True (default) => content-empty sentences are
 2373:     excluded from the verified numerator AND total_in denominator. Kill-switch:
 2374:     PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY=0 reverts to the legacy behavior where a
 2375:     content-empty pass-through fragment is counted as verified (byte-identical
 2376:     to pre-#1241). Read at call time so tests can toggle without re-import."""
 2377:     v = os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY", "1").strip().lower()
 2378:     return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")
 2379: 
 2380: 
 2381: def _is_content_empty_sentence(sentence: str) -> bool:
 2382:     """I-pipe-016 (#1241). True iff `sentence` carries NO real claim content —
 2383:     no content words AND no numeric values — after stripping citation artifacts
 2384:     (provenance/calc tokens, atom_NNN, bare [ev_XXX]) and dose/placebo/threshold
 2385:     structural residue.
 2386: 
 2387:     Uses the SAME stripping the BUG-03 numeric/empty floor uses inside
 2388:     verify_sentence_provenance, so the two definitions agree: a fragment this
 2389:     returns True for is exactly the kind verify_sentence_provenance would drop
 2390:     with `empty_or_contentless_sentence`. A sentence with ANY content word OR
 2391:     ANY number returns False (never excluded), so REAL sentences are untouched.
 2392:     """
 2393:     cleaned = _verifier_cleaned_text(sentence)
 2394:     stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(cleaned)
 2395:     stripped = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(" ", stripped)
 2396:     stripped = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(" ", stripped)
 2397:     no_content = not _content_words(stripped)
 2398:     no_numbers = not _decimals_in(stripped) and not _numbers_in(stripped)
 2399:     return no_content and no_numbers
 2400: 
 2401: 
 2402: def strict_verify(
 2403:     draft_text: str,
 2404:     evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 2405:     *,
 2406:     require_number_match: bool = True,
 2407:     telemetry_block: str | None = None,
 2408:     quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
 2409: ) -> StrictVerificationReport:
 2410:     """Run strict verification on a draft. Drops failing sentences.
 2411: 
 2412:     Gap-3 (original): the Limitations paragraph was pass-through because
 2413:     it discusses pipeline telemetry, not evidence.
 2414: 
 2415:     BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): when `telemetry_block` is supplied,
 2416:     Limitations sentences ARE verified against it — every numeric
 2417:     claim in the sentence must appear verbatim in the telemetry block.
 2418:     This catches a fabricated "only 3% of sources are T1" claim when
 2419:     the telemetry actually says "T1: 9%". Backward-compatible: if
 2420:     telemetry_block is None (default), falls back to pass-through.
 2421:     """
 2422:     findings_text, limitations_text = split_findings_and_limitations(draft_text)
 2423: 
 2424:     kept: list[SentenceVerification] = []
 2425:     dropped: list[SentenceVerification] = []
 2426: 
 2427:     # I-pipe-015 (#1240) + I-pipe-016 (#1241) flags, read once per call.
 2428:     _honest_tokens = _token_honest_drop_enabled()
 2429:     _skip_empty = _skip_empty_enabled()
 2430: 
 2431:     # I-pipe-016 (#1241): content-empty fragments are excluded from total_in
 2432:     # (denominator) — they are neither kept nor dropped, they do not count.
 2433:     _excluded_empty = 0
 2434: 
 2435:     # Findings: strict provenance verification
 2436:     findings_sentences = split_into_sentences(findings_text)
 2437:     for s in findings_sentences:
 2438:         # I-pipe-015 (#1240): canonicalize a malformed `[ev:...]` token to
 2439:         # `[#ev:...]` so an otherwise-valid citation is not silently lost. The
 2440:         # canonical form is what flows to verify_sentence_provenance (and is
 2441:         # stored on .sentence), so downstream token-stripping in
 2442:         # resolve_provenance_to_citations works and no malformed literal leaks
 2443:         # into the rendered report. The canonicalized token STILL runs the full
 2444:         # validation — bracket format is fixed, no check is bypassed.
 2445:         if _honest_tokens:
 2446:             s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
 2447:         # I-pipe-016 (#1241): a content-empty fragment (orphaned citation /
 2448:         # punctuation residue with no content word and no number) is excluded
 2449:         # from BOTH numerator and denominator. verify_sentence_provenance would
 2450:         # already DROP it (BUG-03 / no_provenance_token), so excluding it from
 2451:         # the denominator instead of dropping it only removes empty noise from
 2452:         # the telemetry; it never lets an unverified REAL sentence through.
 2453:         if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
 2454:             _excluded_empty += 1
 2455:             continue
 2456:         v = verify_sentence_provenance(
 2457:             s, evidence_pool,
 2458:             require_number_match=require_number_match,
 2459:             quantified_models=quantified_models,
 2460:         )
 2461:         if v.is_verified:
 2462:             kept.append(v)
 2463:         else:
 2464:             # I-complete-003 (#1189): before dropping, try to RE-ANCHOR the
 2465:             # sentence to a different span in its cited row (or, if uncited
 2466:             # but verbatim-grounded, to a pool row). The env gate early-outs
 2467:             # so OFF-mode is BYTE-IDENTICAL (no _try_reanchor call, no judge
 2468:             # call, no counter mutation). A rescued result has already passed
 2469:             # the SAME full gate, so no fabrication path is introduced; it
 2470:             # flows through the SAME downstream (kept[]) as a normally-verified
 2471:             # sentence.
 2472:             if _provenance_reanchor_enabled():
 2473:                 rescued = _try_reanchor(
 2474:                     s, evidence_pool,
 2475:                     require_number_match=require_number_match,
 2476:                     quantified_models=quantified_models,
 2477:                 )
 2478:                 if rescued is not None:
 2479:                     kept.append(rescued)
 2480:                     continue
 2481:             dropped.append(v)
 2482: 
 2483:     # Limitations: telemetry-grounded verification if block supplied,
 2484:     # else pass-through (M-204 backward-compat).
 2485:     limitations_sentences = split_into_sentences(limitations_text)
 2486:     for s in limitations_sentences:
 2487:         # I-pipe-015 (#1240): same malformed-token canonicalization as findings.
 2488:         if _honest_tokens:
 2489:             s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
 2490:         # I-pipe-016 (#1241): exclude content-empty pass-through fragments from
 2491:         # the verified numerator AND total_in. This is the PRIMARY inflation
 2492:         # site the forensic flagged: the pass-through branch below counted a
 2493:         # bare `[#ev:...]` orphan as is_verified=True. A REAL limitations
 2494:         # sentence carries telemetry numbers / words and is never excluded.
 2495:         if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
 2496:             _excluded_empty += 1
 2497:             continue
 2498:         if telemetry_block is not None:
 2499:             v = verify_limitations_sentence_against_telemetry(
 2500:                 s, telemetry_block,
 2501:             )
 2502:             if v.is_verified:
 2503:                 kept.append(v)
 2504:             else:
 2505:                 dropped.append(v)
 2506:         else:
 2507:             tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(s)
 2508:             kept.append(SentenceVerification(
 2509:                 sentence=s,
 2510:                 tokens=tokens,
 2511:                 is_verified=True,
 2512:                 failure_reasons=[],
 2513:                 soft_warnings=["limitations_paragraph_pass_through"],
 2514:             ))
 2515: 
 2516:     # I-pipe-016 (#1241): total_in excludes content-empty fragments so the
 2517:     # verified ratio reflects only real sentences. When PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY
 2518:     # is OFF, _excluded_empty stays 0 and total_in is byte-identical to before.
 2519:     total_in = (
 2520:         len(findings_sentences) + len(limitations_sentences) - _excluded_empty
 2521:     )
 2522:     return StrictVerificationReport(
 2523:         kept_sentences=kept,
 2524:         dropped_sentences=dropped,
 2525:         total_in=total_in,
 2526:         total_kept=len(kept),
 2527:         total_dropped=len(dropped),
 2528:     )
 2529: 
 2530: 
 2531: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 2532: # Resolution: strip provenance tokens, add citation markers
 2533: # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 2534: 
 2535: 
 2536: def _basket_for_biblio(basket: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
 2537:     """Project a ``credibility_pass.ClaimBasket`` onto the bibliography render
 2538:     contract (design §6, checklist [11] P5.2).
 2539: 
 2540:     Surfaces ONLY what the basket already computed upstream — never recomputes a
 2541:     verdict and never resurrects a dropped sentence (the basket is assembled in
 2542:     ``credibility_pass`` AFTER strict_verify; a dropped sentence never reaches
 2543:     this resolver, so a ``basket_verdict=full`` can't upgrade it). Each member is
 2544:     shown with its OWN isolated ``span_verdict``: a member with no verified span
 2545:     is surfaced as context/unverified, never silently counted as support. The
 2546:     basket itself is labelled ``partial``/``contested`` in that case (never
 2547:     silently ``full``) by the upstream assembly.
 2548: 
 2549:     Read via ``getattr`` (duck-typed) so this module never imports
 2550:     ``credibility_pass`` (which lazy-imports THIS module — a hard cycle).
 2551:     """
 2552:     members_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 2553:     for member in (getattr(basket, "supporting_members", None) or []):
 2554:         members_out.append({
 2555:             "evidence_id": str(getattr(member, "evidence_id", "") or ""),
 2556:             "source_url": str(getattr(member, "source_url", "") or ""),
 2557:             "source_tier": str(getattr(member, "source_tier", "") or ""),
 2558:             "origin_cluster_id": str(getattr(member, "origin_cluster_id", "") or ""),
 2559:             "credibility_weight": getattr(member, "credibility_weight", None),
 2560:             "authority_score": float(getattr(member, "authority_score", 0.0) or 0.0),
 2561:             # the member's OWN isolated span verdict — SUPPORTS members are the
 2562:             # ones counted in verified_support_origin_count; UNSUPPORTED members
 2563:             # are shown as context, never as support strength.
 2564:             "span_verdict": str(getattr(member, "span_verdict", "") or ""),
 2565:             "direct_quote": str(getattr(member, "direct_quote", "") or ""),
 2566:         })
 2567:     refuter_ids = tuple(
 2568:         str(c) for c in (getattr(basket, "refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ())
 2569:     )
 2570:     return {
 2571:         "claim_cluster_id": str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or ""),
 2572:         "claim_text": str(getattr(basket, "claim_text", "") or ""),
 2573:         "subject": str(getattr(basket, "subject", "") or ""),
 2574:         "predicate": str(getattr(basket, "predicate", "") or ""),
 2575:         # the ONLY strengthening count — N VERIFIED independent origins (isolated
 2576:         # per-member verification), NEVER the clustered total.
 2577:         "verified_support_origin_count": int(
 2578:             getattr(basket, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0
 2579:         ),
 2580:         # ADVISORY clustered count — surfaced labelled, never as support strength.
 2581:         "total_clustered_origin_count": int(
 2582:             getattr(basket, "total_clustered_origin_count", 0) or 0
 2583:         ),
 2584:         "weight_mass": float(getattr(basket, "weight_mass", 0.0) or 0.0),
 2585:         "basket_verdict": str(getattr(basket, "basket_verdict", "") or ""),
 2586:         # contested -> reference to the both_sides neutral block (the refuting
 2587:         # cluster ids), never the refuters' content duplicated here.
 2588:         "refuter_cluster_ids": refuter_ids,
 2589:         "supporting_members": members_out,
 2590:     }
 2591: 
 2592: 
 2593: def resolve_provenance_to_citations(
 2594:     kept_sentences: list[SentenceVerification],
 2595:     evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 2596:     *,
 2597:     baskets: list | None = None,
 2598:     cluster_id_by_evidence: dict[str, list[str]] | None = None,
 2599: ) -> tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
 2600:     """Strip [#ev:...] tokens and replace with numbered citations.
 2601: 
 2602:     Returns (rendered_text, bibliography_list). Bibliography is a list
 2603:     of dicts: {num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement}.
 2604: 
 2605:     I-arch-002 [11] P5.2 — basket-carrying bibliography (design §6). When
 2606:     ``baskets`` (the ``credibility_pass.ClaimBasket`` list) AND
 2607:     ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` (the evidence_id -> claim_cluster_id[] binding,
 2608:     both from ``CredibilityAnalysis``) are supplied, each bibliography row ALSO
 2609:     carries the basket(s) the cited source backs: supporting sources + weights +
 2610:     N VERIFIED independent origins, with a contested basket referencing the
 2611:     both_sides neutral block via ``refuter_cluster_ids``. This is the render
 2612:     extension only; the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / provenance / NLI /
 2613:     4-role) runs UPSTREAM and is untouched — a dropped sentence never reaches
 2614:     this resolver, so no basket label can resurrect it.
 2615: 
 2616:     GATE = parameter presence (NOT an env read). All production callers today
 2617:     pass only ``(kept_sentences, evidence_pool)``; with ``baskets is None`` the
 2618:     emitted rows are the legacy ``{num,evidence_id,url,tier,statement}`` dict
 2619:     BYTE-IDENTICAL. The basket list itself only exists when
 2620:     ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` is ON (``credibility_analysis is not
 2621:     None``), so "behind the master flag" holds transitively — without coupling
 2622:     this render layer to global flag state. The new params are keyword-only with
 2623:     ``None`` defaults so positional callers cannot break.
 2624:     """
 2625:     ev_to_num: dict[str, int] = {}
 2626:     biblio: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 2627: 
 2628:     # Index claim_cluster_id -> projected basket dict ONCE (the binding is
 2629:     # 1-to-MANY: one evidence_id can back several baskets, design §5 per-cluster
 2630:     # rule). Built only when basket data is present, so OFF stays a no-op.
 2631:     _basket_by_cluster: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
 2632:     _carry_baskets = baskets is not None and cluster_id_by_evidence is not None
 2633:     if _carry_baskets:
 2634:         for _basket in (baskets or []):
 2635:             _ccid = str(getattr(_basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")
 2636:             if _ccid:
 2637:                 _basket_by_cluster[_ccid] = _basket_for_biblio(_basket)
 2638: 
 2639:     def _num_for(ev_id: str) -> int:
 2640:         if ev_id not in ev_to_num:
 2641:             ev_to_num[ev_id] = len(ev_to_num) + 1
 2642:             ev = evidence_pool.get(ev_id, {})
 2643:             row: dict[str, Any] = {
 2644:                 "num": ev_to_num[ev_id],
 2645:                 "evidence_id": ev_id,
 2646:                 "url": ev.get("source_url", ""),
 2647:                 "tier": ev.get("tier", ""),
 2648:                 "statement": (ev.get("statement") or "")[:300],
 2649:             }
 2650:             # Enrich with the basket(s) this source backs — ONLY when basket data
 2651:             # was supplied. The legacy 5-key dict above is emitted UNCHANGED when
 2652:             # baskets is None (byte-identical OFF path).
 2653:             if _carry_baskets:
 2654:                 _ccids = (cluster_id_by_evidence or {}).get(ev_id, []) or []
 2655:                 _rows_baskets = [
 2656:                     _basket_by_cluster[c]
 2657:                     for c in _ccids
 2658:                     if c in _basket_by_cluster
 2659:                 ]
 2660:                 row["baskets"] = _rows_baskets
 2661:             biblio.append(row)
 2662:         return ev_to_num[ev_id]
 2663: 
 2664:     findings_lines: list[str] = []
 2665:     limitations_lines: list[str] = []
 2666:     for sv in kept_sentences:
 2667:         # Strip provenance tokens first so degenerate fragments can be
 2668:         # detected before we assign citation numbers (otherwise the
 2669:         # bibliography keeps an entry whose only citing sentence we
 2670:         # later drop). `stripped` is the FINAL rendered sentence body
 2671:         # — must PRESERVE atom_NNN so PR #906 Step 3b validator can
 2672:         # see atom citations downstream in verified_text.
 2673:         stripped = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sv.sentence).strip()
 2674:         # Phase 7 (#991): strip calc tokens too so a verified computed-number
 2675:         # sentence carries its source-input [N] citations but no token leak.
 2676:         stripped = _CALC_TOKEN_RE.sub("", stripped).strip()
 2677:         # Clean trailing spaces before punctuation
 2678:         stripped = re.sub(r"\s+([.!?,;])", r"\1", stripped)
 2679:         # BUG-M-8 (Codex pass 9): drop degenerate sentence fragments
 2680:         # that survive strict_verify as bare punctuation + citation
 2681:         # (observed in the Novo sweep as ".[4]", "Morgan analysts.[12]",
 2682:         # ".[14]" between legitimate sentences). A real sentence has
 2683:         # ≥3 content words AND ≥15 chars of prose after provenance
 2684:         # stripping. Lower bounds deliberately conservative — the
 2685:         # shortest legitimate research sentences in smoke runs
 2686:         # ("No contradictions detected.") comfortably clear it.
 2687:         # Step 3b commit 1 follow-up iter-2 (Codex PR #906 iter-2 P1):
 2688:         # use _verifier_cleaned_text ONLY for the word/length count
 2689:         # (so "atom" in "atom_003" does not inflate the count) — but
 2690:         # DO NOT modify the rendered `stripped` itself; the downstream
 2691:         # validator must see atom_NNN tokens in verified_text.
 2692:         _for_count = _verifier_cleaned_text(sv.sentence)
 2693:         _content_w = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z]+", _for_count)
 2694:         if len(_content_w) < 3 or len(_for_count) < 15:
 2695:             continue
 2696:         # Assign citation numbers only for surviving sentences
 2697:         used_nums: list[int] = []
 2698:         for tok in sv.tokens:
 2699:             n = _num_for(tok.evidence_id)
 2700:             if n not in used_nums:
 2701:                 used_nums.append(n)
 2702:         # Append citation markers
 2703:         markers = "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in used_nums)
 2704:         sentence_out = stripped + markers
 2705: 
 2706:         # Gap-3: put Limitations sentences in a separate paragraph so
 2707:         # they render on their own line in the final report.
 2708:         if any("limitations_paragraph_pass_through" in w for w in sv.soft_warnings):
 2709:             limitations_lines.append(sentence_out)
 2710:         else:
 2711:             findings_lines.append(sentence_out)
 2712: 
 2713:     findings_para = " ".join(findings_lines)
 2714:     if limitations_lines:
 2715:         limitations_para = " ".join(limitations_lines)
 2716:         return (findings_para + "\n\n" + limitations_para, biblio)
 2717:     return findings_para, biblio

 6114:                 validate_section,

    2: Evidence selector — BUG-M-201 fix (deep-dive R6).
    3: 
    4: Pre-fix, `run_one_query` passed `retrieval.evidence_rows[:PG_LIVE_MAX_EV_TO_GEN]`
    5: to the generator — raw retrieval order with no tier awareness. Gates
    6: reasoned over the full pool; generation reasoned over a smaller,
    7: order-biased subset. Real artifacts showed 20 corpus sources but
    8: only 4 evidence rows surviving to generation.
    9: 
   10: Post-fix, `select_evidence_for_generation()` produces a deterministic,
   11: tier-balanced, relevance-ranked subset. The result is emitted to
   12: `manifest.json.evidence_selection` so downstream consumers can see
   13: exactly what the generator saw.

   15: from __future__ import annotations

   29: # Tier priority for within-pool ranking. Lower tier number = higher priority.
   30: _TIER_PRIORITY: dict[str, int] = {
   31:     "T1": 1, "T2": 2, "T3": 3, "T4": 4,
   32:     "T5": 5, "T6": 6, "T7": 7, "UNKNOWN": 8,

   35: # I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989): default relevance floor for `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`.
   36: _DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR = 0.30
   37: 
   38: 
   39: def parse_relevance_floor(
   40:     raw: str | None, *, default: float = _DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR,
   41: ) -> float:
   42:     """Parse + validate ``PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`` (I-meta-005 Phase 5 #989).
   43: 
   44:     Range is (0.0, 1.0]. Empty/None -> ``default``. An unparseable float OR an
   45:     out-of-range value raises ``ValueError`` (FAIL LOUD) — a missing/garbage floor
   46:     must never silently send an unbounded, unfiltered pool to the generator.
   47:     """
   48:     if raw is None or not str(raw).strip():
   49:         value = default
   50:     else:
   51:         try:
   52:             value = float(str(raw).strip())
   53:         except ValueError as exc:
   54:             raise ValueError(
   55:                 "PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR must be a float in (0.0, 1.0]; got "
   56:                 f"{raw!r}"
   57:             ) from exc
   58:     if not (0.0 < value <= 1.0):
   59:         raise ValueError(
   60:             f"PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR out of range (0.0, 1.0]: {value}"
   61:         )
   62:     return value
   63: 
   64: _STOPWORDS = frozenset({
   65:     "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "but", "is", "are", "was", "were",
   66:     "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "with", "by", "from", "as",
   67:     "that", "this", "these", "those", "it", "its", "be", "been",
   68:     "what", "which", "who", "how", "why", "when", "where",
   69: })
   70: 
   71: 
   72: def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
   73:     """Lowercase content-word tokens, stopword-filtered, 3+ chars."""
   74:     toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", text.lower())
   75:     return {t for t in toks if t not in _STOPWORDS}

 1401: # Deliberately does NOT denylist .gov / .edu / nber / doi.org (credibility is not

 1408: def _scope_denylist_domains() -> tuple[str, ...]:
 1409:     """Parse `PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS` (comma-separated, default empty = OFF).
 1410:     Each entry is lowercased + stripped; blank entries dropped. Empty env =>
 1411:     `()` => the denylist gate is a byte-identical no-op."""
 1412:     raw = os.environ.get("PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS", "").strip()
 1413:     if not raw:
 1414:         return ()
 1415:     return tuple(
 1416:         entry.strip().lower()
 1417:         for entry in raw.split(",")
 1418:         if entry.strip()
 1419:     )
 1420: 
 1421: 
 1422: def _row_netloc(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 1423:     """Lowercased hostname for an evidence row's source URL. Reuses the
 1424:     urlparse pattern already used elsewhere in this module (lines ~197/751)."""
 1425:     url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip().lower()
 1426:     if not url:
 1427:         return ""
 1428:     from urllib.parse import urlparse
 1429:     try:
 1430:         host = urlparse(
 1431:             url if "://" in url else f"http://{url}"
 1432:         ).hostname or ""
 1433:     except Exception:
 1434:         host = ""
 1435:     return host.lower()
 1436: 
 1437: 
 1438: def _netloc_matches_denylist(netloc: str, denylist: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
 1439:     """True iff a netloc matches a denylist entry.
 1440: 
 1441:     Dotted entries (e.g. `facebook.com`, `en.wikipedia.org`) match on EXACT
 1442:     netloc OR a `.`+entry suffix (so `m.facebook.com` matches but
 1443:     `facebook.com.evil.org` does NOT). Bare-token entries (e.g. `blogspot`,
 1444:     `reddit`) match on substring-in-netloc (catches `foo.blogspot.com`)."""
 1445:     if not netloc or not denylist:
 1446:         return False
 1447:     for entry in denylist:
 1448:         if "." in entry:
 1449:             if netloc == entry or netloc.endswith("." + entry):
 1450:                 return True
 1451:         else:
 1452:             if entry in netloc:
 1453:                 return True
 1454:     return False
 1455: 
 1456: 
 1457: def _apply_scope_denylist(
 1458:     scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
 1459:     primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
 1460: ) -> tuple[list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]], int, list[str]]:
 1461:     """Drop scored rows whose netloc matches `PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS`.
 1462: 
 1463:     EXEMPT: marquee / required-entity anchors AND named-trial primary anchors
 1464:     (the same exemption set as the relevance floor). Returns
 1465:     `(kept_scored, n_dropped, dropped_netlocs)`. When the env var is empty the
 1466:     denylist is `()` and the input is returned UNCHANGED (byte-identical no-op).
 1467:     Pure — does not mutate the caller's rows."""
 1468:     denylist = _scope_denylist_domains()
 1469:     if not denylist:
 1470:         return scored, 0, []
 1471:     anchors = list(primary_trial_anchors or [])
 1472: 
 1473:     def _is_exempt(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
 1474:         if _row_is_marquee_anchor(row):
 1475:             return True
 1476:         return any(_m42e_detect_primary_for_anchor(row, a) for a in anchors)
 1477: 
 1478:     # I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W2scope: under the master redesign flag the denylist becomes
 1479:     # a credibility-CLASS WEIGHT, not a DROP (DNA §-1.3 — social/junk hosts STAY at low
 1480:     # weight; they sometimes report a real journal). The matched row is KEPT, stamped
 1481:     # `scope_denylist_demoted` + a low credibility class, and surfaced per-citation.
 1482:     # OFF => the exact prior `continue`-drop => byte-identical.
 1483:     _cred_redesign = _credibility_redesign_enabled()
 1484:     kept: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]] = []
 1485:     dropped_netlocs: list[str] = []
 1486:     for item in scored:
 1487:         row = item[3]
 1488:         netloc = _row_netloc(row)
 1489:         if _netloc_matches_denylist(netloc, denylist) and not _is_exempt(row):
 1490:             if _cred_redesign:
 1491:                 demoted = dict(row)
 1492:                 demoted["scope_denylist_demoted"] = True
 1493:                 demoted["credibility_class"] = "low_denylist"
 1494:                 kept.append((item[0], item[1], item[2], demoted))
 1495:                 continue
 1496:             dropped_netlocs.append(netloc)
 1497:             continue
 1498:         kept.append(item)
 1499:     return kept, len(dropped_netlocs), dropped_netlocs
