 1501: 
 1502: # ── I-scope-001 (#1244): arXiv -> journal version preference (gate 3, pure) ────
 1503: # Grounded diagnosis: 2 of the 9 drb_72 contaminants were arXiv/preprint twins of
 1504: # a paper that ALSO appears as a published journal/DOI row — the citation should
 1505: # prefer the published version. This gate, when ON (`PG_SCOPE_PREFER_JOURNAL`,
 1506: # default OFF), groups rows by NORMALIZED title and, for any title that appears as
 1507: # BOTH an arxiv.org row AND a non-arxiv journal/DOI row, drops the arXiv twin(s)
 1508: # and keeps the journal row. An arXiv row with NO journal twin is NEVER dropped
 1509: # (two arXiv versions with no journal twin both survive). Default OFF => no-op.
 1510: # Faithfulness-safe (subtract-only, see gate-1 note).
 1511: 
 1512: 
 1513: def _prefer_journal_enabled() -> bool:
 1514:     """Kill-switch `PG_SCOPE_PREFER_JOURNAL` (default OFF). When ON, an arXiv
 1515:     twin of a journal/DOI row is dropped in favor of the published version."""
 1516:     raw = os.environ.get("PG_SCOPE_PREFER_JOURNAL", "0").strip().lower()
 1517:     return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")
 1518: 
 1519: 
 1520: def _normalize_title_for_twin(title: str) -> str:
 1521:     """Normalize a title for arXiv<->journal twin matching: lowercase,
 1522:     collapse all non-alphanumeric runs to single spaces, strip. Empty in =>
 1523:     empty out (an untitled row can never twin-match)."""
 1524:     if not title:
 1525:         return ""
 1526:     return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", " ", title.lower()).strip()
 1527: 
 1528: 
 1529: def _row_is_arxiv(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
 1530:     """True iff the row's source host is arxiv.org (preprint host)."""
 1531:     return "arxiv.org" in _row_netloc(row)
 1532: 
 1533: 
 1534: def _row_has_journal_doi(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
 1535:     """True iff the row looks like a published journal/DOI version (non-arXiv):
 1536:     a non-arxiv host AND a DOI marker (doi field, or `doi.org`/`/10.` in URL).
 1537:     Conservative — a row that is neither arXiv nor a clear DOI/journal row is
 1538:     treated as NEITHER twin side, so it is never used to evict an arXiv row."""
 1539:     if _row_is_arxiv(row):
 1540:         return False
 1541:     if row.get("doi"):
 1542:         return True
 1543:     url = (row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").lower()
 1544:     return "doi.org" in url or "/10." in url
 1545: 
 1546: 
 1547: def prefer_journal_over_arxiv(
 1548:     rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
 1549: ) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], int, list[str]]:
 1550:     """Drop arXiv rows that have a journal/DOI twin (same normalized title).
 1551: 
 1552:     Returns `(kept_rows, n_dropped, dropped_titles)`. Pure — does not mutate
 1553:     rows; preserves input order of the kept rows. Never drops an arXiv row
 1554:     whose normalized title has no non-arXiv journal/DOI twin in the pool."""
 1555:     # Build the set of normalized titles that have a journal/DOI representative.
 1556:     journal_titles: set[str] = set()
 1557:     for row in rows:
 1558:         if _row_has_journal_doi(row):
 1559:             norm = _normalize_title_for_twin(_row_title_text(row))
 1560:             if norm:
 1561:                 journal_titles.add(norm)
 1562:     if not journal_titles:
 1563:         return list(rows), 0, []
 1564:     # I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W2scope: under the master redesign flag the arXiv twin is
 1565:     # CONSOLIDATED as a VERSION of the same source (CONSOLIDATE-don't-DROP, DNA §-1.3),
 1566:     # not dropped — the preprint + journal both stay (journal is the preferred rep).
 1567:     # OFF => the exact prior twin-drop => byte-identical.
 1568:     _cred_redesign = _credibility_redesign_enabled()
 1569:     kept: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 1570:     dropped_titles: list[str] = []
 1571:     for row in rows:
 1572:         if _row_is_arxiv(row):
 1573:             norm = _normalize_title_for_twin(_row_title_text(row))
 1574:             if norm and norm in journal_titles:
 1575:                 if _cred_redesign:
 1576:                     twin = dict(row)
 1577:                     twin["arxiv_journal_twin"] = True
 1578:                     twin["preferred_version"] = "journal"
 1579:                     kept.append(twin)
 1580:                     continue
 1581:                 dropped_titles.append(_row_title_text(row) or "(no title)")
 1582:                 continue
 1583:         kept.append(row)
 1584:     return kept, len(dropped_titles), dropped_titles
 1585: 
 1586: 
 1587: def _credibility_redesign_enabled() -> bool:
 1588:     """I-arch-002 (#1246): master switch for the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE redesign
 1589:     (CLAUDE.md §-1.3). Default OFF — when unset, every redesign branch below stays
 1590:     on the legacy DROP/CAP path, so selection is byte-identical. Read from
 1591:     ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` (the single master flag that governs the whole
 1592:     migration; the selection boundary previously had ZERO flag influence)."""
 1593:     return os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN", "").strip().lower() in (
 1594:         "1", "true", "yes", "on",
 1595:     )
 1596: 
 1597: 
 1598: def _relevance_floor_selection(
 1599:     *,
 1600:     scored: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]],
 1601:     relevance_floor: float,
 1602:     full_counts: dict[str, int],
 1603:     primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None,
 1604: ) -> EvidenceSelection:
 1605:     """I-meta-005 Phase 5 (#989): relevance-floor selection (no max_rows cap).
 1606: 
 1607:     Keep EVERY row whose lexical relevance >= ``relevance_floor``, PLUS any row
 1608:     matching a primary trial anchor (floor-EXEMPT — a relevant primary RCT must
 1609:     never be dropped on a low lexical score). Ranked by ``relevance x authority``
 1610:     (authority = the row's ``authority_score`` sidecar, default 1.0). Each kept
 1611:     row is stamped with the additive ``selection_relevance`` float so
 1612:     ``finding_dedup`` picks representatives on the IDENTICAL score (no recompute
 1613:     drift). Pure; returns SHALLOW COPIES (never mutates the caller's rows).
 1614:     """
 1615:     # I-scope-001 (#1244) gate 1: low-cred domain denylist. Default-OFF — when
 1616:     # `PG_SCOPE_DENYLIST_DOMAINS` is empty, `_apply_scope_denylist` returns
 1617:     # `scored` UNCHANGED (byte-identical), so the keep/sort below is exactly the
 1618:     # prior behavior. Marquee / required-entity / primary-anchor rows are EXEMPT.
 1619:     scored, _denylist_dropped, _denylist_netlocs = _apply_scope_denylist(
 1620:         scored, primary_trial_anchors
 1621:     )
 1622:     if _denylist_dropped:
 1623:         _LOGGER.info(
 1624:             "[scope] denylist dropped %d low-cred source(s): %s",
 1625:             _denylist_dropped,
 2020:         # Preservation guard: don't expand T1 usage beyond its
 2021:         # quota. The floor operates WITHIN the T1 quota — if
 2022:         # T1 quota is 8 and 6 anchor-matched primaries exist, all
 2023:         # 6 primaries + 2 top-scored rows fill the T1 quota.
 2024:         # If primary_count > T1 quota, cap reservations at quota.
 2025:         if len(m42e_primary_ids) > quotas.get("T1", 0):
 2026:             # Reduce reservations to match quota — keep highest-scored
 2027:             # primaries.
 2028:             t1_items_by_score = sorted(
 2029:                 [i for i in t1_items_for_m42e if id(i) in m42e_primary_ids],
 2030:                 key=lambda x: (-x[1], x[0]),
 2031:             )
 2032:             m42e_primary_ids = set(
 2033:                 id(i) for i in t1_items_by_score[:quotas.get("T1", 0)]
 2034:             )
 2035: 
 2036:     selected: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]] = []
 2037:     # M-42d: telemetry notes populated inside the T3 block (HC expansion).
 2038:     # Collected here and flushed to `notes` after the main tier loop.
 2039:     _m42d_pending_notes: list[str] = []
 2040:     # #956: ids of T3 jurisdiction/HC floor-reserved rows, so the diversity
 2041:     # passes never evict a regulatory-floor row (the m42e/m42c/m51 floors are
 2042:     # already tracked by their own id sets).
 2043:     _t3_floor_protected_ids: set[int] = set()
 2044:     for tier, quota in quotas.items():
 2045:         # M-42e + M-42c: for T1 tier, reserve named-trial primary
 2046:         # slots (M-42e) AND mechanism-evidence slots (M-42c) before
 2047:         # filling rest by relevance. Both floors are computed above;
 2048:         # a row can satisfy both (e.g. a SURPASS-2 primary paper
 2049:         # that also has mechanism content — counted once).
 2050:         if tier == "T1" and quota > 0 and (m42e_primary_ids or m42c_mech_ids):
 2051:             tier_items = by_tier.get("T1", [])
 2052:             # Priority order: M-42e primary floor, then M-42c mech
 2053:             # floor, then fill by relevance. Reserved IDs are union
 2054:             # of both; deduped so a row satisfies at most one slot.
 2055:             reserved_t1: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]] = []
 2056:             reserved_t1_ids: set[int] = set()
 2057:             # Pass 1: M-42e primaries
 2058:             for item in tier_items:
 2059:                 if len(reserved_t1) >= quota:
 2060:                     break
 2061:                 if id(item) in m42e_primary_ids and id(item) not in reserved_t1_ids:
 2062:                     reserved_t1.append(item)
 2063:                     reserved_t1_ids.add(id(item))
 2064:             # Pass 2: M-42c mechanism T1 rows
 2065:             for item in tier_items:
 2066:                 if len(reserved_t1) >= quota:
 2067:                     break
 2068:                 if id(item) in m42c_mech_ids and id(item) not in reserved_t1_ids:
 2069:                     reserved_t1.append(item)
 2070:                     reserved_t1_ids.add(id(item))
 2071:             # Pass 3: fill remaining T1 slots by relevance
 2072:             for item in tier_items:
 2073:                 if len(reserved_t1) >= quota:
 2074:                     break
 2075:                 if id(item) not in reserved_t1_ids:
 2076:                     reserved_t1.append(item)
 2077:                     reserved_t1_ids.add(id(item))
 2078:             selected.extend(reserved_t1)
 2079:             continue
 2080:         # M-42c: for T2 tier with mechanism floor, reserve mech T2
 2081:         # rows before filling rest by relevance.
 2082:         if tier == "T2" and quota > 0 and m42c_mech_ids:
 2083:             tier_items = by_tier.get("T2", [])
 2084:             reserved_t2: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]] = []
 2085:             reserved_t2_ids: set[int] = set()
 2086:             # Pass 1: M-42c mechanism T2 rows
 2087:             for item in tier_items:
 2088:                 if len(reserved_t2) >= quota:
 2089:                     break
 2090:                 if id(item) in m42c_mech_ids and id(item) not in reserved_t2_ids:
 2091:                     reserved_t2.append(item)
 2092:                     reserved_t2_ids.add(id(item))
 2093:             # Pass 2: fill remaining T2 by relevance
 2094:             for item in tier_items:
 2095:                 if len(reserved_t2) >= quota:
 2096:                     break
 2097:                 if id(item) not in reserved_t2_ids:
 2098:                     reserved_t2.append(item)
 2099:                     reserved_t2_ids.add(id(item))
 2100:             selected.extend(reserved_t2)
 2101:             continue
 2102:         # M-41d: for T3 (regulatory) tier, enforce a
 2103:         # jurisdictional-diversity floor — reserve one slot per
 2104:         # present jurisdiction (FDA, EMA, NICE, HC, ...) before
 2105:         # filling the rest of the T3 quota by relevance. Prevents the
 2106:         # V24 failure mode where Health Canada evidence was in the
 2107:         # corpus but outcompeted within T3 by higher-scoring FDA/EMA.
 2108:         #
 2109:         # M-42d (2026-04-22): after the per-jurisdiction first-slot
 2110:         # pass, HC gets up to `_m42d_hc_quota()` additional reserved
 2111:         # slots (default 2 total). Preservation guard: HC's 2nd..Nth
 2112:         # slots only fire AFTER every present jurisdiction already has
 2113:         # its 1st, so FDA/EMA/NICE/MHRA are never displaced. The
 2114:         # relevance fill that follows still lets high-scoring
 2115:         # FDA/EMA/NICE rows beyond 1 slot be selected naturally.
 2116:         tier_items = by_tier.get(tier, [])
 2117:         if tier == "T3" and quota > 0 and tier_items:
 2118:             # Group T3 items by jurisdiction.
 2119:             juris_groups: dict[str | None, list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]]] = {}
 2120:             for item in tier_items:
 2121:                 jur = _row_jurisdiction(item[3])
 2122:                 juris_groups.setdefault(jur, []).append(item)
 2123:             present_juris = [j for j in juris_groups if j is not None]
 2124:             # Reserve 1 slot per present jurisdiction (capped at the
 2125:             # T3 quota — we never over-fill). Pick the top-scoring
 2126:             # row per jurisdiction as the reserved slot.
 2127:             reserved: list[tuple[int, float, str, dict[str, Any]]] = []
 2128:             reserved_ids: set[int] = set()
 2129:             slots_left = quota
 2130:             for jur in present_juris:
 2131:                 if slots_left <= 0:
 2132:                     break
 2133:                 for item in juris_groups[jur]:
 2134:                     if id(item) not in reserved_ids:
 2135:                         reserved.append(item)
 2136:                         reserved_ids.add(id(item))
 2137:                         slots_left -= 1
 2138:                         break
 2139:             # M-42d: HC quota expansion. After every present
 2140:             # jurisdiction has its 1st slot, reserve up to
 2141:             # (hc_quota - 1) additional HC rows from the quota that
 2142:             # remains. This NEVER displaces FDA/EMA/NICE's 1st slots
 2143:             # because those are already allocated above.
 2144:             hc_quota = _m42d_hc_quota()
 2145:             m42d_hc_extras = 0
 2146:             if (
 2147:                 hc_quota > 1
 2148:                 and _M42D_HC_JURISDICTION_CODE in juris_groups
 2149:                 and slots_left > 0
 2150:             ):
 2151:                 hc_rows = juris_groups[_M42D_HC_JURISDICTION_CODE]
 2152:                 # Rows are already in tier_items' original order (by
 2153:                 # score then index). Skip the one already reserved.
 2154:                 extras_remaining = hc_quota - 1
 2155:                 for item in hc_rows:
 2156:                     if slots_left <= 0 or extras_remaining <= 0:
 2157:                         break
 2158:                     if id(item) not in reserved_ids:
 2159:                         reserved.append(item)
 2160:                         reserved_ids.add(id(item))
 2161:                         slots_left -= 1
 2162:                         extras_remaining -= 1
 2163:                         m42d_hc_extras += 1
 2164:             # #956: at this point reserved_ids holds ONLY the jurisdiction +
 2165:             # HC floor slots (the relevance-fill below is NOT a floor). Capture
 2166:             # them so the diversity passes treat them as protected.
 2167:             _t3_floor_protected_ids |= set(reserved_ids)
 2168:             # Fill remaining slots from the tier's global score order,
 2169:             # skipping already-reserved items.
 2170:             for item in tier_items:
 2171:                 if slots_left <= 0:
 2172:                     break
 2173:                 if id(item) not in reserved_ids:
 2174:                     reserved.append(item)
 2175:                     reserved_ids.add(id(item))

 4835:         def _compute_m50_skip_anchors(
 4836:             contract_plans: list[Any],
 4837:             primary_anchors: list[str],
 4838:         ) -> set[str]:
 4839:             """Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 2: map contract entity ids
 4840:             to M-50 trial anchor names via a normalized substring
 4841:             match so the sweep can tell M-50 which anchors are
 4842:             already owned by a contract slot.
 4843: 
 4844:             Normalization: strip `_`/`-`, lowercase. Match anchor
 4845:             against each entity_id; a normalized-anchor substring
 4846:             hit inside a normalized-entity-id is strong evidence

 4848:             `surpass_2_primary` contains `surpass2` → SURPASS-2).
 4849: 
 4850:             Conservative: returns empty set if no contract plans
 4851:             present, or if no match found. False negatives cost
 4852:             one duplicate subsection (reader confusion, not
 4853:             pipeline failure); false positives would SUPPRESS a
 4854:             legitimate per-trial section, which is worse.
 4855:             """

1580:def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
1581:    """Gate-B CLI launcher (I-meta-008 #1014). The ONLY entrypoint that runs the 4-role
1582:    benchmark pipeline. NO SPEND / NO NETWORK at import — all runtime work is here.
1583:
1584:    Exactly one of {--only, --all, --list} is required. `--list`/`--dry-run` previews the
1585:    resolved config with NO spend, NO network, and NO env mutation. A real run
1586:    (`--only <slug>` / `--all`) delegates ENTIRELY to `run_gate_b_query` per question (which
1587:    owns the env-flips, the fail-loud preflight BEFORE any token, the stage marker, and
1588:    `run_one_query`). Returns 0 on success, 2 on a usage/loader error, 1 if any question run
1589:    does not reach a `success`/`partial_*` status.
1590:    """

1598:        ),
1599:    )

8692:    print("=" * 72)
8693:    print()
8694:
8695:    # M-INT-0b: enter replay context if a pin was loaded.
8696:    # apply_replay_plan returns a context manager that restores
8697:    # the prior env snapshot on exit, so this whole sweep is

8699:    # don't have to re-indent the existing sweep loop body.
8700:    from contextlib import nullcontext

8704:    else:
8705:        replay_ctx_mgr = nullcontext()

8708:    # list from each query (when defined) and warms the cache so

8710:    # Best-effort — failure logged, sweep proceeds.

8720:            canonical_urls=canonical_urls,

8728:                f"errored={sweep_warm_summary['errored_count']}"
8729:            )
8730:

8732:        sweep_freshness_summary = _check_corpus_freshness(
8733:            workspace_id="sweep",
8734:            canonical_urls=canonical_urls,
8735:            out_root=out_root,
8736:        )

8738:            per_status_counts = ", ".join(
8739:                f"{status}={count}"
8740:                for status, count in sweep_freshness_summary["per_status"].items()
8741:            )

8746:                f"evicted_count={sweep_freshness_summary['evicted_count']}"
8747:            )
8748:
8749:    # M-INT-7: sweep-level billing quota check + consume.
8750:    # Charges one AUDIT_RUN_ENQUEUED unit per sweep invocation
8751:    # (not per query — the sweep is the unit of work).
8752:    # Per LAW II — wrap in try/except for defense-in-depth.
8753:    #
8754:    # Codex round-2 MEDIUM fix (v3): only consume quota when
8755:    # there's actual work to bill for. v1+v2 consumed
8756:    # unconditionally; an empty sweep (e.g. --only matched no
8757:    # slugs, or SWEEP_QUERIES=[]) burned one unit per invocation.
8758:    # Codex repro: cap=1 + SWEEP_QUERIES=[] → first empty sweep
8759:    # returned rc=0 but used the unit; second empty sweep was
8760:    # then refused. Fix: skip the helper entirely when
8761:    # queries_to_run is empty.

8766:        )
8767:        billing_summary = None
8768:    else:
8769:        try:
8770:            billing_summary = _check_audit_run_quota()
8771:        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
8772:            print(f"[M-INT-7] WARN: billing quota helper raised: {exc}")
8773:            billing_summary = None
8774:    if billing_summary is not None:
8775:        if billing_summary.get("exceeded"):
8776:            # Codex round-1 HIGH fix (v2): EXCEEDED must GATE
8777:            # the sweep, not just log. Per FINAL_PLAN M-INT-7:
8778:            # "M-NEW billing/quota gates production". v1 only
8779:            # printed the EXCEEDED line and continued to run
8780:            # queries — Codex repro: exhausted quota + stubbed
8781:            # run_one_query showed the loop still executed.

8783:            # quota-refusal sweep summary so callers can detect
8784:            # the refusal.
8785:            print(

8787:                f"org={billing_summary['org_id']} "
8788:                f"reason={billing_summary.get('reason', '<n/a>')!r}"
8789:            )
8790:            print(
8791:                "[M-INT-7] sweep refused: org over quota; "
8792:                "no queries executed"
8793:            )
8794:            try:
8795:                refusal_summary = {
8796:                    "status": "abort_quota_exceeded",
8797:                    "billing_quota": billing_summary,
8798:                    "queries_attempted": 0,
8799:                    "out_root": str(out_root),
8800:                }
8801:                refusal_path = out_root / "sweep_quota_refusal.json"
8802:                refusal_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
8803:                refusal_path.write_text(
8804:                    json.dumps(refusal_summary, indent=2, sort_keys=True)
8805:                    + "\n",
8806:                    encoding="utf-8",
8807:                )
8808:            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
8809:                # Best-effort artifact write — don't gate on FS errors.
8810:                print(
8811:                    f"[M-INT-7] WARN: refusal summary write failed: {exc}"
8812:                )

8819:            f"remaining={billing_summary['remaining']}"

1834:                        f"missing={sorted(missing_pct_int)}"
1835:                    )

1872:                # judge being in ENFORCE mode — the ONLY mode where the

1874:                # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off the judge never runs, and under
1875:                # =warn the judge runs but NEVER drops (log-only). In both, a

191:    def judge(self, sentence: str, span: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
192:        """Return (verdict, reason).
193:
194:        verdict is one of "ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED".
195:
196:        I-transport-001 (#1191) Site 4: a transient transport/parse/empty-choices/bad-verdict
197:        fault is now RETRIED on the same provider up to `PG_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES` extra times
198:        (default 2 => 3 total attempts; env-driven per LAW VI) before the method emits the
199:        ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...') sentinel on exhaustion. This is NOT a fail-OPEN: both
200:        consumers FAIL CLOSED on that sentinel — `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:295-301`
201:        keys on the `judge_error:` PREFIX alone (drops in enforce); `provenance_generator.py:1795`
202:        sets `judge_error_flag` on `verdict=='ENTAILED' AND reason.startswith('judge_error:')`
203:        (consumed -> drop). The sentinel stays EXACTLY ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...') so that
204:        detection is preserved (do NOT flip to NEUTRAL standalone — that bypasses the
205:        provenance detection at :1795). The retry's only effect is to stop a SINGLE transient
206:        judge fault from OVER-DROPPING a salvageable verdict (the drb_72-class coverage collapse),
207:        without loosening the binding faithfulness gate.
208:
209:        I-bug-100: after each successful httpx call this method records
210:        the call cost via openrouter_client's module-level helpers
211:        (`_add_run_cost`, `check_run_budget`, `_COST_LEDGER_PATH`) so
212:        judge spend is visible to the per-run budget cap and the cost
213:        ledger. `BudgetExceededError` is explicitly re-raised before
214:        the broad retry/fail-closed handler so a cap breach aborts the
215:        sweep cleanly instead of being masked as a transient judge error
216:        or being retried.
217:        """
218:        prompt = _select_entailment_prompt().format(span=span, sentence=sentence)
219:        started = time.monotonic()
220:        # I-bug-946 (#932): when Path-B gate is active, force singleton provider routing in
221:        # the request body to match the resolved-at-preflight per-role provider. Without this,
222:        # this direct httpx path bypasses the gate's routing intent (the OpenRouterClient path
223:        # also got the override via openrouter_client.py:1400-1410). Codex iter-2 P1#2.
224:        # Codex iter-1 diff P1#2: this lookup MUST use explicit role="evaluator", NOT the
225:        # ambient _ROLE contextvar. The entailment judge fires during section generation
226:        # (where _ROLE=="generator"), but it posts the evaluator-family model — using the
227:        # ambient role would route Gemma to the generator's provider (Fireworks, no Gemma).
228:        try:
229:            from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_for_routing
230:            _gate_provider = _pathb_for_routing.get_role_provider("evaluator")
231:        except Exception:
232:            _gate_provider = None
233:        # Operator 2026-06-13: reasoning stays MAX; any sub-max/off effort is coerced UP to high so the
234:        # NLI verdict is never starved. Un-starved max_tokens lets the high-effort reasoning complete AND
235:        # emit the JSON verdict (the old max_tokens=100 truncated mid-reasoning -> empty -> coverage collapse).
236:        _ent_effort = (os.environ.get(_ENV_ENTAILMENT_REASONING_EFFORT, "").strip().lower()
237:                       or _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_REASONING_EFFORT)
238:        if _ent_effort not in ("high", "xhigh"):
239:            _ent_effort = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_REASONING_EFFORT
240:        try:
241:            _ent_maxtok = max(256, int(os.environ.get(_ENV_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS)
242:                                       or _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS))
243:        except (TypeError, ValueError):
244:            _ent_maxtok = _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS

351:    def judge(self, claim_a: str, claim_b: str) -> tuple:
352:        """Return ``(label, confidence)`` with label in {contradict,entail,neutral}.
353:
354:        Fail-open on API/parse error → ("neutral", 0.0) so a transient outage never
355:        FABRICATES a conflict. ``BudgetExceededError`` is re-raised (the caller
356:        stops + keeps partial) — never masked as a neutral result.
357:        """
358:        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc
359:
360:        prompt = _CONTRADICTION_PROMPT.format(claim_a=claim_a, claim_b=claim_b)
361:        # I-arch-002 (#1251 sibling): GLM-5.1 is a REASONING model; at max_tokens=60 it truncated mid-
362:        # reasoning -> EMPTY content -> json.loads(None) NoneType -> fail-open neutral (silently misses real
363:        # conflicts). Operator 2026-06-13: reasoning stays MAX. Un-starve so high-effort reasoning completes
364:        # AND emits the JSON verdict; any sub-max/off effort is coerced UP to high. Env-overridable (LAW VI).
365:        _sc_effort = (os.environ.get("PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_REASONING_EFFORT", "").strip().lower()
366:                      or "high")
367:        if _sc_effort not in ("high", "xhigh"):
368:            _sc_effort = "high"
369:        try:
370:            _sc_maxtok = max(256, int(os.environ.get("PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS", "2000") or "2000"))
371:        except (TypeError, ValueError):
372:            _sc_maxtok = 2000
373:        json_body: dict = {
374:            "model": self._model,
375:            "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
376:            "temperature": 0.0,
377:            "max_tokens": _sc_maxtok,
378:            "reasoning": {"effort": _sc_effort},
379:            "response_format": {"type": "json_object"},
380:        }
381:        try:
382:            from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_for_routing
383:            _gate_provider = _pathb_for_routing.get_role_provider("evaluator")
384:        except Exception:
385:            _gate_provider = None
386:        # I-arch-002 (#1250): operator-directed — skip the single-provider pin when
387:        # PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS is set so the open-weight evaluator model free-routes to its
388:        # fastest provider (the model is the sovereign unit; hosting provider may be US/China).
389:        _free_route = os.environ.get("PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS", "").strip().lower() in (
390:            "1", "true", "yes", "on",
391:        )
392:        if _gate_provider and not _free_route:
393:            json_body["provider"] = {
394:                "order": [_gate_provider],
395:                "allow_fallbacks": False,
396:                "require_parameters": True,
397:            }
398:        def _emit_raw_io(status: str, raw_response) -> None:
399:            # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3 (gate iter-1 P1): ONE raw-IO record per call tagged by its TRUE
400:            # outcome — "ok" only after a parsed verdict; "judge_error" on parse-failure / fail-open.
401:            try:
402:                _io_sink = _orc.current_raw_io_sink()
403:                if _io_sink is None:
404:                    return
405:                import uuid as _uuid
406:                _io_sink.record(
407:                    call_id=_uuid.uuid4().hex, call_type="nli_conflict_judge", role="evaluator",
408:                    request={**json_body, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]},
409:                    raw_response=raw_response, duration_ms=None, status=status,
410:                )
411:            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
412:                pass
413:
414:        data = None  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3: bound before the try so the fail-open capture can prefer
415:        # the served response (post ok, parse failed) over the exception string.
416:        try:
417:            response = self._client.post(
418:                self._endpoint,
419:                headers={
420:                    "Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}",
421:                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
422:                },
423:                json=json_body,
424:            )
425:            response.raise_for_status()
426:            data = response.json()
427:            usage = data.get("usage", {}) or {}
428:            input_tokens = int(usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
429:            output_tokens = int(usage.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
430:            api_cost = float(usage.get("cost", 0) or 0)
431:            actual_cost = api_cost or _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
432:                self._model, input_tokens, output_tokens, 0,
433:            )
434:            if actual_cost == 0 and not usage:
435:                actual_cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(self._model, 400, 60, 0)
436:            _orc._add_run_cost(actual_cost)
437:            # FX-11b (#1117): also write the canonical cost-ledger ROW for this
438:            # NLI-conflict judge call. It already feeds the run BUDGET via
439:            # _add_run_cost above, but without a ledger row the persisted ledger
440:            # total trails the run-budget total whenever PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT is
441:            # on. Route through the same canonical writer + ambient-run-id key the
442:            # judge/role writers use, so all rows of one run share one accumulator.
443:            # append_cost_ledger_row bumps the SEPARATE ledger accumulator (not
444:            # _RUN_COST_CTX), so this does NOT double-count the run budget.
445:            # FX-11c (#1136): write the row BEFORE check_run_budget — a
446:            # budget-BREACHING call is already billed to the accumulator by
447:            # _add_run_cost, so it must also land in the ledger; if check_run_budget
448:            # raised first the breaching call would be billed-but-unledgered (the
449:            # exact ledger<budget drift FX-11/FX-11b exist to eliminate).
450:            try:
451:                _orc.append_cost_ledger_row(
452:                    session_id=_orc.current_run_id() or "",
453:                    call_type="nli_conflict_judge",
454:                    cost_usd=actual_cost,
455:                    input_tokens=input_tokens,
456:                    output_tokens=output_tokens,
457:                )
458:            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger I/O must never abort detection
459:                pass
460:            _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if cap breached
461:            content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
462:            parsed = json.loads(content)
463:            verdict = str(parsed.get("verdict", "")).strip().upper()
464:            confidence = float(parsed.get("confidence", 0.0) or 0.0)
465:            label = {"CONTRADICT": "contradict", "ENTAIL": "entail",
466:                     "NEUTRAL": "neutral"}.get(verdict, "neutral")
467:            # Parsed verdict → the ONE success raw-IO record, AFTER parse (gate iter-1 P1).
468:            _emit_raw_io("ok", data)
469:            return label, confidence
470:        except Exception:
471:            # BudgetExceededError is a subclass-free RuntimeError in openrouter_client;
472:            # re-raise it explicitly so the caller's keep-partial path fires.

474:            import sys

481:            # signal). Prefer the bound served `data`. Default-OFF; never raises.

335:                    self._model, input_tokens, output_tokens, 0,
336:                )
337:                # I-bug-100 iter-1 diff P2 fix: when the entire usage block
338:                # is absent, both api_cost and the imputed value are 0, which
339:                # silently bypasses the budget guard. Fall back to a
340:                # conservative estimate based on typical judge-call shape
341:                # (~500 prompt + ~100 completion tokens) priced at Opus-tier
342:                # so the budget cap is preserved on degraded responses.
343:                if actual_cost == 0 and not usage:
344:                    actual_cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
345:                        self._model, 500, 100, 0,
346:                    )
347:                _orc._add_run_cost(actual_cost)
348:                duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - started) * 1000.0
349:                try:
350:                    _append_judge_ledger_entry(
351:                        input_tokens=input_tokens,
352:                        output_tokens=output_tokens,
353:                        duration_ms=duration_ms,
354:                        actual_cost=actual_cost,
355:                    )
356:                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger IO is non-critical
357:                    logger.warning("entailment ledger write failed: %s", exc)
358:                _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if cap breached
359:
360:                content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
361:                parsed = json.loads(content)
362:                verdict = str(parsed.get("verdict", "")).upper().strip()
363:                reason = str(parsed.get("reason", ""))
364:                if verdict not in ("ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
365:                    # Bad verdict = a recoverable failed verification: raise the RETRYABLE error so a
366:                    # transient garbled verdict re-issues. The reason (with bad_verdict=<v>) is
367:                    # preserved for the terminal sentinel on exhaustion.
368:                    raise _RetryableJudgeError(f"bad_verdict={verdict!r}")
369:                # Validated verdict → the ONE success raw-IO record, AFTER parse (gate iter-1 P1).
370:                _emit_raw_io("ok", data, duration_ms=(time.monotonic() - started) * 1000.0)
371:                return verdict, reason
372:            except _orc.BudgetExceededError:
373:                # I-bug-100: do NOT fail-open OR retry on cap breach. Propagate immediately so the
374:                # sweep aborts with a clear cause (this except MUST stay FIRST/outside the retry).
375:                raise
376:            except _RetryableJudgeError as exc:
377:                last_reason = exc.reason
378:                if attempt < _retries:
379:                    logger.warning(
380:                        "entailment judge bad verdict (attempt %d/%d): %s — retrying.",
381:                        attempt + 1, _retries + 1, exc.reason,
382:                    )
383:                    time.sleep(_backoff)
384:                    continue
385:                break
386:            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — transient transport/parse fault: retry then fail-closed
387:                last_reason = type(exc).__name__
388:                if attempt < _retries:
389:                    logger.warning(
390:                        "entailment judge error (attempt %d/%d): %s — retrying.",
391:                        attempt + 1, _retries + 1, exc,
392:                    )
393:                    time.sleep(_backoff)
394:                    continue
395:                logger.warning("entailment judge error (final): %s", exc)
396:                break
397:
398:        # Retries exhausted: emit EXACTLY ONE terminal judge_error record (no per-attempt emits) and
399:        # return the fail-CLOSED-at-consumer sentinel. Prefer the bound served data (a parse/verdict
400:        # failure on a real response) over a bare error string. The sentinel stays ENTAILED+prefix so
401:        # both consumers DROP it (see method docstring + §5 DIVERGENCE in the brief).
402:        _emit_raw_io("judge_error", data if data is not None else {"error": last_reason})
403:        return "ENTAILED", f"judge_error: {last_reason}"
404:
405:
406:def _append_judge_ledger_entry(
407:    *,
408:    input_tokens: int,
409:    output_tokens: int,
410:    duration_ms: float,

625:# cluster of run-sweep gates that FAIL OPEN on a benchmark run: a skewed corpus is accepted
626:# as sweep-ready with only a disclosure (#1235), a V30-contract fault silently degrades to
627:# the legacy generator (#1238), a manifest can read `success` without the 4-role D8 audit
628:# ever running (#1226), and an enabled-but-empty quantified differentiator passes silently
629:# (#1237). The shared strict-gate flag below turns those four fail-open paths LOUD on a
630:# benchmark run (abort / raise / hold) WITHOUT touching strict_verify, the NLI judge, the
631:# 4-role D8 release policy, or any provenance check. DEFAULT OFF: with the flag unset, every
632:# `_benchmark_strict_gates_on()`-guarded branch is skipped and the file is behaviorally
633:# byte-identical to today.
634:#
635:# #1239 (empty bibliography locator) and #1242 (tier-mix self-contradiction) get their OWN
636:# narrow flags (a selection/rendering change and a pure-correctness change respectively), per
637:# the SAFE-PATTERN gating split — they are NOT folded into the shared strict flag.
638:_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES_ENV = "PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES"
639:_V30_ALLOW_LEGACY_FALLBACK_ENV = "PG_V30_ALLOW_LEGACY_FALLBACK"
640:_BIB_REQUIRE_LOCATOR_ENV = "PG_BIB_REQUIRE_LOCATOR"
641:_TIER_DISCLOSURE_SINGLE_SOURCE_ENV = "PG_TIER_DISCLOSURE_SINGLE_SOURCE"

644:def _benchmark_strict_gates_on() -> bool:
645:    """Shared benchmark strict-gates flag (default OFF). When ON, the four currently
646:    fail-open run-sweep gates (#1235/#1238/#1226/#1237) fail LOUD instead of silently
647:    accepting/degrading. OFF == byte-identical to the legacy behavior. PURE env read."""
648:    return _env_flag(_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES_ENV, default=False)

650:
651:def _corpus_skew_blocks_ready(strict: bool, has_material_deviation: bool) -> bool:
652:    """#1235: under strict gates, a corpus with a MATERIAL tier deviation must NOT be
653:    silently accepted as sweep-ready on the weighted-corpus path — it falls through to the
654:    EXISTING structured-authorization gate (check_auto_approve_allowed, which honors the
655:    operator override PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL) instead. Returns True iff the caller must
656:    bypass the weighted-corpus auto-approve. PURE — no faithfulness logic. Off => always
657:    False => unchanged."""
658:    return bool(strict and has_material_deviation)

660:
661:def _v30_should_reraise(strict: bool, allow_legacy_fallback: bool) -> bool:
662:    """#1238: under strict gates, a V30 contract Phase-2 fault must be FATAL (re-raise to the
663:    run_one_query handler -> terminal error_unexpected manifest) instead of silently falling
664:    back to the legacy generator — UNLESS the operator explicitly allows the fallback via
665:    PG_V30_ALLOW_LEGACY_FALLBACK=1. PURE. Off (or fallback allowed) => False => unchanged."""
666:    return bool(strict and not allow_legacy_fallback)

669:def _d8_success_without_audit(
670:    strict: bool, unified_status: str, d8_audit_ran: bool
671:) -> bool:
672:    """#1226: under strict gates, a manifest may NOT read `success` unless the 4-role D8 audit

675:    the single binding gate); it only forbids a *pre-D8* success on a benchmark run. Off =>
676:    False => unchanged."""
677:    return bool(strict and unified_status == "success" and not d8_audit_ran)

findings:
  - location: "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:5509"
    what: "Section generation uses asyncio.gather without return_exceptions around wall-clock-wrapped section tasks."
    why_it_chokes: "One section timeout from _run_section_with_wallclock raises after two attempts and cancels sibling sections, losing completed/near-complete work and crashing the whole run."
    fix: "Use return_exceptions=True or as_completed, persist completed section payloads, and convert per-section failures into explicit gap/error records instead of aborting all siblings."
    severity: "P0"
    should_be_env: "PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS"

  - location: "scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8837"
    what: "No checkpoint/resume path for run_one_query or Gate-B; the CLIs only replay env pins, not partial data."
    why_it_chokes: "A late report/gate failure after hours forces a full restart and discards retrieval, evidence selection, generated sections, and verifier outputs instead of carrying data forward and re-running verdict gates."
    fix: "Add data checkpoints plus resume-by-run-dir; reload retrieval/evidence/section raw data, but always re-run strict_verify/NLI/4-role/D8 on resume."
    severity: "P0"
    should_be_env: "PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED, PG_RESUME_RUN_DIR"

  - location: "scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:638"
    what: "The shared benchmark strict gate is default-off and Gate-B does not set PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES."
    why_it_chokes: "Documented fail-open guards stay inactive: V30 contract faults can fall back to legacy generation, success can be marked without a completed D8 audit, and forced quantified analysis can no-op silently."
    fix: "Set PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES=1 in the Gate-B slate and preflight it as required."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES"

  - location: "scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:8271"
    what: "Benchmark NLI failures are advisory and fail-open even though Gate-B force-enables PG_NLI_IN_BENCHMARK."
    why_it_chokes: "Import, unavailable backend, or scoring errors leave manifest nli_status=error/unavailable with advisory=true and do not withhold release, so a required faithfulness layer can disappear."
    fix: "In Gate-B/strict mode, require nli_status=ok with nonzero scored pairs when eligible sentences exist; abort or hold on unavailable/error/judge-error rate."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_NLI_IN_BENCHMARK, PG_NLI_REQUIRED_IN_BENCHMARK"

  - location: "src/polaris_graph/retrieval/semantic_conflict_detector.py:478"
    what: "Semantic conflict judge errors return neutral/0.0."
    why_it_chokes: "A transient API, timeout, or parse error silently erases a possible cross-document contradiction; the orchestrator also converts detector exceptions to semantic_records=[]."
    fix: "Track judge_error_count and fail closed in Gate-B when errors occur above a tiny threshold or when all attempted pairs fail."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT, PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_MAX_JUDGE_ERROR_RATE"

  - location: "scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:7126"
    what: "Qualitative conflict records are written/rendered but not passed to the evaluator PT08 contradiction gate."
    why_it_chokes: "A high/medium clinical qualitative safety disagreement can exist in contradictions.json, but missing disclosure in the final report will not trigger the evaluator abort path."
    fix: "Include qualitative_records in the evaluator contradiction payload or add a dedicated required-disclosure gate for high/medium qualitative conflicts."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_SWEEP_QUALITATIVE_CONFLICT"

  - location: "src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:2694"
    what: "Citation resolver silently skips kept sentences that clean down to short/degenerate fragments."
    why_it_chokes: "SectionResult.sentences_verified is set from report.total_kept before this skip, so verified counts can stay positive while rendered verified_text is empty or missing sentences."
    fix: "Return resolver drop counts/reasons and compute sentences_verified/is_gap_stub from post-resolve rendered sentences."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "n/a"

  - location: "src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:180"
    what: "Side-judge model is read from PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL but Gate-B does not force it to the locked mirror model."
    why_it_chokes: "A stale env override can route NLI/semantic/entailment side judging to an unlocked model while preflight only force-enables the features."
    fix: "Force PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL=z-ai/glm-5.1 in Gate-B and fail preflight on any mismatch."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL"

  - location: "src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:81"
    what: "Entailment judge uses a hardcoded 30s httpx client timeout and default max_tokens=2000."
    why_it_chokes: "The binding verifier can over-drop or abort from under-sized judge calls, and the timeout is not a PG tunable despite the max-timeout/max-budget directive."
    fix: "Move timeout to PG_ENTAILMENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and default max_tokens to the governed model/provider completion cap."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_ENTAILMENT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, PG_ENTAILMENT_MAX_TOKENS"

  - location: "src/polaris_graph/retrieval/semantic_conflict_detector.py:69"
    what: "Semantic conflict judge has a hardcoded 30s timeout and default max_tokens=2000."
    why_it_chokes: "This combines with fail-open neutral handling: slow or starved GLM calls become missing contradictions rather than run-visible verifier degradation."
    fix: "Move timeout to PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and default max_tokens to the governed mirror cap."
    severity: "P1"
    should_be_env: "PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS"

top_3_surprises:
  - "Qualitative conflict records reach contradictions.json and report rendering, but not the evaluator contradiction gate."
  - "The strict-gate framework explicitly documents several fail-open benchmark paths, yet Gate-B does not enable PG_BENCHMARK_STRICT_GATES."
  - "Verified sentence counts are computed before citation-resolution skips, so the rendered report can have fewer verified sentences than telemetry claims."
